title: ANC00093 Blood Clot Formation Around Plaque
summary_zh: 血栓形成过程：展示血凝块在动脉斑块周围形成，阻碍血流，可导致心脏病发作或中风
text_en: Formation of a blood clot which can impede blood flow through the artery, leading to a heart attack or a stroke.

title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
summary_zh: 冠状动脉血管成形术（经皮冠状动脉介入术）的介绍：冠状动脉为心肌供氧，当粥样硬化斑块显著狭窄血管时，手术目标是恢复血流。
text_en: A coronary angioplasty procedure is also known as percutaneous coronary intervention. The procedure is done on blood vessels called coronary arteries. They supply your heart muscle with oxygen. The goal is to restore blood flow if a substance called plaque has significantly narrowed these vessels.

title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
summary_zh: 经桡动脉入路建立血管通路：局部麻醉腕部皮肤，穿刺针刺入桡动脉，送入导丝后撤出穿刺针，置换为鞘管以建立动脉通路。
text_en: To begin the procedure, the doctor will numb the skin in your wrist. A needle will be placed through your skin and into your radial artery. Next, a flexible guide wire will be passed through the needle into your artery. Then, the needle will be withdrawn. It will be exchanged for a small, flexible tube called a sheath. This permits access into your artery. You may feel pressure when the doctor inserts the sheath, but you will not feel it moving inside your artery.

title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
summary_zh: 导丝与导管推进至冠状动脉并造影：导丝推进至心脏，导管沿导丝到达冠状动脉，透视下确认位置后撤出导丝，将导管尖端送入目标冠状动脉并注入造影剂，透视下显示血管狭窄部位。
text_en: Next, the guide wire will be advanced up to your heart. A flexible tube called a catheter will be advanced over the wire to your coronary arteries. The progress of the procedure will be checked with an x-ray device called a flora scope. At this point, your doctor will remove the guide wire. Then, the doctor will move the tip of the catheter just inside the coronary artery to be examined. A special die will be injected into the artery. This allows your doctor to view it better with a flora scope. The die will make any blockages in the artery stand out.

title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
summary_zh: 球囊扩张狭窄血管：发现显著狭窄后，送入导丝至病变处，球囊导管沿导丝推送至狭窄部位，充盈球囊扩张动脉以改善血流，随后球囊回缩并撤出。
text_en: If a significant blockage is found, your doctor will insert a guide wire into the artery. A balloon on the tip of the catheter will be moved along the wire to the blockage. When the balloon inflates, it will expand the artery and improve the blood flow. You may feel some chest discomfort while this is happening. After this, your doctor will deflate and remove the balloon.

title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
summary_zh: 支架置入：将金属网状支架压缩于球囊上，沿同一导丝送至治疗部位，球囊充盈使支架膨胀并锁定就位，撤出球囊导管后支架永久留置以维持动脉通畅。
text_en: A wire mesh tube called a stent may be placed in the treated area. The stent helps keep the coronary artery open. Your doctor will choose the proper size stent, which is compressed over a balloon. The stent will be moved into the artery over the same guide wire. When the balloon is inflated, the stent will expand and lock into place. After the balloon catheter is taken out, the stent will stay in place to hold the artery open.

title: ANCE00174 Coronary Artery Angioplasty (Radial Access)
summary_zh: 手术结束：撤出导丝，完成整个冠状动脉血管成形术操作。
text_en: At the end of the procedure, the guide wire will be removed.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
summary_zh: 介绍冠状动脉血管成形术（经皮冠状动脉介入治疗）的定义：冠状动脉为心肌供氧，当动脉粥样硬化斑块导致血管显著狭窄时，手术目的是恢复血流。
text_en: A coronary angioplasty procedure is also known as percutaneous coronary intervention. The procedure is done on blood vessels called coronary arteries. They supply your heart muscle with oxygen. The goal is to restore blood flow if a substance called plaque has significantly narrowed these vessels.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
summary_zh: 股动脉穿刺与鞘管置入：局部麻醉腹股沟皮肤，经皮穿刺股动脉，送入导丝后撤出穿刺针，交换置入血管鞘管以建立动脉通路。患者可能感到压迫感但不会感觉鞘管在动脉内移动。
text_en: To begin the procedure, the doctor will numb the skin in your groin. A needle will be placed through your skin and into the femoral artery. Next, a flexible guide wire will be passed through the needle into your artery. Then, the needle will be withdrawn. It will be exchanged for a small, flexible tube called a sheath. This permits access into your artery. You may feel pressure when the doctor inserts the sheath, but you will not feel it moving inside your artery.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
summary_zh: 导管推送至心脏：导丝沿动脉上行至心脏，导管沿导丝推进至冠状动脉开口处，全程在X线透视（fluoroscopy）下监视进展，随后撤出导丝。
text_en: Next, the guide wire will be advanced up to your heart. A flexible tube called a catheter will be advanced over the wire to your coronary arteries. The progress of the procedure will be checked with an x-ray device called a flora scope. At this point, your doctor will remove the guide wire.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
summary_zh: 冠状动脉造影：将导管头端送入目标冠状动脉，注射造影剂，在透视下清晰显示冠状动脉走行及狭窄/阻塞部位。
text_en: Then, the doctor will move the tip of the catheter just inside the coronary artery to be examined. A special die will be injected into the artery. This allows your doctor to view it better with a flora scope. The die will make any blockages in the artery stand out.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
summary_zh: 球囊扩张术：发现显著狭窄后，沿导丝将球囊导管送至阻塞处，充盈球囊使狭窄段扩张以改善血流。患者可能感到胸部不适。随后球囊减压并撤出。
text_en: If a significant blockage is found, your doctor will insert a guide wire into the artery. A balloon on the tip of the catheter will be moved along the wire to the blockage. When the balloon inflates, it will expand the artery and improve the blood flow. You may feel some chest discomfort while this is happening. After this, your doctor will deflate and remove the balloon.

title: ANCE00178 Coronary Angioplasty (Femoral Approach)
summary_zh: 支架植入：在治疗部位放置金属网状支架以维持冠状动脉通畅。选择合适尺寸的支架压缩在球囊上，沿导丝送入病变处，球囊充盈使支架膨胀并锁定就位，撤出球囊后支架永久留置以支撑动脉壁。
text_en: A wire mesh tube called a stent may be placed in the treated area. The stent helps keep the coronary artery open. Your doctor will choose the proper size stent, which is compressed over a balloon. The stent will be moved into the artery over the same guide wire. When the balloon is inflated, the stent will expand and lock into place. After the balloon catheter is taken out, the stent will stay in place to hold the artery

title: ANCE00178S101 What is a Coronary Angioplasty
summary_zh: 冠状动脉介入术（PCI）的定义：介绍冠状动脉血管成形术的名称，说明冠状动脉的功能——为心肌供氧。
text_en: A coronary angioplasty procedure is also known as percutaneous coronary intervention. The procedure is done on blood vessels called coronary arteries. They supply your heart muscle with oxygen.

title: ANCE00178S101 What is a Coronary Angioplasty
summary_zh: 手术目标与支架概念：当斑块显著狭窄冠状动脉时，手术旨在恢复血流；引入支架（金属网管）的概念，说明其作用是保持冠状动脉通畅。
text_en: The goal is to restore blood flow if a substance called plaque has significantly narrowed these vessels. A wire mesh tube called a stent may be placed in the treated area. The stent helps keep the coronary artery open.

title: ANCE00178S101 What is a Coronary Angioplasty
summary_zh: 支架植入操作步骤：医生选择合适尺寸的支架并压缩在球囊上，沿导丝送入动脉，球囊膨胀使支架扩张并锁定就位，撤出球囊导管后支架永久留置以保持动脉开放。
text_en: Your doctor will choose the proper size stent, which is compressed over a balloon. The stent will be moved into the artery over the same guide wire. When the balloon is inflated, the stent will expand and lock into place. After the balloon catheter is taken out, the stent will stay in place to hold the artery open.

file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
summary_zh: 血管造影术的准备与穿刺：医生对腹股沟皮肤进行局部麻醉，然后将穿刺针经皮肤刺入股动脉
text_en: To begin the procedure, the doctor will numb the skin in your groin. A needle will be placed through your skin and into the femoral artery.

file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
summary_zh: 导丝插入与鞘管置换：将柔性导丝通过穿刺针送入动脉，随后撤出穿刺针，换入称为鞘管的柔性管道，建立动脉通路
text_en: Next, a flexible guide wire will be passed through the needle into your artery. Then, the needle will be withdrawn. It will be exchanged for a small, flexible tube called a sheath. This permits access into your artery.

file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
summary_zh: 导丝与导管推进至心脏：导丝沿动脉推进至心脏，随后将导管沿导丝推进至冠状动脉，过程中使用透视（荧光镜）进行X线监控
text_en: Next, the guide wire will be advanced up to your heart. A flexible tube called a catheter will be advanced over the wire to your coronary arteries. The progress of the procedure will be checked with an x-ray device called a flora scope.

file: ANCE00178S102_How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S102 How are Angiograms Used to Find Coronary Artery Blockages
summary_zh: 导管定位与造影剂注射：撤出导丝后，医生将导管尖端送入待检查的冠状动脉内，注入造影剂使动脉在透视下清晰显影，从而发现冠状动脉中的阻塞病变
text_en: At this point, your doctor will remove the guide wire. Then, the doctor will move the tip of the catheter just inside the coronary artery to be examined. A special die will be injected into the artery. This allows your doctor to view it better with the flora scope. The die will make any blockages in the artery stand out.

file: ANCE00178S103_How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S103 How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart
summary_zh: 球囊血管成形术过程：医生将导丝插入动脉，球囊导管沿导丝推进至堵塞处，充气扩张动脉以改善血流，患者可能感到胸部不适
text_en: If a significant blockage is found, your doctor will insert a guide wire into the artery. A balloon on the tip of the catheter will be moved along the wire to the blockage. When the balloon inflates, it will expand the artery and improve the blood flow. You may feel some chest discomfort while this is happening.

file: ANCE00178S103_How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S103 How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart
summary_zh: 球囊回撤：球囊放气后取出
text_en: After this, your doctor will deflate and remove the balloon.

file: ANCE00178S103_How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart_.mp4
title: ANCE00178S103 How Does an Angioplasty Open Arteries in the Heart
summary_zh: 支架植入过程：将金属网状支架压缩在球囊上，沿同一导丝送入动脉，球囊充气使支架扩张并锁定就位，取出球囊导管后支架留在原位保持动脉通畅
text_en: A wire mesh tube called a stent may be placed in the treated area. The stent helps keep the coronary artery open. Your doctor will choose the proper size stent, which is compressed over a balloon. The stent will be moved into the artery over the same guide wire. When the balloon is inflated, the stent will expand and lock into place. After the balloon catheter is taken out, the stent will stay in place to hold the artery open.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
summary_zh: 冠状动脉造影术简介：这是一种心脏X射线检查，可以观察冠状动脉，检测斑块导致的血管狭窄，并评估心脏瓣膜和腔室的功能。
text_en: Coronary artery angiography is an x-ray test of the heart. This test can view blood vessels in your heart called coronary arteries. It can find out if a waxy substance called plaque has narrowed these vessels. And, it can show how well the valves and chambers of your heart are working.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
summary_zh: 术前准备与入路选择：冠状动脉造影通常通过心导管术进行，患者平卧于配有透视设备的检查台上。通常经手腕桡动脉入路（出血和并发症较少），有时也可经腹股沟股动脉入路。
text_en: Coronary angiography is often done with a medical procedure called cardiac catheterization. Before the procedure, you will be placed on a table with an x-ray device called a florescope. Usually cardiac catheterization takes place through the radial artery in your wrist. This route normally has less bleeding and complications.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
summary_zh: 穿刺与鞘管置入：医生在手腕处局部麻醉后，将针刺入桡动脉，穿入导丝并沿动脉引导至心脏，随后拔出针头，置入柔性鞘管以建立桡动脉通路。
text_en: Sometimes the femoral artery in your groin may be used instead. To begin, the doctor will numb the skin in your wrist. A needle will be inserted into your radial artery. A wire will be passed through this needle. The wire will be gently guided through your arteries to your heart. The needle will be taken out and replaced with a small flexible tube called a sheath. This permits access to your radial artery.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
summary_zh: 导管推进与透视引导：将柔性导管经鞘管沿导丝送入心脏，透视设备围绕胸部移动拍摄X射线图像，医生借此监控导管位置。患者在置入导丝和鞘管时可能感到压迫感，但不会感觉到它们在动脉内部移动。
text_en: Next, a soft flexible tube called a catheter will be slipped over the wire through the sheath. The catheter will be threaded up to your heart. Then, the florescope will be moved around your chest to take x-ray images of your heart. Your doctor will use the images to check on the catheter. You may feel pressure as the wire and sheath are put in, but you will not feel them inside your arteries.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
summary_zh: 造影剂注射与血管成像：导管到达冠状动脉后拔出导丝，将导管尖端分别置入各冠状动脉口注射造影剂，透视设备拍摄血管造影图像（即冠脉造影图），动脉充盈造影剂后可清晰显示任何阻塞。注射时患者可能有潮热或轻微恶心感。
text_en: Once the catheter reaches your coronary arteries, the wire will be removed. The catheter tip will be placed just inside each artery to inject a special die. This die allows the florescope to take x-ray pictures of your arteries, called angiograms. Any blockages will be clearly identified as the arteries fill with die. You may feel flushed or slightly nauseated when the die is injected.

title: ANCE00182 Coronary Artery Angiography (Cardiac Catheterization)
summary_zh: 术后处理：手术结束后经手腕拔除导管，在桡动脉处使用压迫带防止出血，随后拔除鞘管，压迫带持续留置直至出血停止。
text_en: At the end of the procedure, the catheter will be removed through your wrist. A pressure band will be used over the radial artery to prevent bleeding. Then, the sheath will be removed. The pressure band will remain on your wrist until bleeding has stopped.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: CABG手术概述：用身体其他部位的健康动脉或静脉连接到阻塞的冠状动脉，绕过阻塞段，将富氧血液输送至心肌；一次手术可搭桥多条冠状动脉
text_en: A coronary artery bypass graft or cabbage is performed to improve circulation to the heart muscle. In this procedure a healthy artery or vein from another part of the body is connected or grafted to the blocked coronary artery. The grafted artery or vein bypasses the blocked portion of the coronary artery, carrying oxygen-rich blood to the heart muscle. One or more coronary arteries may be bypassed during a single operation.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 术前准备：建立静脉通路、给予镇静药物、全身麻醉、插入气管导管辅助呼吸、留置导尿管；手术一般耗时3至6小时
text_en: Before the surgery, an intravenous line will be started and you may be given a medication to help you relax. Cabbage procedures are done under general anesthesia, which will put you to sleep for the duration of the operation. A breathing tube will be inserted through your mouth and into your throat to help you breathe. A catheter will be placed in your bladder to drain your urine. Coronary artery bypass surgery generally takes 3 to 6 hours.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 常规搭桥手术开胸过程：沿胸骨正中切开皮肤和胸骨、撑开肋骨暴露心脏，连接体外循环（心肺机）以在手术期间暂时替代心肺功能，必要时停跳心脏进行吻合
text_en: During a conventional bypass surgery, your surgeon begins by making an incision in the skin over your breastbone or sternum. He or she will then cut the sternum and move your ribcage in order to get to your heart. Throughout the procedure, your circulatory system may be connected to a cardiopulmonary bypass pump or heart lung machine. This machine may temporarily perform the functions of your heart and lungs during the surgery, allowing your heart to be stopped if desired while the surgeon shows the grafts into place.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 内乳动脉（胸廓内动脉）搭桥：常用移植血管包括胸廓内动脉和大隐静脉；内乳动脉搭桥时保留其上端与锁骨下动脉的连接，将下端从胸壁游离后吻合至冠状动脉阻塞远端
text_en: Different blood vessels may typically be used for the grafts. The internal thoracic arteries in the chest, also known as internal mammary arteries, or the saffinous veins in the legs. For the internal thoracic artery graft, your surgeon will leave the upper end attached to the subclavian artery and divert the lower end from your chest wall to your coronary artery just beyond the blockage. Your surgeon will then sew the graft into place.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 大隐静脉搭桥：将静脉一端缝合至主动脉、另一端缝合至阻塞远端的冠状动脉；吻合完成后通过电信号恢复心跳并安装临时起搏器
text_en: For the saffinous vein graft, your surgeon will suture one end to the aorta and the other end to the narrowed artery just beyond the blockage. With the grafts securely in place, your surgeon may use electrical signals to restore the heart beat and attach a temporary pacemaker to the heart.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 关胸与术后引流：确认心跳恢复正常后断开心肺机，用钢丝固定胸骨、缝合皮肤切口，放置临时胸腔引流管
text_en: Once your heart is again beating normally, if the surgeon used the heart lung machine, it would be disconnected. Your surgeon will wire the breastbone back together and suture the skin incision closed. A temporary drainage tube will be placed through the skin beneath the incision.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 非体外循环（Off-Pump）搭桥与微创手术：不使用心肺机、心脏持续跳动，用机械稳定器固定吻合区域；微创术式通过小切口或端口使用特殊器械完成
text_en: When the surgeon chooses to do the surgery without using the heart lung machine, the heart continues to beat. This may be referred to as off-pump bypass surgery or minimally invasive surgery. Instead, a mechanical device is used to steady the part of the heart where the grafting is being done. Surgeons perform minimally invasive bypass procedures using specially designed instruments inserted through small incisions or ports in the chest.

title: ANCE00199 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 术后ICU监护与恢复：持续心电监护、必要时使用临时起搏导线控制心率、保留胸腔引流管和导尿管、拔除气管导管改用氧气面罩；术后3至5天逐步撤除各类管路
text_en: After surgery, you will be taken to the intensive care unit. The activity of your heart will be carefully monitored. If necessary, pacing wires will be used to temporarily control your heart rate. The chest tube will remain in place to drain excess blood and air from the chest cavity. Once you can breathe on your own, your breathing tube will be removed and replaced with an oxygen mask. The bladder catheter will remain in place. As you recover from surgery over the next three to five days, all of these devices will be gradually removed.

file: ANCE00199S101_What is a Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S101 What is a Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 冠状动脉搭桥术（CABG）的定义与目的：通过手术改善心肌的血液循环
text_en: A coronary artery bypass graft, or cabbage, is performed to improve circulation to the heart muscle.

file: ANCE00199S101_What is a Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S101 What is a Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG)
summary_zh: 搭桥手术的具体过程：取身体其他部位的健康动脉或静脉，连接到阻塞的冠状动脉上，绕过阻塞部分，将富氧血液输送至心肌；一次手术可同时对多条冠状动脉进行搭桥
text_en: In this procedure, a healthy artery or vein from another part of the body is connected or grafted to the blocked coronary artery. The grafted artery or vein bypasses the blocked portion of the coronary artery, carrying oxygen-rich blood to the heart muscle. One or more coronary arteries may be bypassed during a single operation.

file: ANCE00199S102_What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S102 What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass
summary_zh: 介绍心脏搭桥手术常用的血管移植物类型：胸廓内动脉（又称乳内动脉）和腿部大隐静脉
text_en: Different blood vessels may typically be used for the grafts. The internal thoracic arteries in the chest, also known as internal mammary arteries, or the saffonous veins in the legs.

file: ANCE00199S102_What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S102 What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass
summary_zh: 胸廓内动脉移植物的手术方法：上端保留与锁骨下动脉的连接，下端从胸壁分离后缝合至冠状动脉阻塞远端
text_en: For the internal thoracic artery graft, your surgeon will leave the upper end attached to the subclavian artery, and divert the lower end from your chest wall to your coronary artery, just beyond the blockage. Your surgeon will then sew the graft into place.

file: ANCE00199S102_What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S102 What Blood Vessels are Commonly Used as a Graft for a Heart Bypass
summary_zh: 大隐静脉移植物的手术方法：一端缝合至主动脉，另一端缝合至狭窄冠状动脉的阻塞远端
text_en: For the saffonous vein graft, your surgeon will suture one end to the aorta, and the other end to the narrowed artery, just beyond the blockage.

file: ANCE00199S103_What are the Different Types of Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedures_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S103 What are the Different Types of Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedures
summary_zh: 常规冠状动脉搭桥手术（体外循环下）：外科医生沿胸骨做切口，切开胸骨并撑开肋骨暴露心脏，术中将循环系统连接至体外循环机（心肺机）以维持血液循环
text_en: During a conventional bypass surgery, your surgeon begins by making an incision in the skin over your breastbone or sternum. He or she will then cut the sternum and move your ribcage in order to get to your heart. Throughout the procedure, your circulatory system may be connected to a cardiopulmonary bypass pump or heart lung machine.

file: ANCE00199S103_What are the Different Types of Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedures_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S103 What are the Different Types of Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedures
summary_zh: 非体外循环搭桥手术（off-pump CABG）：不使用心肺机，心脏在术中持续跳动，也称为不停跳搭桥或微创手术；使用机械稳定装置固定心脏上需要进行吻合的区域，以便精确操作
text_en: When the surgeon chooses to do the surgery without using the heart lung machine, the heart continues to beat. This may be referred to as off-pump bypass surgery or minimally invasive surgery. Instead, a mechanical device is used to steady the part of the heart where the grafting is being done.

file: ANCE00199S103_What are the Different Types of Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedures_.mp4
title: ANCE00199S103 What are the Different Types of Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedures
summary_zh: 微创搭桥手术：外科医生通过胸壁上的小切口或端口，使用专门设计的器械完成搭桥操作，避免了传统的大切口开胸
text_en: Surgeons perform minimally invasive bypass procedures using specially designed instruments inserted through small incisions or ports in the chest.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 外科引流管的基本介绍：引流管的作用是排出手术部位皮下积液，预防感染并促进愈合；引流装置由引流管和收集球囊组成
text_en: Curgical drains remove fluid from under your skin near your surgical site. This helps prevent infection and encourages healing. A surgical drain consists of a drain tube that runs from your surgical site to a bulb that collects the fluid.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 引流管护理概述与准备工作：需要定期挤捋引流管以清除血凝块，球囊半满时排空；所需物品包括酒精棉片、量杯和引流记录表，必要时佩戴一次性手套
text_en: You strip the drain tube to keep it clear and remove any clots or blockages. Then you empty the bulb when it is half full or as instructed by your healthcare provider. You will need alcohol wipes, a measuring cup and a drainage record sheet. Be sure to wear disposable gloves if your healthcare provider says you should.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 操作前注意事项与前置步骤（步骤1-2）：避免拉扯引流管，用肥皂水洗手并擦干，按医嘱佩戴一次性手套
text_en: Be careful not to pull on the tubing. You should not feel any tugging where the tube enters your skin. Step 1. Wash your hands with soap and water and then dry them. Step 2. Put on disposable gloves if your healthcare provider says you should.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 检查与松解血凝块（步骤3-5）：检查引流管内是否有阻塞血凝块（包括皮肤下隐藏的），轻轻挤压管壁松解凝块，一只手在皮肤出口处固定引流管
text_en: Step 3. Look for clots or blockages that may prevent the fluid from flowing out of the tube and into the bulb. Some clots may be hidden inside the tube under your skin. Step 4. Loosen the clots by gently squeezing the tubes surrounding them. Step 5. Use one hand to hold the drain tube in place where it leaves your skin.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 挤捋引流管操作（步骤6-7及重复）：用酒精棉片夹住引流管，沿管滑动手指将液体挤入球囊；可能需要重复多次，中途不要松手，若松手需换新酒精棉片重新操作
text_en: Step 6. Use your other hand to pinch the tube with an alcohol wipe between your finger and thumb. Step 7. Slide your pinched fingers along the tube to force any fluid out of the tube and into the bulb. You may need to repeat steps 5 through 7 several times to clear the tube. Try not to let go of the tube between steps. If fluid remains in the tube or you accidentally let go, repeat steps 5 through 7 using a new alcohol wipe.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 排空球囊准备（步骤8-10）：球囊不能超过半满，否则影响吸引力；将球囊置于切口下方使液体流入球囊，球囊远离身体，取下瓶盖前不可挤压球囊
text_en: Do not allow the bulb to become more than half full. Too much fluid in the bulb reduces its ability to remove fluid from underneath your skin. Now you will begin emptying the bulb. Step 8. Hold the bulb lower than your incision so that fluid moves out of the tube and into the bulb. Step 9. Point the bulb away from your body. Never squeeze the bulb before taking the cap off. Step 10. Remove the cap.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 排空球囊并重建负压（步骤11-14）：裸手不可触碰开口，将球囊倒置挤出液体至量杯；排空后持续挤压球囊并用酒精棉片擦拭顶部，保持挤压状态盖回瓶盖，使球囊形成持续负压吸引
text_en: Never touch the opening with your bare hands. Step 11. Hold the measuring cup under the bulb. Step 12. Turn the bulb upside down and squeeze the fluid into the cup. Step 13. After removing the fluid, continue squeezing the bulb and use a new alcohol wipe to clean the top. Step 14. While still squeezing the bulb, put the cap back on the top. The depressed bulb creates suction that continuously removes fluid from underneath your skin.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 记录与日常维护（步骤15-17）：读取量杯中液体量并记录在记录表上，按指示清洗量杯；日常保持球囊低于切口水平以利引流
text_en: Step 15. Read the amount of fluid in the measuring cup. Step 16. Write the amount on your record sheet. Step 17. Empty and rinse the cup as directed. Keep the bulb below the level of your incision to help the fluid move out of the tube and into the bulb.

title: AND12001 Drainage Tube Care
summary_zh: 警示信号与就医指征：引流液量突然增减、气味改变、出现脓液或变稠、引流管脱出、切口裂开、切口红肿疼痛化脓、体温达101°F以上时应联系外科医生
text_en: Contact your surgeon if you notice the amount of fluid suddenly increases or decreases, the odor of the fluid changes. The fluid contains pus or becomes thicker over time. Your drain tube falls out or your incision opens. Your incision is red, swollen, painful or has pus coming out or your temperature is 101 degrees Fahrenheit or higher.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 步骤1——目视检查：观察术侧足部和脚趾，与另一侧对比；正常应温暖、肤色正常、无明显肿胀，这些是血流良好的表现
text_en: Step 1. Look at your foot and toes on the leg recovering from the surgical procedure. Compare your feet. Do you see any differences? Your foot and toes on the surgical side should feel warm, be normal in color, and have little to no swelling. These signs mean you have good blood flow in your leg.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 血流不良的警示信号：足部发红或发紫、触感凉冷、严重肿胀，提示可能存在血液循环障碍
text_en: If your foot looks red or blue, feels cool, or has a lot of swelling, your leg may have poor blood flow.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 步骤2——感觉检查：足部麻木或刺痛可能是血流不良的征兆，但也可能是神经阻滞麻醉尚未消退所致，不确定时应咨询医生
text_en: Step 2. Does your foot feel numb or tingly? A feeling of numbness and tingling may also be a sign of poor blood flow. Or, you may feel numbness from a type of anesthesia called a nerve block until it wears off. If you are not sure if you had a nerve block, ask your surgeon.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 步骤3——活动脚趾：扭动脚趾有助于促进足部血液循环；以上三步应每4小时重复一次或遵医嘱
text_en: Step 3. Wiggle your toes. Wiggling your toes may improve blood flow to your foot. Repeat steps 1 through 3 every 4 hours, or as directed by your surgeon.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 毛细血管再充盈测试介绍：毛细血管是将富氧血液输送到脚趾的微小血管，该测试可评估术侧脚趾的血液循环状况
text_en: Another way to check for good blood flow is by doing a capillary refill test. Capillaries are small blood vessels that carry oxygen-rich blood to your toes. The capillary refill test allows you to see how well blood is flowing through these blood vessels to your toes on the surgical side.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 毛细血管再充盈测试操作步骤：捏住脚趾趾甲处使其变白后松开，趾甲应在2-3秒内恢复粉色，表明血流良好；每4小时重复一次或遵医嘱
text_en: Step 1. Gently pinch the tip of your toe directly over the toenail with your hand. Step 2. Squeeze until your toenail turns white. Then let go. Your toenail should turn pink again within 2-3 seconds. This sign means that you have good blood flow in your leg and foot. Repeat steps 1 and 2 every 4 hours, or as directed by your surgeon.

title: AND12002 Circulation Checks  Lower Extremity
summary_zh: 需联系医生的警示情况：足部发红/发紫、严重肿胀、冰冷麻木刺痛（非神经阻滞所致），或毛细血管再充盈时间超过2-3秒
text_en: Contact your surgeon if you notice. Your foot looks red or blue. Your foot looks very swollen. Your foot feels cold, numb or tingly, unless you had a nerve block during surgery, making your foot feel numb until it wears off. Or, your toenail takes more than 2-3 seconds to return to a pink color when doing the capillary refill test.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
summary_zh: 术后血液循环的重要性及视觉检查第一步：观察手术侧手部和手指，与另一侧进行对比
text_en: Good blood flow is important for recovery after the surgical procedure on your arm. Step 1. Look at your hand and fingers on the arm recovering from the surgical procedure.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
summary_zh: 正常与异常血流的体征判断：正常应温暖、肤色正常、无明显肿胀；异常表现为发红/发蓝、触感凉冷、严重肿胀，提示血流不良
text_en: Compare your hands. Do you see any differences? Your hand and fingers on the surgical side should feel warm, be normal in color, and have little to no swelling. These signs mean you have good blood flow in your arm. If your hand looks red or blue, feels cool, or has a lot of swelling, your arm may have poor blood flow.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
summary_zh: 第二步感觉检查：麻木和刺痛感可能提示血流不良，也可能是神经阻滞麻醉未消退的正常反应，如不确定应咨询医生
text_en: Step 2. Does your hand feel numb and tingly? A feeling of numbness and tingling can also be a sign of poor blood flow. Or, you may feel numbness from a type of anesthesia called a nerve block until it wears off. If you are not sure if you had a nerve block, ask your surgeon.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
summary_zh: 第三步活动手指：通过活动手指促进血液循环，建议每四小时重复以上三个步骤
text_en: Step 3. Wiggle your fingers. Wiggling your fingers may improve blood flow to your hand. Repeat steps one through three every four hours, or as directed by your surgeon.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
summary_zh: 毛细血管再充盈测试介绍：毛细血管是将富氧血液输送到手指的微小血管，该测试用于评估手术侧手指的血流情况
text_en: Another way to check for good blood flow is by doing a capillary refill test. Capillaries are small blood vessels that carry oxygen-rich blood to your fingers. The capillary refill test allows you to see how well blood is flowing through these blood vessels to your fingers on the surgical side.

title: AND12003 Circulation Checks  Upper Extremity
summary_zh: 毛细血管再充盈测试操作步骤：捏住指尖至指甲变白后松开，正常应在2-3秒内恢复粉色，表明血流良好；建议每四小时重复
text_en: Step 1. Gently pinch the tip of your finger directly over the fingernail with your other hand. Step 2. Squeeze until your fingernail turns white. Then let go. Your fingernail should turn pink again within two to three seconds. This sign means that you have good blood flow in your arm and hand. Repeat steps one and two every four hours, or as directed by your surgeon.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 切口基本概念：介绍切口的定义（手术中在皮肤上的切割），切口边缘的闭合方式（缝线、钉、皮肤胶、皮肤闭合条），以及敷料的作用——保持清洁干燥以预防感染、促进愈合。
text_en: An incision is a cut made in your skin during an operation. The edges of your incision may be held together with stitches, staples, skin glue, or skin closure strips. A bandage called addressing covers your incision. To prevent skin infections, keep your dressing clean and dry. Taking good care of your incision will help it heal more quickly.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 所需材料准备：列出切口护理所需物品——新绷带、医用胶带卷、干净剪刀、两副一次性手套、两块干净软布或纸巾、一次性塑料袋和废纸篓，并提醒将材料远离切口以降低感染风险。
text_en: Caring for your incision involves cleaning your incision and changing your dressing. You will need New bandages A roll of medical tape Clean scissors Two pairs of disposable gloves Two clean, soft cloths or paper towels A disposable plastic bag And a waste basket. Keep these materials away from your incision to reduce your risk of infection. Prepare materials

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 准备阶段操作：用肥皂和水洗手30秒并擦干，打开新绷带包装但不触碰绷带本体，剪四段与旧胶带等长的新胶带并挂在桌边备用。
text_en: Wash your hands with soap and water for 30 seconds and then dry them with a clean towel or paper towels. Clean hands are the best protection against spreading infection. Open a new bandage wrapper. Leave the bandage inside the wrapper and be careful not to touch it. Cut four pieces of tape and hang them from the edge of a nearby table or counter. The pieces of tape should be the same length as the old ones around your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 移除旧敷料：再次洗手后戴上一次性手套，一手按压皮肤、一手向切口方向撕开旧胶带，取下旧敷料。注意不要撕掉皮肤闭合条（让其自然脱落）。若敷料粘连切口，用温水湿润后再移除。检查旧敷料是否有异味或黄绿色污渍（如有则联系医疗提供者）。将旧敷料放入塑料袋，脱手套后一同丢弃。
text_en: Remove old dressing. Wash your hands again with soap and water and then dry them. Put on disposable gloves. With one hand, press down gently on the skin next to the old tape around your incision. With the other hand, carefully peel away the old tape toward your incision. After loosening all of the tape, remove your old dressing. Do not remove any skin closure strips covering your incision. Let them fall off on their own in about 5-10 days. If your dressing sticks to your incision, don't tear it off. Tearing it off may cause bleeding and delay healing. Moisten the dressing with warm water. Wait for a minute or two until you can easily remove it. Look at your old dressing after you remove it. If the dressing has a foul or sweet smell or it has yellow or greenish stain, call your healthcare provider. Place the old dressing in a disposable plastic bag. Remove the disposable gloves. Throw the gloves and the plastic bag containing your old dressing in the waste basket.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 清洁切口：仅在医疗提供者指示下清洁切口。洗手后戴新手套，用干净布、温水和（经许可的）温和抗菌肥皂轻柔清除切口周围干燥物质，从靠近切口处向外擦拭，每次使用布的不同部位。不要擦洗或去除痂皮（痂皮保护愈合中的切口），不要擦拭缝线、钉或闭合条上方，不要用酒精、碘或过氧化氢清洁切口。
text_en: Clean the incision. Clean your incision only if your healthcare provider says you should. Your healthcare provider may only want you to change the dressing. Make sure you understand what your healthcare provider wants you to do and be sure to call with questions or concerns. Wash your hands with soap and water and dry them. Put on a new pair of disposable gloves. Always use a clean cloth, warm water, and if your healthcare provider says you can, a mild antibacterial soap to gently remove dried material from the area around your incision. Start with the area's nearest urinsision and wipe outwards or away from the incision. Use a different part of the cloth each time you wipe your skin. When the cloth is dirty, use a new clean cloth. Do not scrub or remove any scabs. Scabs protect your incision while it heals. Allow them to fall off on their own. Do not wipe or rub over stitches, staples, or skin closure strips. Unless your healthcare provider says you should, do not clean your incision with alcohol, iodine, or peroxide.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 检查切口异常征象：让切口自然风干，同时检查是否存在以下异常——缝线/钉移位、切口边缘裂开、出血、切口边缘超过1英寸的发红、切口周围皮肤温度升高、肿胀、异味、白色/黄色/绿色分泌物。如发现任何变化应联系医疗提供者。未经许可不要在切口上使用药膏、乳液或粉末。
text_en: Let your incision air dry. As it dries, check your incision for the following. Stitches or staples that are out of place. The edges of your incision pulling open, bleeding around your incision, redness that extends more than one inch from your incision edges, increased skin warmth around your incision, swelling around your incision, foul or sweet smelling incision odor, or a white, yellow, or green discharge coming from your incision. Call your healthcare provider if you notice any of these changes. Unless your healthcare provider says you should, do not use, appointment, lotion, or powder on your incision.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 敷贴新敷料：脱旧手套丢弃，洗手后戴新手套。拿纱布垫一角、不触碰贴切口的那面，覆盖切口（如仍有渗液可多用几块纱布）。一手固定纱布、另一手用胶带封好四边。若旧胶带区域皮肤发红则换位置贴新胶带；若出现肿胀或水泡，可能是胶带过敏，需联系医疗提供者。脱手套丢弃后再次洗手。
text_en: Remove the disposable gloves and throw them away in the waste basket. Apply new dressing. Wash your hands with soap and water and dry them. Put on a new pair of disposable gloves. Pick up a gauze pad by one corner. Do not touch the side that will be next to your incision. Carefully cover your incision with the gauze pad. You may need more than one gauze pad if fluid is still coming out of your incision. Hold the gauze pad in place with one hand and tape the gauze pad to your skin with the other hand. Make sure that you seal all four edges of the dressing. If your skin is red from the tape on your old dressing, put the new tape on a different part of your skin. If your skin is swollen or has blisters, contact your healthcare provider. You may be allergic to the tape and need to try a different kind. Put your gloves and put them into the waste basket. Wash your hands again with soap and water and dry them.

title: AND13008 Incision Care Discharge Instructions
summary_zh: 术后警示征象总结：切口边缘轻微发红肿胀或清澈/粉色渗液属正常。但出现白/黄/绿色分泌物、异味、切口形状改变、超过1英寸的发红、皮肤温度升高、出血或肿胀时应联系医疗提供者。出现恶心、呕吐、腹泻或体温达101°F以上也应立即就医。
text_en: It's normal to see mild redness and swelling around your incision edges or a clear or pinkish However, you should contact your healthcare provider if you notice. Any white, yellow or green discharge coming from your incision. A foul or sweet incision odor, the shape of your incision changes. Redness that extends more than one inch from your incision edges, increased warmth of the skin around your incision, and bleeding or swelling around your incision. Also call your healthcare provider if you have nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or a temperature of 101 degrees Fahrenheit or higher.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 疼痛泵的基本结构与工作原理：术后疼痛泵通过皮下细管（导管）将麻醉药物持续缓慢地输送到手术切口附近，麻痹周围神经以减轻疼痛。导管连接泵体，管路上附有夹子和过滤器。
text_en: A pain pump is used after surgery to deliver numbing medicine directly to the area that was operated on. The medicine numbs the nerves near your incision, so you have less pain. The medicine continuously flows very slowly out of the pump through a small tube placed under the skin near your surgical incision. The small tube, called a catheter, connects to the pump. And a clamp. And filter are attached along the tube.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 疼痛泵的使用方式：可将泵放入小袋中随身携带，药物按外科医生设定的速率输送（不可调节），药物在2至4天内输完，泵排空后即可拔管并丢弃。
text_en: You can wear a small pouch to hold the pump so you can go about your daily activities. The pump gives you numbing medicine at a rate ordered by your surgeon. The rate on the pump cannot be changed. You receive the medicine over two to four days. When the pump is empty, you remove the tube and throw the pump and tube away.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 如何判断疼痛泵是否正常工作：药物流速极慢肉眼不可见，需24-48小时才能观察到球囊体积变化；正常工作的标志是手术部位感觉麻木、疼痛减轻、球囊逐渐缩小、外袋松弛起皱；泵排空时袋子变平，可触及中间硬管，通常需48-72小时。
text_en: How to tell if your pain pump is working? The pump delivers medicine so slowly that you cannot see it moving in the tube. It may take 24 to 48 hours before you notice a change in the size and look of the ball. You can tell when the pump is working if your surgical site feels numb and the medicine reduces your pain. The ball gradually gets smaller and smaller. And the outside bag on the pump gets looser and begins to wrinkle. The pump is empty when the bag looks flat and you can feel a hard tube in the middle. This usually takes 48 to 72 hours.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 疼痛泵的日常护理与故障处理：每天检查夹子是否通畅、过滤器无遮挡、管路无折弯；若泵或管路漏液、管路与泵分离、或管路从皮肤脱出，均应关闭夹子并联系外科医生，切勿自行重新连接或插回。
text_en: Carrying for your pain pump. Check your pain pump several times a day to make sure. The clamp is open and slides easily. Nothing is covering the filter. And there are no kinks or bends in the tubing. If you notice the pump or tube is leaking, close the clamp and call your surgeon. If the tube separates from the pump, do not reconnect it. Close the clamp and call your surgeon. If the tube comes out of your skin, do not put it back in. Close the clamp and call your surgeon.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 需要关闭夹子并立即联系医生的警告症状：耳鸣、视力模糊、口中金属味、手指脚趾或口周麻木刺痛、嗜睡或意识混乱、疼痛加重、发热寒战出汗、呼吸困难、管口周围红肿热痛、管口出血或渗液、头晕目眩。
text_en: You should also close the clamp on the tube and call your surgeon if you notice, ringing or buzzing in your ears. Blurred vision. A metal taste in your mouth. Numbness or tingling in your fingers, toes or around your mouth. Feel drowsy or confused. Increased pain. Fever chills or sweats. Trouble breathing. Redness, swelling, warmth or pain around the site where the tube enters your skin. Bleeding or discharge from the tube site. Feel dizzy or lightheaded.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 疼痛泵的防水与睡眠注意事项：泵、过滤器和管路须防水，不可泡澡或浸水，按医嘱淋浴；睡觉时将泵放在小袋中置于身旁被子上方，保持管路松弛，不可压在被子下、放地上或悬挂。
text_en: Make sure to protect your pump, filter and tube from water. Do not take a tub bath or put your pump underwater and be sure to follow your surgeon's instructions about showering. When sleeping, keep the pump inside of the small pouch and place it next to you on top of the covers. This helps keep the tube loose and in place. Do not put the pump under the covers on the floor or hang it from anything.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 拆除疼痛泵所需材料：创可贴和垃圾袋。
text_en: Materials for removing the pump. To remove your pain pump you will need an adhesive bandage and a trash bag. Removing the pump.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 拆除疼痛泵的7个步骤：洗手→松开管口敷料→移除固定胶带→贴近皮肤以90度角轻拉导管（若阻力大则停止联系医生以防断管）→检查管尖黑色标记确认完整取出→用创可贴覆盖管口→将管路、泵和敷料丢入垃圾袋。
text_en: Step 1. Wash your hands with soap and water and then dry them. Step 2. Gently loosen the bandage around the site where the tube enters your skin. Remove it by slowly peeling it back. Step 3. Remove the tape used to hold the tube in place. Step 4. Hold the tube close to the skin. Gently pull the tube out at a 90 degree angle. Do not tug or pull hard. The tube should come out easily. If it feels hard to remove or stretches, stop and call your surgeon. Continued pulling could break the tube. Step 5. After the tube comes out, check the tip for a black mark to make sure you removed the entire tube. Call your surgeon if you do not see the black mark. Step 6. Cover the site where the tube entered your skin with an adhesive bandage. Step 7. Throw away the tube, pump and bandages into a trash bag.

title: AND13015 Removing Your Pain Pump
summary_zh: 次日管口检查：洗手后取下创可贴检查管口部位，若出现红肿、疼痛或发热应联系外科医生。
text_en: Checking the tube entry site the next day. Step 1. Wash your hands again and then dry them. Step 2. Remove the adhesive bandage the next day and look at the site. If you have redness, pain or warmth at the site, call your surgeon.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 心肌梗死的定义与病理机制：心脏某区域血流严重减少，心肌细胞因供血不足而坏死。
text_en: A heart attack, also called a myocardial infarction, or MI, occurs when blood flow to an area of your heart is greatly reduced. When the blood supply is not able to keep up with the demand, the heart muscle cells don't get enough blood to stay alive. The result is death of heart muscle cells in the affected area of the heart.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 心梗后的康复目标与总体策略：保持心脏健康、预防再次心梗，康复时间因心肌损伤程度和个体差异而异；降低风险的三大方向——遵医嘱、改变生活方式、识别危险信号。
text_en: The goals after a heart attack are to keep your heart healthy and avoid another heart attack. Recovery takes time and the amount is different for each patient. It depends upon how much of your heart muscle is damaged and your individual rate of recovery. You may also have had a procedure. This may also affect your recovery time. You can lower your risk of having another heart attack by taking these steps, follow your doctor's instructions, make some lifestyle changes, and know when to seek medical attention.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 遵从医嘱的具体要求：按处方服药、不擅自停药或减量、注意药物副作用、避免自行服用非处方药或补充剂、告知医生其他处方药、按时复诊和定期检查（如血压监测）。
text_en: Following the instructions of your doctor and healthcare team is very important to your recovery. This includes taking your medications exactly as prescribed by your doctor. Don't stop taking your medications or skip doses. Take note of any side effects and tell your doctor. Don't take any over-the-counter medication or supplement without first consulting your doctor. Tell your doctor about any other prescriptions you're taking. Some medications can prevent your heart medicine from working. It's important that your recovery is monitored. Keep all of your follow-up appointments. Have exams done as directed by your doctor. For example, if your doctor has told you to check your blood pressure regularly, do as directed.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 饮食方面的生活方式调整：限制盐和钠的摄入（烹饪不加盐、避免加工食品和快餐、对比食品标签），采用心脏健康饮食（低脂、低胆固醇、高纤维），以维持健康的血压和体重。
text_en: Lowering your risk of another heart attack usually requires some lifestyle changes. Limit your salt and sodium intake by doing the following. Don't add salt when you cook or to food at the table. Avoid processed and fast foods. Compare food labels and choose the items that are lower in salt and sodium. Avoid a heart-healthy diet, including low fat, low cholesterol, and high fiber foods. A heart-healthy diet helps you maintain or achieve a good blood pressure and weight. It also helps prevent other conditions that may further stress your heart.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 其他生活方式调整：限制饮酒、保持健康体重、戒烟并远离二手烟；尼古丁会升高心率和血压，同时减少输送到身体的富氧血液。
text_en: Limit your alcohol intake. Risk your doctor if it's safe to include any alcohol in your diet. Maintain a healthy weight. Don't smoke. Avoid all tobacco products and secondhand smoke. Nicotine increases heart rate and blood pressure. At the same time, it decreases the amount of oxygen-rich blood delivered to your body's

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 心梗后的休息与活动恢复：前四周充分休息、保证睡眠、避免重体力劳动、逐步增加活动量；咨询医生何时可恢复驾驶、性生活和工作；数周后开始心脏康复计划（含监控下的运动训练）。
text_en: Allow yourself time to heal by taking it easy for the first four weeks. Getting a good night's sleep. Rest in the afternoon. Avoid heavy lifting and gradually increasing your physical activity. Ask your doctor when you can resume driving, sexual activity, and return to work. For weeks after your heart attack, you will begin cardiac rehab, which includes a closely monitored exercise regimen.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 心脏康复后的持续运动建议：通过日常活动（轻度家务和园艺）及医生批准的运动（步行、游泳、骑自行车）保持锻炼；运动注意事项——适时休息、胸痛或呼吸困难时停止、避免极端温度。
text_en: After completing the cardiac rehab program, continue with regular exercise through daily activities such as light yard and housework. Other good exercise options with your doctor's approval are walking, swimming, and bicycling. As you exercise, keep these precautions in mind. Rest as needed. Stop if you feel chest pain or have shortness of breath. And don't exercise when temperatures are very hot or very cold.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 压力与心理健康管理：压力影响血压和健康生活方式的维持，需识别和管理压力源；心梗后可能出现抑郁，可通过药物、心理咨询或互助小组获得帮助。
text_en: Stress can affect your blood pressure and your ability to maintain a healthy lifestyle. Identifying the causes of your stress and learning to eliminate or manage them can help with your recovery. You may feel depressed. Just these feelings with your doctor. He or she can prescribe medicine, counseling, or a support group to help you adjust.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 术后伤口监测与心绞痛症状识别：每日检查手术切口有无感染迹象（发热、红肿、疼痛、脓液、出血）；警惕胸部疼痛、压迫感、紧缩感等症状，这些可能提示心血管阻塞（心绞痛）。
text_en: It is important to stay aware of changes in your symptoms and know when to get help. If your hospital stay included a procedure with an incision, check the site each day for any signs of infection. Call your doctor if you develop a fever or redness, heat, pain, tenderness, pus, or bleeding at the incision site. Be mindful of any symptoms of chest pain, pressure, tightness, heaviness, ache, or any other kind of unusual sensation in your chest as any of these could mean you have a blockage in the blood vessels of the heart. Symptoms related to blockage in the blood vessels of the heart are called angina and could be any one of these symptoms.

file: AND14017_After a Heart Attack_ Managing Your Recovery.mp4
title: AND14017 After a Heart Attack  Managing Your Recovery
summary_zh: 心绞痛的放射部位与紧急处理：心绞痛可放射至手臂、肩部、颈部、下颌、喉咙或背部；出现心绞痛应联系医生；若药物不能缓解心绞痛、伴呼吸困难、冷汗或头晕，需立即拨打急救电话。
text_en: Angina symptoms may also be felt in your arms, shoulders, neck, jaw, throat, or back. Call your doctor if you think you're experiencing angina. Call 911 immediately if you have any of these symptoms. Angina that doesn't go away with medication is prescribed by your doctor. Shortness of breath. Cold sweats. Or lightheadedness.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 介绍抗凝药物（血液稀释剂）的用途：自行注射抗凝剂，帮助预防血管内血栓形成
text_en: This video will teach you how to give yourself a shot of anti-coagulant medicine, also called a blood thinner. Blood thinners help prevent clots from forming inside your blood vessels.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 注射前准备物品：酒精棉片、预充式抗凝剂注射器、利器盒；介绍注射器主要部件（药筒、推杆、针帽、针头）
text_en: Before you begin, you will need alcohol wipes, your pre-filled syringe of anti-coagulant medicine, and a sharp spot. The main parts of the syringe are the barrel containing the medicine, the plunger, the needle cap, and the needle.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 选择注射部位：肚脐左右两侧至少两英寸处或大腿；护理人员也可在背部、上臂或上臀部注射；每天更换注射部位以避免肿胀或瘀伤
text_en: Select your injection site at least two inches to the right or left of your belly button or on either of your thighs. A caregiver may also use these instructions to give you a shot on either side of your back, upper arms, or upper buttocks. Use a different injection site each day to avoid swelling or bruising.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 用药注意事项：每天同一时间注射，遵医嘱，不跳过剂量，不超量注射
text_en: And take your shot at the same time each day. Be sure to follow your doctor's instructions. Do not skip any doses. Do not inject more medicine than prescribed.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 步骤1-2：用肥皂和水洗手并擦干；用酒精棉片以画圈方式清洁注射部位皮肤
text_en: Step 1. Before you begin, wash your hands with soap and water and then dry them. Step 2. Clean the skin over the injection site by rubbing with an alcohol wipe in a circular motion.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 步骤3-4：撕开铝箔封盖取出注射器（不可推穿封盖）；侧握药筒直拔针帽，不要按压推杆排出气泡
text_en: Step 3. To open your syringe, peel the foil cover off the back. Do not push the syringe through the foil cover because it could damage the syringe. Step 4. Hold the syringe sideways by the barrel. Then, pull the needle cap straight off without bending the needle. There will be an air bubble in the medicine. Do not press the plunger to squirt the air bubble out of the barrel.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 步骤5-6：捏起注射部位约一英寸皮肤，以90度角将针头刺入皮下脂肪组织；缓慢推注全部药物（包括气泡）
text_en: Step 5. With your free hand, pinch and hold an inch of skin at the injection site. Insert the needle at a 90-degree angle through your skin and into the fatty tissue beneath it. Step 6. Slowly press the plunger to deliver all of the medicine, including the air bubble.

file: AND16019_How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot.mp4
title: AND16019 How To Give An Anticoagulant Shot
summary_zh: 步骤7-9：垂直拔出针头；用力按下推杆激活安全护针装置；将用过的注射器丢入利器盒
text_en: Step 7. Pull the needle straight out of your skin. Step 8. Press down firmly on the plunger to activate the safety guard which covers the needle. Step 9. Throw away the used syringe in your sharp spots.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的定义：也称充血性心力衰竭（CHF），是一种终身性疾病，心脏仍在工作但泵血功能不足，无法为身体提供足够的血液和氧气
text_en: Heart failure, also called congestive heart failure, or CHF, is a lifelong condition in which your heart continues to work, but not as well as it should. Your heart can't pump enough blood and oxygen to meet the needs of your body.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的症状：液体在肺部、腿部和其他组织中积聚，导致呼吸困难、慢性咳嗽或喘息、腿脚或腹部肿胀、体重增加、持续疲劳、恶心或食欲不振、心悸、难以平躺呼吸等。长期心衰会削弱心脏并缩短寿命
text_en: As fluid builds up in the lungs, legs, and other body tissues, the symptoms of heart failure include shortness of breath, chronic coughing or wheezing, swelling of the legs, feet or abdomen, weight gain, feeling tired all the time, nausea or lack of appetite, heart palpitations, difficulty lying flat due to feelings of suffocation, and having to sit up to breathe. Over time, heart failure weakens your heart and can shorten your life if not well-managed.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 管理心衰的总体策略：遵医嘱、采取有益心脏的生活方式、关注症状变化，遵从医疗团队指导以管理症状并减少住院
text_en: Fortunately, you can help control your symptoms by following your doctor's instructions, opting a heart-healthy lifestyle, and staying aware of changes in your symptoms. Following the instructions of your healthcare team is very important in managing your symptoms and preventing unnecessary trips to the hospital.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 药物治疗：严格按处方服药，不要停药或漏服，注意副作用。常用药物包括ACE抑制剂、ARB、ARNI、β受体阻滞剂、盐皮质激素受体拮抗剂（醛固酮阻滞剂）和SGLT2抑制剂。不要擅自服用非处方药或补充剂
text_en: This includes taking your medications exactly as prescribed by your doctor. Don't stop taking your medications or skip doses. Take note of any side effects and tell your doctor. If the cost of your medication is an issue, discuss other options with your doctor. Your medications may include angiotensin converting enzyme inhibitors, angiotensin-2 receptor blockers, or angiotensin receptor-neprolycin inhibitors, beta blockers. Mineralo corticoid receptor antagonists, also known as aldosterone blockers, or sodium glucose cotransporter-2 inhibitors. These medicines are important in managing heart failure. Don't take any over-the-counter medication or supplement without asking your doctor if

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 随访与疫苗：按时参加所有复诊和化验预约，按医嘱接种流感和肺炎疫苗
text_en: Make and keep all of your follow-up appointments for both doctor's visits and lab tests. Get vaccinated against the flu and pneumonia as directed by your doctor. Taking your heart failure also means adopting a healthy lifestyle.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 心脏健康饮食：低脂、低胆固醇、高纤维饮食，有助于维持良好血压和体重。限制每日钠摄入量在2000毫克以下，避免烹饪和餐桌加盐，远离加工食品和快餐，比较食品标签选择低钠食品
text_en: You may need to make the following changes. Eat a heart-healthy diet including low fat, localesterole, and high fiber foods. A heart-healthy diet helps you maintain or achieve a good blood pressure and weight and helps prevent other conditions that may further stress your heart. Check food labels for the amounts of these ingredients and others. Eat your salt and sodium intake to 2,000 milligrams a day, which is less than a teaspoon by doing the following. Don't add salt when you cook or to food at the table. Avoid processed and fast foods. Compare food labels and choose the items that are lower in salt and sodium. Limit your alcohol intake.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 限酒、限液、戒烟：限制酒精摄入，控制液体摄入以减轻心脏负担，戒烟——尼古丁会升高心率和血压，同时减少输送到身体的含氧血液
text_en: Ask your doctor if your condition allows you to include any alcohol in your diet. After your fluid intake, too much fluid makes your heart work harder. Ask your doctor how much fluid is the right amount for you. Don't smoke. Nicotine increases heart rate and blood pressure. At the same time, it decreases the amount of oxygen-rich blood delivered to your body's Become physically active.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 体力活动与心脏康复：通过日常活动和散步、游泳、骑车等运动保持活跃，注意运动时适当休息、出现胸痛或异常气短时停止、避免极端温度。医生可能还会处方心脏康复计划，包括医学监督下的运动、生活方式咨询和健康教育
text_en: Regular exercise through daily activities such as gardening and housework can make you feel better. Other good exercise options with your doctor's approval are walking, swimming and bicycling. As you exercise, keep these precautions in mind. Rest as needed. Stop if you feel chest pain or more than usual shortness of breath. And don't exercise when temperatures are very hot or very cold. Your doctor may also prescribe a cardiac rehabilitation program that is a combination of medically supervised exercise, lifestyle counseling and education.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 症状监测——绿灯区：用交通灯颜色评估症状状态。绿色表示症状受控，应继续遵医嘱、保持复诊。每天固定时间称重以监测体液潴留，记录体重变化
text_en: It is important to stay aware of changes in your symptoms and know when to get help. Using the traffic light colors, green, yellow and red to assess how you feel can help you take the proper action to manage your heart failure symptoms. Green is your goal and means that your symptoms are under control. Continue to follow your doctor's directions and keep all medical appointments. Wear yourself daily to see if your body is retaining fluids. Do it at the same time each day before eating or drinking and wear the same type of clothing or no clothes at all. Record and take note of any changes in your weight.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 症状监测——黄灯区（警告信号）：一天内体重增加2-3磅或一周增加5磅、呼吸困难加重、因呼吸困难难以入睡、持续快速或不规则心跳、头晕或晕厥感，应立即联系医生
text_en: Symptoms in the yellow zone are warning signs. Call your doctor immediately if you gained 2-3 or more pounds in one day or 5 pounds in Have an increased shortness of breath. Have difficulty sleeping due to trouble breathing. Will persistent, fast or irregular heartbeats are feeling light-headed or as if you may faint.

title: AND23020 Managing Heart Failure
summary_zh: 症状监测——红灯区（紧急情况）：胸痛持续超过15分钟、严重且持续的呼吸困难、咳出粉红色泡沫痰或晕厥，应立即拨打急救电话911
text_en: Red zone symptoms are emergencies that mean you need medical evaluation right away. Call 911 if you experience chest pain that lasts more than 15 minutes. Severe don going shortness of breath. Coughing up pink frothy foam or fainting.

title: ANH00007 General Anesthesia
summary_zh: 术前静脉通路建立：通过套管针在手臂静脉建立IV通路，用于输注液体和药物直接进入血液，并可给予镇静药物帮助患者放松。
text_en: Before your procedure, an IV line will be placed in a vein in your arm using a small tube called a cannula. The IV will deliver fluid and medications directly into your bloodstream. You may receive some medication to help you relax.

title: ANH00007 General Anesthesia
summary_zh: 术前监护设备安置：患者被安置在手术台上，依次放置血压袖带监测血压、胸部电极片监测心率、指夹式脉搏血氧仪监测血氧饱和度，麻醉医师通过这些设备在术前、术中和术后持续监测生命体征。
text_en: You will be placed on the operating table and made as comfortable as possible. A blood pressure cuff will be placed on your arm to check your blood pressure readings. Sticky pads will be placed on your chest to check your heart rate. And a clip will be put on your finger to check your body's oxygen levels. These devices allow the anesthesia specialist to closely monitor your vital signs before, during and after your procedure.

title: ANH00007 General Anesthesia
summary_zh: 麻醉诱导与气道管理：通过面罩吸入麻醉气体或静脉注射药物使患者入睡，随后经口插入气管导管至气管内，导管连接呼吸机辅助呼吸并输送麻醉气体至肺部。
text_en: You will begin receiving general anesthesia by either breathing anesthetic gases through a mask or through IV injection, which will cause you to fall asleep. Once you are asleep, you will be given a mixture of oxygen and anesthetic gases either through your mask or through a special tube inserted through your mouth and into your windpipe. The tube is attached to a respirator, which helps you breathe while delivering the gases to your lungs.

title: ANH00007 General Anesthesia
summary_zh: 全身麻醉的作用机制：麻醉气体在肺组织深处被吸收进入血液，由血细胞携带至大脑，阻断大脑接收来自身体神经的信号，使患者在手术期间保持无意识、无痛觉，且醒后无手术记忆。
text_en: Deep in your lung tissues, the gases are absorbed into your bloodstream and carried by blood cells to your brain. The anesthesia prevents your brain from receiving messages from nerves in your body. As a result, you will remain asleep and pain-free during your procedure, and you will have no memory of it when you wake up.

title: ANH00007 General Anesthesia
summary_zh: 术后苏醒与恢复：麻醉医师给予拮抗药物逆转麻醉效果，患者快速苏醒后拔除气管导管；转入恢复室密切监护并按需给予镇痛药物；患者可能出现短暂头晕和轻微意识模糊，待生命体征稳定后转入病房或出院。
text_en: After your operation, the anesthesia specialist will give you medications to reverse the anesthesia, and you will awaken quickly. If you had a breathing tube in place, he or she will remove it as soon as you can safely breathe on your own. You will be taken to the recovery room where you will be closely monitored, and given pain medication is needed. You may feel light-headed and slightly disoriented, but this feeling should pass quickly. Once you are stable, you will be sent to a hospital room or home.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 引言：说明当心脏传导系统（控制心跳的电信号系统）无法正常工作时，外科医生会植入起搏器。
text_en: A surgeon will insert a pacemaker into your body if your heart's conduction system, the electrical system that makes your heart beat, is not working properly.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 心脏基本解剖结构：心脏是四腔肌性器官，血液先汇入上方两个心房，再充入下方两个心室；右心室将血液泵向肺部，左心室将血液泵向全身。
text_en: The heart is a muscular organ with four chambers. Blood collects in the heart's two upper chambers or atria before filling the two lower chambers or ventricles. In the ventricles contract, blood in the right ventricle is pumped to the lungs and blood in the left ventricle is pumped to the rest of the body.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 心脏传导系统：窦房结（SA结）作为天然起搏器控制心率，电信号经房室结（AV结）和束支传导至心室，这些通路统称为心脏传导系统。
text_en: The heart has a natural pacemaker called the Sinoatrial node or SA node. The pacemaker controls the heart rate by precisely determining when the muscles of the atria and ventricles contract. The electrical signal produced by the SA node reaches the ventricles through a narrow pathway that includes the so-called AV node and bundle branches. Taken together, these pathways are referred to as the conduction system of the heart.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 传导系统异常：窦房结功能障碍可导致心跳过快、过慢或不规则；束支传导阻滞可使心跳变慢或不规则。这些问题可由心脏病、药物或心脏手术引起。
text_en: Problems with the conduction system can result if the SA node malfunctions, causing the heart to beat too fast, too slow or irregularly. Alternatively, a blockage in the bundle branches can develop, causing the heart to beat more slowly or irregularly. These problems can be due to heart disease, medications, or cardiac surgery.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 术前准备：进行血液和尿液检查、胸部X光检查、心电图（EKG）记录心脏电活动，并在植入部位注射局部麻醉剂。
text_en: Before the procedure, your blood and urine will be tested. You will also have x-rays taken of your chest, and an electrocardiogram or EKG will record the electrical activity in your heart. A local anesthetic will be injected into your skin to numb the area where the pacemaker will be inserted.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 起搏器植入手术过程：在锁骨下方做小切口，将导线经锁骨下静脉送入右心房，通过X光影像引导定位；测试导线后连接脉冲发生器，将其放入皮下囊袋中，最后缝合切口。
text_en: The surgeon will make a small incision beneath your collarbone. The pacemaker's leads will be inserted into the subclavian vein, and then threaded toward your heart until they reach the right atrium. Your surgeon will view x-ray images on a video screen to position the leads correctly. Once the leads have been tested, they will be connected to the pulse generator. The pulse generator will then be placed in a small pouch that your surgeon will create under the skin beneath your collarbone. Your surgeon will then sew the incision back together.

title: ANH00010 Pacemaker Insertion
summary_zh: 术后恢复与随访：住院期间监测心率和血压以确认起搏器正常工作，出院前调整起搏器参数；术后可用非处方止痛药缓解疼痛，约两周内不能驾车，需定期复诊检查和调整起搏器。
text_en: While you're in the hospital, your doctor will monitor your heart rate and blood pressure to be certain the pacemaker is working properly. Before you leave, he or she will make final adjustments to the pacemaker settings. After your procedure, over the counter pain medications such as acetaminophen or ibuprofen should be sufficient to relieve any pain. You will not be able to drive for about two weeks. Periodically, you will need to follow up with your doctor to test and adjust your new

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 主动脉解剖结构：主动脉是人体最大的动脉，连接心脏左心室，分为升主动脉、主动脉弓、胸主动脉和腹主动脉四部分。
text_en: The largest artery in the body is the aorta, which is attached to the heart and receives blood directly from the left ventricle or main pumping chamber. The aorta is divided into four parts, the ascending aorta, aortic arch, thoracic aorta, and the abdominal aorta.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 腹主动脉的功能与结构：腹主动脉是主动脉最后也是最大的部分，向腹部和盆腔器官及下肢供血，在脐部水平分为两条髂动脉；主动脉壁厚实，能承受心脏泵血的高压。
text_en: The abdominal aorta is the last and largest section of the aorta. It supplies blood to the lower regions of your body, including the organs in the abdomen and pelvis. The abdominal aorta ends around naval level, where it splits into the two iliac arteries that carry blood to the legs. The aorta has a thick wall so it can withstand the high pressure of blood being pumped from the heart.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 腹主动脉瘤的形成：随着时间推移，主动脉壁可能变弱，像气球一样向外膨出，当这种情况发生在腹部区域时称为腹主动脉瘤。
text_en: Over time, the wall of the aorta may weaken, slowly bulging outward like a balloon. When this condition occurs in the abdominal region, it is called an abdominal aortic aneurysm.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 开放手术适应证：当主动脉直径超过两英寸，或出现剧烈腹痛、异常腹部搏动等症状时，外科医生将进行开放式腹主动脉瘤修复。
text_en: Your surgeon will perform an open abdominal aortic aneurysm repair when the diameter of your aorta exceeds two inches, or if you're having symptoms such as intense abdominal pain, or an abnormally prominent abdominal pulsation.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 开放手术术前准备：建立静脉通路输注液体、抗生素和麻醉药；全身麻醉下插入气管插管辅助呼吸；插入导尿管监测肾功能。
text_en: Before the open procedure an IV line will be started to provide you with fluids, antibiotics, and anesthesia. A traditional open surgical repair is performed under general anesthesia, which means you will be asleep for the duration of the procedure. A breathing tube will be inserted through your mouth and into your windpipe to help you breathe during the operation. When you're asleep, a catheter will be inserted into your bladder to drain your urine and monitor your kidney function.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 开放手术操作过程：从胸骨至脐下做切口，定位动脉瘤并在其上下钳夹主动脉，切开动脉瘤缝合人工移植物，将动脉瘤壁缝合覆盖移植物以加固主动脉壁，最后缝合腹部切口，手术通常耗时4-6小时。
text_en: Your surgeon will begin by making an incision from the breastbone to below the navel. He or she will then locate the aneurysm and clamp the aorta just above and below it. Your surgeon will then open the aneurysm and sew an artificial graft, the same size and shape as a healthy aorta, into place. Your surgeon will stitch the wall of the aneurysm closed over the newly placed graft. The graft will reinforce the walls of the aorta. Your surgeon will close the abdominal incision with stitches. The total length of this procedure is usually four to six hours.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 血管内修复术适应证与术前准备：适用于严重心脏病或因年龄等原因手术风险较高的患者；建立静脉通路，可选全麻或局部麻醉（镇静剂+硬膜外/区域麻醉）。
text_en: Your surgeon will perform endovascular aneurysm repair, a newer, minimally invasive procedure, if you have severe heart disease or are at increased surgical risk due to age or other medical conditions. Before the endovascular procedure, as with the open procedure, an IV line will be started to provide you with fluids and antibiotics. You may receive general anesthesia or you may receive a sedative and an epidural or regional anesthesia to numb only the lower half of your body.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 血管内修复术操作过程：在腹股沟股动脉处做小切口，插入导丝推进至动脉瘤部位，通过导管在X线引导下输送压缩的支架移植物至动脉瘤处并展开，用金属钩固定；可插入额外移植物组件延伸至下肢血管；血流通过移植物流动，不再进入动脉瘤囊，解除动脉瘤压力；最后撤除导管缝合切口，手术约2-3小时。
text_en: Your surgeon will begin by making a small incision in your groin over your femoral artery. Then he or she will insert a guide wire into the artery and gently push it up toward the site of your aneurysm. A catheter or hollow tube will be passed over the guide wire and pushed toward your aneurysm. During live x-ray pictures as a guide, your surgeon will pass an endovascular stent graft, a compressed fabric and metal tube, through the catheter to the aneurysm. Then he or she will open the graft which will be held in place with metallic hooks and Your surgeon may insert additional graft components to extend the graft into the blood vessels supplying your legs. Once the graft is in place, your blood will flow through it, not into the surrounding aneurysm sac which will remove the pressure from your aneurysm. Finally, your surgeon will remove the catheters and close the incisions in your groin. The procedure will generally take 2-3 hours.

title: ANH00012 Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm
summary_zh: 术后恢复：拔除气管插管后转入ICU，通过静脉输液补充营养，可能经鼻插胃管引流分泌物直至肠道恢复功能；开放手术住院5-10天，血管内修复术住院2-3天。
text_en: After your procedure, if you had a breathing tube, it will be removed and you will be taken to the intensive care unit for recovery. You will receive fluids and nutrition through your IV. You may also have a tube inserted through your nose and into your stomach to remove secretions until your intestines regain normal functioning. The usual hospital stay for an open procedure is 5-10 days, while the stay for an endovascular procedure is usually shorter at 2-3 days.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
summary_zh: 血液的基本功能：运输氧气和营养物质、带走二氧化碳和废物、抵抗感染和愈合伤口
text_en: Your blood carries oxygen and nutrients to all of your tissues and removes carbon dioxide and other waste products from them. Blood also helps fight infection and heal wounds.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
summary_zh: 血液的组成成分：液体部分（血浆，含营养素、激素、凝血因子等）和细胞成分（红细胞运输氧气和二氧化碳、白细胞抵抗感染、血小板辅助凝血）
text_en: Blood consists of a liquid portion and cellular components. The liquid called plasma is where nutrients, hormones, clotting factors and other chemicals are dissolved. The cellular components are suspended in the plasma. They consist of red blood cells which transport oxygen and carbon dioxide. White blood cells which fight infection. Blood cell and platelets which are small subcells that help the blood to clot.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
summary_zh: 血细胞在骨髓中生成；输血利用供体血液补充红细胞、白细胞、血小板、凝血因子、血浆或全血
text_en: Blood cells are made in the bone marrow, a soft tissue housed inside many of your bones. A blood transfusion uses donor blood to replenish red blood cells, white blood cells, platelets, clotting factors, plasma or whole blood.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
summary_zh: 需要输血的常见情况：创伤失血、心脏或大手术、器官移植、血友病等出血性疾病、严重贫血（含镰状细胞贫血）、白血病或其他癌症治疗、骨髓破坏性疾病、新生儿Rh血型不合
text_en: Some stances that may require a blood transfusion include blood loss due to trauma, heart or other major surgery, organ transplants, bleeding disorders such as hemophilia, severe anemia including sickle cell anemia, treatment for leukemia or other types of cancer, disorders that destroy blood cells or bone marrow or RH incompatibility in newborn babies.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
summary_zh: 输血操作过程：在手部静脉插入静脉针，悬挂供体血袋，血液通过管路缓慢滴入静脉进入血液循环；全程监测体温、心率、呼吸和血压
text_en: To begin the transfusion an intravenous needle will be inserted into a vein in your hand A bag containing the donor blood will be hung nearby and the blood will drip slowly through a tube attached to the IV needle, allowing it to run through your vein into your bloodstream. Throughout the transfusion your temperature, heart rate, breathing and blood pressure will be monitored.

title: ANH00016 Blood Transfusion
summary_zh: 输血完成与术后处理：血袋用完可更换新袋；输血结束后拔除针头；医生监测生命体征以排查不良反应；必要时使用利尿剂减轻水肿或液体潴留
text_en: After the bag is empty if you require more blood, another bag will be attached to the When your transfusion is completed, the needle will be removed. After your transfusion, your doctor will monitor your vital signs to make sure you're not having an adverse reaction. If necessary, you may be given a diuretic to reduce any swelling or fluid retention.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
summary_zh: 介绍电复律的定义：当患者出现心律异常时，医生通过电击恢复正常心跳节律。
text_en: If you have an abnormal heart rhythm, your doctor may perform electrical cardioversion, where he or she uses an electrical shock to restore your normal heart beat.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
summary_zh: 正常心脏电传导系统：心脏正常以60-100次/分的规律节律跳动，电信号起源于右心房壁的窦房结（SA结），传导至心肌引起收缩泵血。
text_en: The heart normally beats in a regular rhythm at 60 to 100 beats per minute. This steady and coordinated rhythm allows the heart to effectively pump blood. The electrical signals that travel through the heart control the heart's rhythm. These electrical signals start in a cluster of cells called the Sinoatrial node or pacemaker. The Sinoatrial node or SA node is located in the wall of the right upper chamber of the heart, called the right atrium. When the electrical signals reach the heart muscle, they cause it to contract and pump blood.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
summary_zh: 房颤及其并发症：房颤时心房因混乱电信号而颤动，导致心跳异常加快或不规则；血液在心房内淤积可形成血栓，血栓进入循环系统可导致中风。
text_en: An abnormal heart rhythm is called an arrhythmia. During atrial fibrillation, the atria quiver because of chaotic electrical signals in the heart, resulting in an abnormally fast or irregular heartbeat. Because of the abnormal heartbeat, blood may pool in the atria, which can result in the formation of a blood clot. If the blood clot passes into the general circulation, it may cause a stroke.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
summary_zh: 电复律手术操作流程：建立静脉通路并镇静，放置心电图电极监测心脏电活动，在胸部放置复律电极片或电极板，通过电击使心脏恢复正常节律，必要时重复电击。
text_en: Before the procedure, you will be connected to an intravenous line. You will be sedated so you will sleep through the procedure. EKG electrodes will be placed on your chest to monitor your heart's electrical activity. Once you are sedated, special cardioversion pads or paddles will be placed on the skin of your chest or your chest and back. These pads or paddles will deliver electrical shock through your chest to your heart. The shock may be repeated several times until the heart resumes its normal rhythm.

title: ANH00019 Cardioversion
summary_zh: 术后恢复：患者在恢复室或冠心病监护病房接受密切监测，待镇静消退且病情稳定后出院。
text_en: After your electrical cardioversion, you will be monitored closely in a recovery room or in the coronary care unit of the hospital. Once the sedation wears off and you are in stable condition, you will be set home.

title: ANH00023 Non-cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (NCPTA)
summary_zh: 动脉粥样硬化与血管狭窄的病理机制：脂肪沉积形成粥样硬化斑块，堵塞动脉通道，导致血流受阻（狭窄/stenosis），引起组织缺血疼痛
text_en: transliminal angioplasty, or NCPTA, to restore blood flow in an artery blocked by an atherosclerotic plaque. Fatty deposits called atherosclerotic plaques can build up inside your arteries, narrowing the passageway and interfering with blood flow. This arterial blockage is called stenosis. Insufficient blood flow to a tissue generally causes pain. A non-cardiac percutaneous

title: ANH00023 Non-cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (NCPTA)
summary_zh: 手术适应症与术前说明：NCPTA仅在生活方式改变和药物治疗等保守治疗无效时才进行；患者被置于配有透视机的手术台上
text_en: transliminal angioplasty is only done when your condition has not responded to less invasive treatment options, such as lifestyle changes and medications. To begin the procedure, you'll

title: ANH00023 Non-cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (NCPTA)
summary_zh: 血管穿刺与导管置入过程：透视机可多角度拍摄X光影像；医生在皮肤局部麻醉后，通常经腹股沟股动脉穿刺，通过空心针插入带球囊的导管，并在透视引导下推进导管至目标动脉
text_en: be placed on a table equipped with a fluoroscope. A fluoroscope is capable of taking x-ray images of many parts of the body from multiple angles. Your doctor will numb your skin around where the catheter is to be inserted. Since the arteries of the leg are most easily accessed, the femoral artery in the groin serves as the insertion site in most cases. Your doctor will initially gain access to the artery by inserting a hollow needle through which the balloon-tipped catheter is passed. Watching on the fluoroscopy screen, your doctor will gently advance the catheter until it

title: ANH00023 Non-cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (NCPTA)
summary_zh: 造影定位与球囊扩张过程：注入造影剂拍摄血管造影图，精确定位阻塞位置；将球囊定位于狭窄处，反复快速充气放气，将斑块和血管壁向外推开，部分恢复血流
text_en: is positioned in the artery being treated and eject a special die. This die allows the fluoroscope to take x-ray images called angiograms, which lets your doctor identify the exact location of the blockages. Next, your doctor will move the catheter until the deflated balloon at its tip is positioned at the site of the stenosis. Then, by rapidly inflating and deflating the balloon, your doctor will gently push the plaque and vessel wall out, partially reestablishing blood flow.

title: ANH00023 Non-cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (NCPTA)
summary_zh: 支架植入过程：对于额外阻塞可重复球囊扩张；部分病例需植入金属网状支架，将折叠支架套在球囊上送入血管，球囊充气时支架膨胀并锁定在血管壁上，保持血管通畅
text_en: This procedure may be repeated for any additional blockages. In some cases, your doctor may place a stent, which is a wire mesh that remains in the artery to hold it open. To perform this procedure, your doctor will place a collapsed stent over the deflated balloon on the tip of the catheter. When the balloon is inflated, the stent expands and locks into place.

title: ANH00023 Non-cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (NCPTA)
summary_zh: 术后处理与恢复：拔除导管，穿刺部位加压包扎，患者需平卧防止出血；在恢复区监测血流情况，多数患者当日出院，部分需住院观察一至两天
text_en: At the end of the procedure, your doctor will remove the catheter through the original insertion site. After your procedure, a pressure bandage will be applied to the catheter insertion site, and you will be asked to lie still to prevent bleeding. You will be carefully monitored in the recovery area to be sure blood flow through the affected artery is maintained. Most patients are discharged the same day as their procedure, but some remain in the hospital one to two days for observation.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 心脏基本解剖结构：介绍心脏的四个腔室（左右心房、左右心室）、各腔室之间的连接关系（右心室连接肺动脉、左心室连接主动脉），以及瓣膜的位置和单向血流功能
text_en: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood through the body. It consists of four chambers. The upper chambers are the right and left atria. The lower chambers are the right and left ventricles. The right and left atria connect to their respective ventricles. The right ventricle connects to the pulmonary artery and the left ventricle to the aorta. Valves are located at these connections to ensure that blood moves in only one direction.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 主动脉瓣的结构与正常功能：瓣膜由称为瓣叶的组织瓣组成，主动脉瓣位于左心室与主动脉之间，心脏收缩时瓣膜打开将血液泵入主动脉，舒张时关闭防止血液回流
text_en: The parts of a valve that open and close are flaps of tissue called cusps. The aortic valve is located between the left ventricle and the aorta.
