When the heart contracts, the aortic valve opens and blood is pumped into the aorta. When the heart relaxes, it closes to prevent blood from leaking back into the left ventricle.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 主动脉瓣病变类型：正常瓣膜有三个光滑瓣叶；瓣膜关闭不全导致血液反流（主动脉瓣反流）；瓣膜狭窄导致开放受限、血流减少（主动脉瓣狭窄）
text_en: A normal aortic valve has three smooth cusps that open widely and close tightly. Your aortic valve may be leaky or narrowed, causing your heart to work harder and disrupt your blood flow. A leaky valve does not close all the way, causing blood to leak back into the ventricle between each heartbeat. This condition is called aortic regurgitation. A narrowed aortic valve does not open all the way, which restricts the amount of blood passing out of the ventricle into the aorta. This condition is called aortic stenosis.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 主动脉瓣病变的病因：先天性异常（如二叶式主动脉瓣）；后天获得性病变包括风湿热（链球菌咽喉炎并发症）、心内膜炎（细菌感染）和主动脉瓣叶硬化
text_en: You may have been born with an abnormal aortic valve. For example, your aortic valve may only have two cusps instead of three, so it may not open and close properly. Your aortic valve may have become damaged during your lifetime, a condition called acquired aortic valve disease. Causes include rheumatic fever, which can be a complication of untreated strep throat. Andocarditis, which is a bacterial infection of the valve, and sclerosis or hardening of the aortic leaflets.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 主动脉瓣病变的后果与症状：长期瓣膜故障导致心肌肥大或变薄，最终发展为心力衰竭，症状包括呼吸困难、胸痛（心绞痛）、头晕晕厥、下肢水肿和疲劳
text_en: After a period of time, a faulty aortic valve can cause the heart muscles to become enlarged and weakened. Depending on the nature of the valve's defect, the walls of the left ventricle may become abnormally thick or thin, and the problem can eventually affect the right side of the heart as well. Patients with this condition known as heart failure may experience shortness of breath, chest pain or angina pectoris, dizziness, lightheadedness, and or fainting, swelling of the ankles, feet and legs, and fatigue.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 术前准备：建立静脉通路、留置导尿管、全身麻醉、气管插管辅助呼吸
text_en: An aortic valve replacement is performed to restore the valve to normal function. Before your procedure, an IV line will be started in your arm to provide fluids and medications. A catheter will be inserted into your bladder to drain urine. Aortic valve replacements are done under general anesthesia. This means you will be asleep for the duration of the operation, and a tube will be placed through your mouth and into your windpipe to help you breathe during surgery.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 手术入路：胸骨正中切口，分离胸骨暴露心包膜，打开心包暴露心脏
text_en: Once the anesthesia takes effect, your surgeon will begin by making an incision in the chest. The traditional incision is made in the middle of the chest, overlying the breastbone or sternum. The surgeon then separates the sternum to expose the pericardium, which is a protective membrane encircling the heart. Your surgeon will then open the pericardium to expose the heart.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 体外循环建立：连接心肺机，通过管路将血液引流至机器代替心肺功能，心脏暂时停跳并排空血液，使主动脉瓣静止便于手术操作
text_en: At this point, your heart will be connected to a heart lung machine. Tubes are used to rerot your blood into this machine, which takes over the functions of the heart and lungs during the operation. Once the heart lung machine has taken over, your heart will be temporarily stopped and the blood inside removed. This keeps the aortic valve still and allows your surgeon to see it.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 瓣膜置换核心操作：切开主动脉暴露病变瓣膜，沿边缘切除旧瓣膜，缝合植入人工瓣膜，缝合主动脉，恢复血流检查新瓣膜功能
text_en: Your surgeon will open the aorta to expose the aortic valve, make an incision around the edge of the damaged valve, and remove it. Your surgeon will then carefully sew the replacement valve into place and close the aorta with stitches. At this point, blood will again be allowed to flow into your heart to check the function of the new valve.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 心脏复跳与脱离体外循环：必要时电击除颤重启心脏，心脏自主搏动后脱离心肺机，放置临时起搏导线确保恢复期心律正常
text_en: If needed, an electric shock will be given to restart the heart. Once your heart is beating on its own, it will be detached from the heart lung machine. Generally, temporary pacemaker wires will be placed on the surface of the heart to ensure the heart maintains a normal rhythm during recovery.

title: ANH00026 Aortic Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 术后监护：转入重症监护室监测生命体征，保留气管插管（无法说话）、胸腔引流管和导尿管
text_en: After surgery, you'll be taken to the intensive care unit where your vital functions will be monitored. You will still have a breathing tube in your throat, which will prevent you from talking. Several tubes will be in place to drain blood and fluid from your incision, and your bladder catheter will still be draining your urine.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
summary_zh: 颈动脉解剖：颈部有两条主要血管称为颈动脉，将富氧血液从心脏输送到大脑，可在颈侧触及其脉搏
text_en: In your neck are two major blood vessels called the carotid arteries, which carry oxygen rich blood from your heart to your brain. You can feel the pulse of the carotid artery in the side of your neck.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
summary_zh: 动脉粥样硬化与后果：脂肪沉积物（粥样硬化斑块）在颈动脉内壁堆积，导致管腔狭窄（狭窄症），可引起短暂性脑缺血发作（TIA）；血流完全阻断则导致中风
text_en: Three deposits called atherosclerotic plaques can build up inside the carotid artery. These plaques can narrow the passageway of the artery and interfere with blood flow to the brain. This blockage or narrowing is called a stenosis. If blood flow to the brain is blocked temporarily, a transient ischemic attack, also known as a TIA, may occur. When the blood flow is completely obstructed, as in a carotid artery blockage, a stroke

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
summary_zh: 术前准备与麻醉：建立静脉通路，通常采用全身麻醉并插入气管导管辅助呼吸；也可采用局部麻醉，患者保持清醒但颈部麻木，并给予适度镇静
text_en: Before your procedure, an intravenous line will be started. A carotid and dardirectomy is usually done under general anesthesia, which will put you to sleep for the duration of the procedure. In this case, a breathing tube will be inserted through your mouth and into your windpipe to help you breathe during the operation. Sometimes a carotid and dardirectomy is done with local anesthesia. If local anesthesia is used, you will remain awake, but your neck will be numbed. You will probably also receive some sedation.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
summary_zh: 手术切开与暴露动脉：沿阻塞动脉在颈侧做切口（从耳后到锁骨上方），找到颈动脉并临时夹闭血流，切开动脉后可放置分流管以维持脑部供血
text_en: On the side of your neck along the blocked artery, the surgeon will make an incision that may run from just behind the ear to a point above the collarbone. Your surgeon will find the carotid artery and temporarily clamp off blood flow through Your surgeon will open up the artery and may place a shunt or temporary bypass to keep blood flowing to the brain.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
summary_zh: 斑块切除与动脉修复：外科医生剥离动脉壁内的斑块，随后缝合动脉并通常放置补片（取自自体静脉或人工材料如涤纶）以略微扩宽动脉管腔
text_en: Your surgeon will then remove the plaque within the arterial walls. After the blockage has been removed, here she will sew the artery back together, usually placing a patch to widen the artery slightly. The patch may be made of part of one of your veins or an artificial material such as Finally, your surgeon will close the incision with sutures or staples and cover it with

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
summary_zh: 切口关闭：用缝线或钉合器关闭切口并覆盖敷料，伤口内常留置临时引流管
text_en: A temporary drain is frequently left in the wound.

title: ANH00028 Carotid Endarterectomy
summary_zh: 术后恢复：患者被送至恢复区监测生命体征和神经功能状态，按需给予止痛药，饮食从流质逐步过渡到固体食物
text_en: After your operation, you will be taken to a recovery area where the staff will monitor your vital signs and neurologic condition. You will be given pain medication as needed and will gradually progress from a liquid to a solid diet.

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
summary_zh: 静脉回流机制：与动脉依靠心脏泵血不同，静脉依靠肌肉收缩和瓣膜辅助血液回流，防止血液倒流和淤积
text_en: A condition where a blood clot forms in a deep vein in the body, typically the leg. Unlike arteries which rely on the heart to force blood through them, veins transport blood with the help of muscle contractions while you move and walk. Plans also have valves that keep blood from flowing backward and pooling in your arms and legs.

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
summary_zh: 深静脉血栓形成过程：长期不活动导致静脉循环减慢，凝血因子使缓慢流动的血液凝聚形成血栓（DVT）
text_en: When you are a mobile for an extended period, your venous circulation slows down. As a result, clotting factors in the blood may cause the slow moving blood to clump together, forming a blood clot or thrombus. This condition is called deep vein thrombosis or DVT.

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
summary_zh: DVT常见症状：腿部肿胀、静脉肿胀、疼痛或压痛、皮肤温度升高、皮肤发红或变色
text_en: Common symptoms of DVT include swelling of the leg or a vein in the leg, pain or tenderness in the area, increased warmth in the skin, and red or discolored skin.

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
summary_zh: 肺栓塞形成过程：血栓从深静脉脱落，经腹部静脉进入心脏，最终堵塞肺动脉或其分支，阻断肺部血流，可造成肺损伤甚至死亡
text_en: In some DVT cases, a blood clot may detach from a deep vein and join the bloodstream. The clot travels from the deep veins of the leg through the veins of the abdomen, into the heart, and finally lodges in the pulmonary artery or its branches, blocking blood flow to the lungs. This condition called pulmonary embolism is very serious and may permanently damage a portion of the lung. If a clot is large or if there are many clots, pulmonary embolism may also cause death.

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
summary_zh: 肺栓塞症状：呼吸急促、突发胸部剧痛、头晕目眩、心跳加快或不规则、咳血
text_en: If you experience symptoms of PE, please tell your doctor or nurse immediately. These symptoms may include shortness of breath, a sudden sharp pain in the chest, a feeling of lightheadedness or dizziness, rapid or irregular heartbeat, or coughing that produces

file: ANH00029_Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism.mp4
title: ANH00029 Prevention of Deep Vein Thrombosis (DVT) and Pulmonary Embolism
summary_zh: 预防措施：定期走动防止血液淤积、穿弹力压缩袜、服用抗凝药物
text_en: To help prevent deep vein thrombosis and pulmonary embolism, your doctor may require you to do the following. Walk around periodically to keep the blood from pooling in your legs, wear compression stockings, and take anti-clothing medication.

title: ANH11047 Atrial Fibrillation
summary_zh: 房颤的定义：房颤是一种心律失常，心房以快速、颤动的方式收缩，导致心跳不规则、不协调。
text_en: Atrial fibrillation is a type of arrhythmia, which is an abnormality in the pace or force of your heartbeat. If you have atrial fibrillation, the heart's upper chambers contract in a fast, twitchy fashion, resulting in an irregular, uncoordinated heartbeat.

title: ANH11047 Atrial Fibrillation
summary_zh: 正常心脏结构与血液循环：心脏由四个腔室组成（左右心房和左右心室），正常心跳时各腔室有节律地收缩和舒张，右心房收集全身血液经右心室泵入肺部进行气体交换，左心房接收肺部含氧血经左心室泵向全身。
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ, made up of four chambers, the right and left atria, and the right and left ventricles. During a normal heartbeat, the chambers of your heart contract and relax in a rhythmic pattern to deliver a consistent flow of blood throughout your body. The right atrium collects blood from the body, and the right ventricle pumps it to the lungs, which bring oxygen to and remove carbon dioxide from the blood. At the same time, the left atrium receives blood from the lungs, and the left ventricle pumps it out to supply your body's tissues with oxygen and nutrients.

title: ANH11047 Atrial Fibrillation
summary_zh: 正常心脏电传导系统：窦房结（SA结）位于右心房壁，产生电信号扩散至两个心房使其收缩，信号经房室结（AV结）传至心室壁引起心室收缩，心室舒张后完成一次完整心跳，随即SA结产生新的电脉冲重复该过程。
text_en: Each heartbeat begins in the heart's natural pacemaker called the Sinoatrial or SA node located in the wall of the right atrium. An electrical signal starts here, then spreads across both atria, causing them to contract and push blood into the ventricles. As the atria relax, the signal travels through the atrial ventricular or AV node to the ventricular walls, causing them to contract. The ventricles relax, completing one full heartbeat. Immediately the SA node generates a new electrical impulse, repeating the pattern.

title: ANH11047 Atrial Fibrillation
summary_zh: 房颤的电生理机制：异常电信号起源于心房和邻近肺静脉的异常位置，信号在心房内杂乱传播导致心房快速颤动，信号轰击AV结使心室快速收缩，但心室无法跟上心房的快速收缩节奏，导致心跳不协调。
text_en: In atrial fibrillation, the electrical signals originate from unusual locations in the atria and nearby pulmonary veins. These abnormal signals travel through the atria haphazardly, causing the atria to contract in a jumble of rapid quivers and twitches. The signals bombard the AV node, causing the ventricles to contract rapidly. Even at a faster pace, the ventricular contractions cannot keep up with the rapid atrial contractions, resulting in an uncordenated heartbeat.

title: ANH11047 Atrial Fibrillation
summary_zh: 房颤的并发症：不规则收缩后心腔无法完全排空，血液在心房内淤积可形成血栓；血栓可随血流进入脑部导致中风；心室血液淤积使组织缺氧缺营养；长期泵血不足可发展为心力衰竭。
text_en: After these irregular contractions, the chambers may not empty completely, when blood collects in the atria, clots may form. A clot can flow out of your heart, through your neck, into your brain, where it can cause If your blood, pooling in your ventricles, deprives your body's tissues of oxygen and nutrients. If your heart is consistently unable to pump enough blood to meet the body's needs, you can develop heart failure.

title: ANH11047 Atrial Fibrillation
summary_zh: 房颤的药物治疗：医生可使用抗心律失常药物控制心房电信号恢复正常节律；若心率仍过快，可使用β受体阻滞剂减慢心率；此外医生还可能推荐电复律、消融术或起搏器植入等手术。
text_en: Your doctor may treat your atrial fibrillation, with anti-arhythmic medications. Your atrial fibrillation control the electrical signals sent to your atria, which help your heart beat in a regular rhythm. After treatment, your heart may still beat too quickly. In this case, your doctor may give you beta-blocker medications, to slow your heart rate down to In addition, your doctor may recommend procedures that restore your heart's normal rhythm and rate, such as cardioversion, ablation, or pacemaker placement.

title: ANH11047 Atrial Fibrillation
summary_zh: 房颤的手术治疗详解：电复律通过电击恢复正常心律；消融术使用射频能量摧毁产生异常电信号的心脏组织；起搏器植入是在心脏附近皮下植入小型装置以调节心律。
text_en: In electrical cardioversion, your doctor will give your heart a shock, to start a normal heart rhythm. During ablation, your surgeon will use radio wave energy to destroy the heart tissue generating the abnormal electrical signals. In pacemaker placement, your surgeon will implant a small device under the skin near your heart to regulate your heart's rhythm.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
summary_zh: 叶酸与食物中天然叶酸的区别：叶酸（folic acid）是人工合成形式，添加于维生素和强化食品中；folate是食物中天然存在的B族维生素形式
text_en: Folic acid and folate are two forms of the same B vitamin. Folate is the form that occurs naturally in foods, while folic acid is the man-made form added to multivitamins and fortified foods.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
summary_zh: 叶酸的生理功能与孕期重要性：身体需要叶酸来生成新细胞（脑细胞、血细胞、DNA/RNA）；孕期需求增加，叶酸帮助胎儿大脑和脊髓正常发育，显著降低神经管缺陷风险
text_en: Your body needs folic acid to help make new cells, including brain cells, blood cells, and DNA and RNA, the genetic material inside your cells. During pregnancy, you need even more folic acid because your baby's body needs it too. Folic acid helps your baby's brain and spinal cord develop normally. Taking folic acid can significantly reduce the risk of neural tube defects in your developing baby, called an embryo at this stage.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
summary_zh: 神经管的正常发育过程：神经管从椭圆形胚胎组织开始，在受孕约28天后折叠成管状；正常闭合后发育为大脑和脊髓；若因缺乏叶酸未能正常闭合，则可能导致神经管缺陷
text_en: The neural tube begins as a tiny oval-shaped piece of embryonic tissue, which folds into a tube about 28 days after conception. When this tube closes properly, it continues to develop into your baby's brain and spinal If the neural tube does not close due to the lack of folic acid in your diet, your baby

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
summary_zh: 脊柱裂（spina bifida）：神经管底部未能闭合，导致脊柱无法完全包裹保护下段脊髓，脊髓突出；患儿常需手术，可出现腿部瘫痪、发育迟缓和神经系统问题等永久性残疾
text_en: may have a neural tube defect, such as spina bifida or anencephaly. In spina bifida, the neural tube fails to close at the bottom. As a result, the parts of the spine that enclose and protect the lower spinal cord do not completely form, allowing the cord to protrude. Children with this defect often require surgery and have permanent disabilities, including leg paralysis, developmental delays, and nervous system problems.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
summary_zh: 无脑畸形（anencephaly）：神经管顶部未能闭合，导致大脑和颅骨大部分无法完整发育；患此缺陷的胎儿无法存活，通常在出生前或出生后不久死亡
text_en: In the condition called anencephaly, the neural tube fails to close at the top, preventing large parts of the brain and skull from developing completely. These with this defect are unable to live and usually die before birth or soon after.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
summary_zh: 叶酸可能预防的其他出生缺陷：唇腭裂（cleft lip and palate）以及房间隔缺损（atrial septal defect，即心脏左右上腔室之间的孔洞在发育中未能闭合）
text_en: Folic acid may also protect against other birth defects, such as cleft lip and palate, and atrial septal defect, which is when a hole between the right and left upper chambers of the heart, fails to close during development.

title: ANH11056 Folic Acid
summary_zh: 叶酸补充建议：由于神经管在孕早期即发育（常在女性知晓怀孕之前），专家建议所有育龄女性每日服用含400微克叶酸的多种维生素；孕期增至每日600微克；需额外补充因为天然食物叶酸吸收率低于人工叶酸
text_en: As the neural tube develops so early in pregnancy, often before a woman knows she's pregnant, experts recommend all women who are able to become pregnant take a daily multivitamin containing 400 micrograms of folic acid every day. During pregnancy, the need increases to 600 micrograms of folic acid each day. Experts recommend you take this multivitamin in addition to the food folate in your diet, as your body does not absorb folate found naturally in food, as easily as the man made folic acid.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
summary_zh: 主动脉概述：主动脉是人体最大的动脉，心脏通过主动脉将富含氧气的血液输送到全身器官和组织
text_en: The aorta is the largest artery in your body. Your heart pumps fresh, oxygen-rich blood through your aorta to deliver oxygen and nutrients to all your organs and tissues.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
summary_zh: 主动脉的解剖结构：分为胸主动脉和腹主动脉两部分；胸主动脉包括主动脉根部、升主动脉、主动脉弓和降主动脉；主动脉壁由内膜、中膜和外膜三层组成
text_en: Your aorta is divided into two main sections, the thoracic aorta and the abdominal aorta. The thoracic aorta has four parts, the aortic root, ascending aorta, aortic arch, and descending aorta. The wall of your aorta is composed of three layers that give it strength, the intima or inner layer, the media or middle layer, and the adventisha or outer layer.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
summary_zh: 动脉瘤的形成机制：弹性中膜层防止血压撑破管壁，但退行性病变可导致中膜层退化、管壁减弱，血流冲击弱化区域使其像气球一样向外膨出
text_en: The elastic muscular media prevents your blood pressure from rupturing the wall. Over time, degenerative disease may cause the media layer to break down, weakening the wall of your aorta. Blood pumping against the weakened area may cause it to bulge outward like a balloon.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
summary_zh: 胸主动脉瘤的定义与好发部位：胸腔内的主动脉膨出称为胸主动脉瘤，可发生在主动脉根部、升主动脉或降主动脉
text_en: When this condition occurs in your chest, it is called a thoracic aortic aneurysm. These aneurysms may occur in one of several places, including the aortic root, the ascending aorta, or the descending aorta.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
summary_zh: 胸主动脉瘤的症状：多数无症状；增大后可出现气短、胸背腹痛、吞咽困难、声嘶；若破裂或夹层则出现胸部撕裂感、肩胛间剧痛、头晕、行走和言语困难等危及生命的症状
text_en: Most thoracic aneurysms have no symptoms, however, when they reach larger sizes, various symptoms may appear, depending on the severity and location of the aneurysm. Symptoms of stable thoracic aneurysms may include shortness of breath, chest, back, or abdominal pain, difficulty swallowing, and horse-ness. If the aneurysm ruptures or dissects, more dramatic symptoms will appear, including a ripping sensation in the chest, severe pain in the back between the shoulder blades, dizziness, and difficulty walking and speaking. If you have these symptoms, you should seek immediate medical attention, as this condition is life-threatening.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
summary_zh: 主动脉夹层的形成过程：血流冲击弱化管壁导致内膜撕裂，血液进入内膜与中膜之间形成假腔，隔膜（septum）将真腔与假腔分开；血液可能穿破外膜导致致命出血，或通过另一内膜裂口重新进入真腔；假腔内可形成血栓
text_en: Blood pumping against weakened aortic walls can lead to another life-threatening condition, called aortic dissection, which may or may not occur within an aneurysm. Most commonly, aortic dissection begins with damage to the intima layer. Blood moves through the break in the intima, separating it from the media, causing bleeding inside the wall of your aorta. As a result, a flap called a septum forms between the true aortic channel and the false channel. From here, the blood may break through the outer layer of the aortic wall, causing an immediate life-threatening condition, or it may re-enter the aorta through another tear in the intima. Over time, a blood clot may form in the false channel.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
summary_zh: 主动脉夹层的Stanford分型：根据位置和症状持续时间分类——累及升主动脉为A型夹层，其余为B型夹层
text_en: An aortic dissection may be classified, according to its location and duration of symptoms. In the Stanford system, if you have an aortic dissection in your ascending aorta, it is called a type A dissection. All other dissections are called type B.

title: ANH12061 Aortic Aneurysm and Dissection
summary_zh: 主动脉夹层的症状与就医建议：最常见症状为突发剧烈刺痛或刀割样胸痛；A型多感胸痛，B型多感背痛；出现症状应立即就医，此病可危及生命
text_en: The most common symptom of the aortic dissection is sharp or stabbing chest pain, usually sudden and severe. If you have a type A dissection, you're most likely to feel pain in your chest. If you have a type B dissection, you're more likely to feel pain in your back than your chest. If you have these symptoms, you should seek immediate medical attention, as this condition can be life-threatening.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
summary_zh: 医疗相关感染（HAI）的定义与常见致病菌：包括耐甲氧西林金黄色葡萄球菌（MRSA）、肠球菌和艰难梭菌（C. diff）
text_en: Health Care Associated Infections or HAIs are infections you may get in a health care facility while you are receiving treatment for a different condition. Bacteria such as methamillicillin-resistant staphlococcus aureus, also known as merse, interococcus species, and claustridium difficile or C-diff are frequently the cause of health care associated infections.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
summary_zh: HAI的严重性及主要传播途径：手卫生不当导致细菌在患者之间或同一患者不同部位之间传播
text_en: These infections are a major cause of death around the world. One of the most common ways these bacteria get passed from person to person is through lack of proper hand hygiene. When people don't clean their hands, they can transfer microbes from one patient to another, or from one part of the body to another on the same patient.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
summary_zh: 四种常见HAI之一——手术切口感染：细菌侵入手术切口并在组织中扩散导致感染
text_en: There are four common types of health care associated infections. A surgical wound infection can result when bacteria get into your incision and spread through your tissues, leading to infection.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
summary_zh: 四种常见HAI之二——导尿管相关尿路感染：细菌通过导尿管进入泌尿系统并在膀胱中繁殖
text_en: A urinary tract infection can occur when you have a catheter in your bladder, which is a tube used to drain your urine. Bacteria can enter your urinary system on the catheter, enabling the bacteria to travel into your bladder where they can grow.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
summary_zh: 四种常见HAI之三——中心静脉导管相关血流感染：细菌从穿刺部位皮肤或直接进入血液导致感染
text_en: A bloodstream infection can occur when you have a central line in your vein, which is a slender tube used to deliver fluids or medications into a large vein near your heart. Bacteria can enter the skin around the insertion site, or directly into your bloodstream, where they can cause infection.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
summary_zh: 四种常见HAI之四——呼吸机相关肺炎：细菌在置入呼吸机时进入咽喉，被吸入肺部引发肺炎
text_en: Numonia can result when you are on a ventilator or breathing machine. Bacteria can enter your throat while you are being placed on the ventilator. Then you may inhale the bacteria into your lungs, leading to infection that can cause pneumonia.

title: ANH12066 Healthcare-Associated Infections
summary_zh: 手卫生是预防HAI最重要的措施：用肥皂流水洗手40-60秒，或用含酒精手消毒剂揉搓20-30秒
text_en: Hand hygiene, a simple, low-cost and effective procedure, is the single most important method of preventing health care associated infections. All people in health care facilities, including patients, visitors and medical personnel, can help prevent these infections by cleaning their hands frequently. Hand hygiene can be performed with soap and running water for 40 to 60 seconds, or by thoroughly rubbing on an alcohol-based hand sanitizer for 20 to 30 seconds.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
summary_zh: CT扫描（也称CAT扫描或计算机轴向断层扫描）的定义：一种无痛的诊断检查，利用X射线和计算机生成骨骼及组织的横截面图像。
text_en: A CT scan, also known as a CAT scan or computerized axial tomography, is a painless diagnostic test that uses X-rays and computers to create cross-sectional images of bones and tissues inside

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
summary_zh: CT扫描的临床适应症：医生可能建议进行CT扫描以检查血栓、骨折、癌性肿瘤、感染、内伤出血以及心血管疾病的征兆。
text_en: Your doctor may recommend a CT scan to examine your body for any of the following. Ant clots, broken bones, cancerous tumors, infections, internal injuries and bleeding, and signs of heart and vascular disease.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
summary_zh: CT扫描的临床用途：帮助医生确定手术、活检或放射治疗的正确位置，评估癌症或心脏病的治疗效果，以及术后复查。
text_en: A CT scan helps your doctor select the correct location for surgery, biopsy, or radiation therapy, check the treatment of cancer or heart disease, and check your condition after

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
summary_zh: CT扫描的工作原理：患者躺在可移动的检查台上通过隧道，环形机架（Gantry）围绕身体旋转，内置X射线管发射射线束，探测器测量人体吸收的辐射量，从多角度采集数据后由计算机生成骨骼和软组织的横截面图像。
text_en: A CT scanner is a large square or round X-ray machine with a tunnel through the center. During your CT scan, you will lie on a table that slowly passes through the tunnel. As you move through the tunnel, a giant ring called a gantry will rotate around your body. The gantry contains a tube that will release X-ray beams and detectors that will measure the amount of radiation absorbed by your body. The X-ray beams will capture many views of your body from different angles as the gantry The detectors will send data to a computer that will create cross-sectional images of the bones and soft tissues inside your body.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
summary_zh: CT扫描的诊断价值：帮助医生观察病变在体内的位置，辅助制定治疗方案或评估治疗进展。
text_en: The scan allows your doctor to see the location of a condition inside your body, which will help them decide how to treat it or to see how well your treatment is progressing.

title: ANH12068 CT Scan
summary_zh: 造影剂的使用：检查前可能通过口服饮品、注射或钡灌肠给予造影剂。口服造影剂可高亮食管和胃，注射造影剂可高亮血管、胆囊、肝脏或泌尿道，钡灌肠可高亮大肠。
text_en: In some cases, you may receive contrast dye before your procedure in a drink, an injection, or a barium anema to make it easier for your doctor to see certain areas of your body. If you received a drink with contrast dye, your esophagus or stomach will be highlighted. If you received an injection, your blood vessels, gallbladder, liver, or urinary tract will be highlighted. If you received a barium anema, your large intestine will be highlighted.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 压力性损伤的定义与好发部位：压力性损伤是由皮肤持续受压引起的伤口，也称为压疮、褥疮等，常发生在骨骼靠近皮肤的部位，如脚跟、踝部、髋部、尾骨和肘部。
text_en: A pressure injury or ulcer is a wound caused by constant pressure on your skin. It may also be called a pressure sore, pressure ulcer, bed sore, or de-cubitus ulcer. Pressure injuries occur most frequently in areas where your bones are close to the skin, including your heels, ankles, hips, tailbone, or elbows.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 皮肤的三层结构：表皮层（保护身体）、真皮层（供给营养、调节体温、提供弹性）和皮下组织层（储存脂肪、缓冲肌肉和骨骼）。
text_en: In order to understand how pressure injuries happen, it's important to know about the layers of the skin. Your skin is made up of three main layers. The top layer called the epidermis is a thin layer that protects your body from the environment. The thicker middle layer is called the dermis. Its main functions are to supply the epidermis with nutrients, help regulate your body temperature, and provide strength and elasticity to your skin. The innermost layer is called the hypodermis, or subcutaneous layer. This layer stores fat, which cushions the muscles and bones beneath it.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 压力性损伤的形成机制与分期概念：持续压力压迫血管，导致皮肤组织缺血缺氧坏死，形成压力性损伤；医疗人员根据组织损伤深度进行分期。
text_en: In a pressure injury, constant pressure on the skin over a long period of time can compress the blood vessels that supply the layers with the oxygen and nutrients they need to survive. If there's not enough blood flow, your skin tissue begins to die and a pressure injury If you have a pressure injury, your healthcare provider will classify it based on what can be known about the depth of tissue damage.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 1期和2期压力性损伤：1期表现为皮肤完整但发红、不可按压变白（非苍白性红斑）；2期表现为水疱或开放性溃疡，表皮和真皮部分缺失，周围皮肤发红。
text_en: This is called staging. In a stage 1 pressure injury, the skin is intact, red, and inflamed. The redness is non-blanchable. This means it doesn't become pale if you press on it. A stage 2 pressure injury appears as a blister or an open sore. There is partial thickness loss of the epidermis and dermis. The areas surrounding the sore may be red and irritated.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 3期和4期压力性损伤：3期为火山口样全层皮肤缺失，深达皮下脂肪层；4期最严重，全层组织缺失，深达肌肉或骨骼。
text_en: A stage 3 pressure injury is a crater-like, full thickness loss of skin. It extends down to the fat tissue of the hypodermis. Stage 4 is the most severe. A stage 4 pressure injury is a full thickness loss of tissue. It extends through all three layers of skin and into the underlying muscle or bone.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 不可分期压力性损伤与疑似深部组织损伤：不可分期损伤因被坏死组织覆盖而无法判断深度；疑似深部组织损伤表现为紫红色皮肤区域或含血性水疱，提示皮下软组织损伤。
text_en: An unstageable pressure injury is a full thickness loss of tissue. It's called unstageable because the injury is covered by dead tissue, making it hard to tell how deep it is. And a suspected deep tissue injury appears as a maroon or purple area of the skin. It may contain a blister filled with blood. It looks like this because of damaged soft tissue underneath your skin.

title: ANH12069 Pressure Injuries
summary_zh: 压力性损伤的治疗方法：用清水、温和肥皂或生理盐水清洁伤口预防感染；严重时进行清创术去除坏死组织；使用专用敷料保护伤口并保持湿润促进愈合。
text_en: To prevent infection in your pressure injury, your doctor will clean your wound using water and a mild soap or saline solution. If your wound is severe, your doctor will debris or surgically remove the dead tissue from the pressure injury. To protect your wound and keep it from drying out while it heals, your doctor may cover it with a specialized bandage.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 香烟的成分：烟草叶含尼古丁及多种化合物，燃烧释放数千种有害化学物质（包括40余种致癌物）、一氧化碳、氮氧化物及放射性颗粒；所有烟草形式（雪茄、烟斗、无烟烟草）均有害
text_en: stream, where they spread to every organ in your body. A cigarette is made using the tobacco leaf, which contains nicotine and a variety of other compounds. As the tobacco and compounds burn, they release thousands of dangerous chemicals, including over 40 known to cause cancer. Cigarette smoke contains the poisonous gases carbon monoxide and nitrogen oxide, as well as trace amounts of cancer-causing radioactive particles. All forms of tobacco are dangerous, including cigars, pipes, and smokeless tobacco, such as chewing tobacco and snuff.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 尼古丁的成瘾机制：尼古丁经血流到达大脑，诱发愉悦感；反复暴露导致大脑脱敏，需要更多尼古丁才能维持正常感觉
text_en: Nicotine is an addictive chemical in tobacco. After you inhale tobacco smoke, nicotine flows through the bloodstream to your brain, where it induces a pleasurable feeling. When you repeatedly expose your brain to nicotine, it becomes desensitized, making you crave more and more nicotine just to feel normal.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟导致的死亡与主要健康风险概述：吸烟者寿命缩短，美国每五例死亡中有一例与吸烟相关；显著增加心脏病、心脏病发作、中风、肺癌和慢性阻塞性肺病死亡风险
text_en: Smoking causes death. People who smoke typically die at an earlier age than non-smokers. In fact, one of every five deaths in the United States is linked to cigarette smoking. If you smoke your risk of major health problems increases dramatically, including heart disease, heart attack, stroke, lung cancer, and death from chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟与心血管疾病（一）：尼古丁刺激肾上腺释放肾上腺素导致血压升高；尼古丁和一氧化碳损伤动脉内壁，脂肪沉积形成斑块，导致动脉粥样硬化，严重减少血流
text_en: Smoking causes cardiovascular disease. When nicotine flows through your adrenal glands, it stimulates the release of epinephrine, a hormone that raises your blood pressure. In addition, nicotine and carbon monoxide can damage the lining of the inner walls in your arteries. Fatty deposits, called plaque, can build up at these injury sites and become large enough to narrow the arteries and severely reduce blood flow, resulting in a condition called atherosclerosis.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟与心血管疾病（二）：冠状动脉疾病中动脉粥样硬化使心脏供氧减少，增加心脏病发作风险；吸烟促进血小板聚集形成血栓；引发外周血管疾病阻塞四肢大动脉；可导致腹主动脉瘤
text_en: In coronary artery disease, atherosclerosis narrows the arteries that supply the heart, which reduces the supply of oxygen to your heart muscle, increasing your risk for a heart attack. Smoking also raises your risk for blood clots, because it causes platelets in your blood to clump together. Smoking increases your risk for peripheral vascular disease, in which atherosclerotic plaques block the large arteries in your arms and legs. Smoking can also cause an abdominal aortic aneurysm, which is a swelling or weakening of your aorta where it runs through your abdomen.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 肺部结构与正常呼吸过程：肺部的两个主要结构——支气管和肺泡；空气经气管进入支气管再到肺泡，完成氧气和二氧化碳的气体交换；纤毛负责清除有害物质
text_en: Smoking damages two main parts of your lungs. Your airways also called bronchial tubes, and small air sacs called alveoli. With each breath, air travels down your windpipe, called the trachea, and enters your lungs through your bronchial tubes. Air then moves in two thousands of tiny alveoli, where oxygen from the air moves into your blood stream, and the waste product carbon dioxide moves out of your blood stream. Tiny hair-like projections, called cilia, line your bronchial tubes and sweep harmful substances out of your lungs.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟对气道的损害：烟雾刺激支气管内壁导致肿胀和黏液增多；减缓纤毛运动使烟雾和黏液滞留肺中；睡眠时纤毛部分恢复，晨起咳嗽排出污染物（吸烟者咳嗽）；长期发展为慢性支气管炎
text_en: Cigarette smoke irritates the lining of your bronchial tubes, causing them to swell and make Cigarette smoke also slows the movement of your cilia, causing some of the smoke and mucus to stay in your lungs. While you're sleeping, some of the cilia recover and start pushing more pollutants and mucus out of your lungs. When you wake up, your body attempts to expel this material by coughing repeatedly, a condition known as smokers cough. Over time, chronic bronchitis develops as your cilia stop working, your airways become clogged with scars and mucus, and breathing becomes difficult. Your lungs are now more vulnerable to further disease.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 肺泡损伤与慢性阻塞性肺病（COPD）：烟雾损伤肺泡，阻碍气体交换；长期导致肺气肿，患者呼吸困难需佩戴氧气管；慢性支气管炎和肺气肿统称为COPD，不可逆转
text_en: Cigarette smoke also damages your LVLI, making it harder for oxygen and carbon dioxide to exchange with your blood. Over time, so little oxygen can reach your blood that you may develop emphysema, a condition in which you must gasp for every breath and wear an oxygen tube under your nose in order to breathe. Chronic bronchitis and emphysema are collectively called chronic obstructive pulmonary disease COPD is a gradual loss of the ability to breathe for which there is no cure.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟致癌机制：香烟含至少40种致癌物（氰化物、甲醛、苯、氨）；有毒化学物质损伤健康细胞的DNA，导致受损细胞失控增殖并可能扩散；肺癌是全球最常见癌症
text_en: Cigarette smoke contains at least 40 cancer-causing substances called carcinogens, including cyanide, formaldehyde, benzene, and ammonia. In your body, healthy cells grow, make new cells, then die. Genetic material inside each cell, called DNA, directs this process. If you smoke toxic chemicals, can damage the DNA in your healthy cells. As a result, your damaged cells create new unhealthy cells, which grow out of control and may spread to other parts of your body. The most common cancer in the world is lung cancer, with over a million new cases diagnosed every year.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟可导致的其他部位癌症：血液和骨髓、口腔、喉部、咽喉、食道、胃、胰腺、肾脏、膀胱、子宫和宫颈的癌症
text_en: Harmful chemicals in cigarettes can cause cancer in other parts of your body, such as, in the blood and bone marrow, mouth, larynx, throat, esophagus, stomach, pancreas, kidney, bladder, uterus, and cervix.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟对生育和妊娠的影响：导致男女不育；孕期吸烟增加低出生体重、流产、早产、死产、婴儿死亡及婴儿猝死综合征风险；哺乳期尼古丁通过母乳传递给婴儿，引起烦躁、心跳加速、呕吐等症状
text_en: Cigarette can cause infertility in both men and women. If a woman is pregnant and smokes during pregnancy, she exposes her baby to the cigarette's poisonous chemicals, causing a greater risk of low birth weight, miscarriage, pre-term delivery, still birth, infant death, and sudden infant death syndrome. Hitting is also dangerous if a mother is breastfeeding. Nicotine passes to the baby through breast milk, and can cause restlessness, rapid heartbeat, vomiting, interrupted sleep, or diarrhea.

title: ANH12071 Health Effects of Smoking
summary_zh: 吸烟的其他健康影响：女性骨密度降低和髋部骨折风险增加、牙龈疾病导致牙齿脱落、免疫系统功能障碍、伤口愈合延迟、男性性功能障碍
text_en: Other health effects of smoking include low bone density and increased risk for hip fracture among women, gum disease often leading to tooth loss and surgery, immune system dysfunction, delayed wound healing, and sexual impotence in men.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 心脏基本结构与正常血液循环：介绍心脏四个腔室（右心房、右心室、左心房、左心室），以及缺氧血从体循环回流经右心至肺部获取氧气，再经左心泵出至全身的正常循环过程。
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients your body needs. The pumping sections of the heart are the right atrium, right ventricle, left atrium, and left ventricle. In your heart, oxygen poor blood flows from your body through large veins into your right atrium. Next, your blood moves into your right ventricle, which contracts, sending blood out of the heart to pick up oxygen from your lungs. Oxygen rich blood moves out of your lungs into your left atrium, then moves into your left ventricle. Finally, your left ventricle contracts, sending oxygen rich blood out of your heart to your

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的定义与基本病理：心脏丧失足够泵血能力，可能因心室壁薄弱无力（收缩功能障碍）或心室壁僵硬增厚导致充盈不足（舒张功能障碍）。
text_en: If you have heart failure, your heart has lost the ability to pump enough blood to meet your body's needs. You may have weak or damaged ventricular walls that are not able to push enough blood out of your heart. You may also have stiff and thickened ventricular walls that do not allow your heart to fill with enough blood.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 左心衰竭的病理机制：左心室泵血不足导致疲劳和呼吸困难；左心衰竭使肺静脉压力升高，液体渗入肺组织，造成肺水肿和呼吸困难。
text_en: If you have left-sided heart failure, your left ventricle does not deliver enough oxygen-rich blood to your body, making you feel tired and out of breath. Your failing left ventricle also increases the blood pressure in the blood vessels between your lungs and left ventricle. This increased pressure forces fluid out of your blood and into your lung tissues, which makes it difficult for you to breathe.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 右心衰竭的病理机制与心衰进展：右心室收缩力不足无法将血液泵入肺部，导致静脉血液淤积和全身组织水肿；长期心衰导致心室扩大、泵血量进一步减少的恶性循环。
text_en: If you have right-sided heart failure, your right ventricle is unable to contract with enough force to push blood to your lungs. The result is a build-up of blood in your veins, which causes a build-up of fluid called edema throughout the tissues in your body. Over time, heart failure on either side of your heart results in weakened and large ventricles that deliver less blood to your body.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 神经内分泌代偿机制及其危害：神经系统释放应激激素加快心率和增强收缩力以代偿泵血不足，但长期应激激素反而损伤心肌细胞，加重心衰。
text_en: To make up for the decreased amount of blood, your nervous system releases stress hormones that increase the speed and force of your heart beat. Unfortunately, the continued release of these stress hormones makes your heart failure worse because they damage the muscle cells in your ventricles.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的病因：最常见病因为冠状动脉疾病；其他病因包括高血压、糖尿病、心脏瓣膜病变、心肌病变、心律失常、先天性心脏缺陷、中毒或药物滥用、肺部疾病及睡眠呼吸暂停。
text_en: The most common cause of heart failure is coronary artery disease. Other causes that damage your heart and lead to heart failure include high blood pressure, diabetes, diseased infected or damaged heart valves, diseased infected or damaged heart muscle, irregular heartbeats called arrhythmias, heart defects, poisons or substance abuse, lung diseases, and breathing problems during sleep called sleep apnea.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 心衰的药物治疗：利尿剂通过增加排尿减轻水肿；ACE抑制剂扩张血管、降低血压、减轻心脏泵血负担；β受体阻滞剂阻断应激激素对心脏的损害作用，保护心肌。
text_en: Treatment for heart failure includes medications, lifestyle changes, or in extreme heart failure, heart beat assisting devices or surgery. Medications reduce the swelling in your body by increasing the amount of urine produced by your kidneys. Ace inhibitors are medications that allow your blood vessels to expand. This helps decrease your blood pressure and prevents further damage to your heart by making it easier for your heart to pump blood. Beta blockers are medications that block the effects of stress hormones on your heart. Although beta blockers slow down your heart beat, they are mainly used to protect your heart muscle from the long-term damage caused by stress hormones.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 心衰的生活方式调整：规律锻炼、保持健康体重、戒烟、限制盐和酒精摄入、采用有益心脏健康的饮食。
text_en: You may need to make some lifestyle changes, including exercising on a regular basis, maintaining a healthy weight, quitting smoking, limiting salt and alcohol, and eating a heart-healthy diet.

title: ANH12076 Heart Failure Overview - Detailed
summary_zh: 心衰的手术治疗：冠状动脉搭桥术改善心肌血供、心脏瓣膜修复术、左心室重建术切除受损心肌；极重度心衰可植入心室辅助装置或进行心脏移植。
text_en: Surgery for advanced heart failure include coronary artery bypass surgery to improve blood flow to your heart muscle, heart valve reconstruction surgery, to improve blood flow through your heart, and left ventricle reconstruction surgery to remove damaged heart muscle. Surgeries for extreme heart failure include insertion of a device to help your heart pump blood, and heart transplant.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
summary_zh: 心脏四腔结构：上方为左右心房，下方为左右心室，按稳定节律收缩形成心跳。正常心跳时血液从组织和肺流入心房再进入心室。房间隔和室间隔防止左右两侧血液混合。
text_en: Your heart has four chambers. Two upper chambers, called the left and right atria, and two lower chambers, called the left and right ventricles, contract in a steady rhythm known as your heart beat. During a normal heart beat, blood from your tissues and lungs flows into your atria, then into your ventricles. Inside your heart, called the inter atrial and interventricular septa, help keep the blood on left and right sides from mixing.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
summary_zh: 房室瓣膜：三尖瓣和二尖瓣如同门一样位于心房与心室之间，防止血液回流。腱索（corda tendonae）在心室强力收缩时固定瓣膜位置。
text_en: Two valves sit like doors between your atria and ventricles to prevent blood from flowing backward into your atria. The tricuspid valve opens into your right ventricle, and the mitral valve opens into your left ventricle. Strong, thin tissues, called corda tendonae, hold your valves in place during the forceful contractions of your ventricles.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
summary_zh: 半月瓣膜：血液离开心室时经过肺动脉瓣（右心室与肺动脉干之间）和主动脉瓣（左心室与主动脉之间）。
text_en: Blood leaving the ventricles passes through another set of valves, the pulmonary valve between your right ventricle and pulmonary trunk, and the aortic valve connecting your left ventricle and aorta.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
summary_zh: 心肌结构与电传导：心肌（myocardium）以独特方式排列，三层心肌包裹心脏下部，沿不同方向扭转收紧以推动血液。起搏细胞产生电信号，使心肌细胞（myocytes）协同收缩。
text_en: In order to pump blood more efficiently, your heart muscle, called myocardium, is arranged in a unique pattern. Three layers of myocardium wrap around the lower part of your heart. They twist and tighten in different directions to push blood through your heart. When special cells, called pacemaker cells, generate electrical signals inside your heart. The heart muscle cells, called myocytes, contract as a group.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
summary_zh: 左右心双泵循环：右心接收经上下腔静脉回流的缺氧血，由右心室泵入肺进行气体交换；左心接收经肺静脉回流的富氧血，由左心室经主动脉泵出供应全身组织。
text_en: Your heart is divided into left and right halves, which work together like a dual pump. On the right side of your heart, deoxygenated blood from your body's tissues flows through large veins, called the superior and inferior vena cava, into your right atrium. Next, the blood moves into your right ventricle, which contracts and sends blood out of your heart to your lungs to gather oxygen and get rid of carbon dioxide. On the left side of your heart, oxygen-rich blood from your lungs flows through your pulmonary veins into your left atrium. The blood then moves into your left ventricle, which contracts and sends blood out of your heart through the aorta to feed your cells and tissues.

title: ANH12082 Heart Anatomy
summary_zh: 主动脉分支与供血：主动脉首先分出冠状动脉为心肌供氧和营养，顶部分支供应头部和上肢，中下部分支供应身体其余部位。
text_en: The first branches off your aorta are the coronary arteries, which supply your heart muscle with oxygen and nutrients. At the top of your aorta, arteries branch off to carry blood to your head and arms. Arteries branching from the middle and lower parts of your aorta, supply blood to the rest of your body.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
summary_zh: 心脏基本结构介绍：心脏是泵血的肌肉器官，由右心房、右心室、左心房和左心室四个腔室组成
text_en: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients that the body needs. The pumping sections of the heart are the right atrium, right ventricle, left atrium, and left ventricle.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
summary_zh: 正常心脏血液循环过程：缺氧血经上下腔静脉进入右心房→右心室→经肺动脉到肺部获取氧气→经肺静脉回到左心房→左心室→经主动脉泵向全身
text_en: In a normal heart, oxygen poor blood flows from the body through large veins, called the superior vena cava and inferior vena cava, into the right atrium. Next, blood moves into the right ventricle, which contracts. Sending blood to the lungs through the pulmonary artery. Blood picks up oxygen in the lungs and returns through the pulmonary veins to the left atrium, then moves into the left ventricle. Finally, the left ventricle contracts. Sending oxygen-rich blood to the body through the aorta.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
summary_zh: 胎儿循环特点：胎儿不通过肺呼吸，氧气经胎盘和脐带从母体获得；血液通过动脉导管和卵圆孔两条通路绕过肺部循环
text_en: For birth, the heart doesn't send blood to the lungs to get oxygen, since the baby is not breathing yet. Instead, all the oxygen the baby needs passes from the mother's blood to the baby through the placenta and a miracle cord. In addition, blood from the baby's heart bypasses the lungs in two ways. One is through a connection between the pulmonary artery and the aorta, called the ductus arteriosus. The other is through an opening in the wall between the two atria, called the foreamen ovale. These extra openings allow oxygen-rich blood to bypass the baby's lungs and still circulate through the body.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
summary_zh: 出生后正常变化：动脉导管和卵圆孔在出生后一两天内关闭，迫使血液必须经过肺部获取氧气
text_en: Normally, within a day or two after birth, both the ductus arteriosus and the foreamen ovale Forcing the baby's blood to travel to the lungs to pick up oxygen.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
summary_zh: 左心发育不全综合征的病理机制：左心室和主动脉发育过小，导致全身供血不足；若卵圆孔和动脉导管正常关闭，供血进一步减少，危及生命；症状包括皮肤灰蓝、呼吸急促，不手术则致命
text_en: In hypoplastic left heart syndrome, a newborn baby's heart has severe birth defects that prevent blood from flowing normally to the rest of the body. If the body can't get oxygen and nutrients, it can't survive. The left ventricle, which is the main pump and the aorta, which is the main pipeline, are smaller than normal, so the baby's body does not receive enough blood. If the foreamen ovale and ductus arteriosus are allowed to close as they would normally, blood flow to the body will decrease even more. Resulting in a life-threatening situation Symptoms of hypoplastic left heart syndrome include gray or bluish skin color and rapid breathing as the baby tries to get more oxygen to the body. Without surgical intervention, this condition is fatal.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
summary_zh: 第一阶段Norwood手术（出生后第一周）：分离肺动脉并连接主动脉，植入移植管连接主动脉分支与右肺动脉，移除房间隔，从而增加肺部含氧血回流、增加全身供血和肺部血流
text_en: Surgeons will perform three separate operations to treat hypoplastic left heart syndrome. The goal of these operations is to improve the flow of oxygen-rich blood to the baby's body. The Stage 1 operation, called the Norwood procedure, is performed during the first week after birth. During the operation, the surgeon will make three main changes. First, the pulmonary artery will be separated from the right and left pulmonary arteries and connected to the aorta. A small piece of tubing, called a graft, will be inserted to connect a branch of the aorta and the right pulmonary artery. Finally, the surgeon will remove the wall between the right and left atria. These changes increase the flow of oxygen-rich blood from the lungs through both atria into the right ventricle, increase the blood flow to the body through the new aorta, and increase the blood flow to the lungs through the graft.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
summary_zh: 第二阶段Glenn手术（约6个月后）：移除移植管，将上腔静脉从心脏断开并重新连接至右肺动脉，使头部和上肢的缺氧血直接进入肺部获取氧气
text_en: The Stage 2 operation, called the Glenn procedure, is performed about six months later when the baby is stronger. The surgeon will remove the graft connecting the branch of the aorta and the right pulmonary To replace the graft's function, the surgeon will disconnect the superior vena-cava from the heart and reconnect it to the right pulmonary artery. This connection allows oxygen-poor blood from the head and arms to go directly to the lungs to pick up oxygen.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
summary_zh: 第三阶段Fontan手术（18个月至3岁）：将下腔静脉从心脏断开，通过移植管连接至右肺动脉，使下半身缺氧血直接入肺；完成心脏修复的三大目标：建立新主动脉供血通路、建立缺氧血入肺新通路、防止含氧血与缺氧血混合
text_en: The Stage 3 operation, called the Fontan procedure, is performed when the baby is 18 months to three years old. The surgeon will disconnect the inferior vena-cava from the heart and reconnect it to the right pulmonary artery with graft tubing. This connection allows oxygen-poor blood from the lower body to go directly to the lungs to pick up oxygen. The Fontan procedure completes the surgical repair of the heart by achieving these goals. The discovery of oxygen-rich blood to the body through the creation of a new and expanded aorta, creation of a new path for oxygen-poor blood to reach the lungs, and prevention of the mixing of oxygen-rich blood and oxygen-poor blood.

title: ANH13087 Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome
summary_zh: 术后说明与结语：三次手术缓解了危及生命的状况但未恢复正常循环；部分患者可能需要心脏移植；术后需长期服用抗凝药物，移植患者还需服用抗排斥药物
text_en: These three operations relieve the life-threatening condition caused by hypoplastic left heart syndrome but do not restore normal circulation through the heart. In some cases, a heart transplant may be done if a donor heart is available or the changes from the three surgical procedures aren't working. After any of these operations, the child may receive blood thinner medication to prevent the formation of blood clots. Those with heart transplants will also need medication to prevent their body from rejecting the new heart. The child may need these medications for the rest of their life.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
summary_zh: 心脏通过颈部血管向大脑供血，这些动脉分支为更小的动脉，将血液输送到脑细胞
text_en: Your heart pumps blood to your brain through blood vessels in your neck. These blood vessels, called arteries, branch into even smaller arteries that bring blood to your brain cells.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
summary_zh: 部分小动脉相互连接形成Willis环；动脉壁含有特殊的肌肉层和弹性层，帮助适应血液脉动
text_en: Some of these small arteries join each other to form a loop known as the circle of willis. The walls of the arteries contain special muscle and elastic layers that help them adapt to the blood pulsing through them.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
summary_zh: 疾病、损伤或先天缺陷可导致Willis环动脉壁出现薄弱点，通常在动脉分叉处形成；血压持续作用使薄弱点膨出，形成脑动脉瘤
text_en: Sometimes diseases, injuries, or birth defects can cause a weak spot in the wall of the arteries in the circle of willis. These weak spots usually form where arteries branch. Over time, the pressure of blood flowing through the artery stresses the weak spot further. The constant blood pressure can cause the weak spot to balloon out and form a bulge called a cerebral aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
summary_zh: 最常见的囊状（浆果状）脑动脉瘤可逐渐增大压迫周围脑组织或神经，发生渗漏甚至破裂，高压血液溢入蛛网膜下腔，压迫脑组织、刺激周围动脉收缩，导致脑水肿和严重脑损伤
text_en: This cerebral aneurysm is the most common type called a sacular or berry aneurysm. Over time, the aneurysm may grow and push on nearby brain tissue or nerves. Blood may also leak from the aneurysm. If its wall gets too thin, the aneurysm can burst or rupture and spill high pressure blood into the space around your brain. The blood presses on your brain tissue and can cause injury to your brain cells. Chemicals in the escaped blood can irritate other brain arteries and cause them to narrow. As a result, your brain can swell, leading to serious brain injury.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
summary_zh: 破裂脑动脉瘤的常见症状：突发剧烈头痛（雷击样头痛）、视力模糊或复视、恶心呕吐、颈部僵硬疼痛、意识混乱
text_en: Common symptoms of a ruptured cerebral aneurysm include sudden severe headache, sometimes called a thunder clap headache, problems with your vision such as blurred or double vision, nausea and vomiting, a stiff or painful neck, or confusion.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
summary_zh: 未破裂脑动脉瘤可能无症状，但较大者可引起头痛、眼部周围疼痛、复视或视力模糊、眼睑下垂、面部一侧麻木或无力
text_en: An unruptured cerebral aneurysm may not cause symptoms, but large unruptured aneurysms may cause headache and pain near or behind one eye, double or blurred vision, a drooping eyelid and numbness or weakness on one side of your face.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
summary_zh: 手术夹闭术：外科医生在颅骨上开孔，在动脉瘤颈部放置金属夹，阻断血流进入动脉瘤
text_en: If you have a ruptured cerebral aneurysm, your doctor may recommend surgical clipping or endovascular coiling. In surgical clipping, your surgeon will make an opening in your skull, near your aneurysm, and place a small metal clip onto the stem that connects the aneurysm to your artery. The clip blocks blood flow into your aneurysm.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
summary_zh: 血管内弹簧圈栓塞术：经腹股沟大动脉插入导管，在X线引导下推进至动脉瘤处，通过导管送入柔性金属丝盘绕形成血栓，封堵动脉瘤
text_en: In endovascular coiling, your doctor will put a plastic tube called a catheter into a large artery in your groin. Using a live X-ray view, your doctor will advance the catheter through your arteries until it reaches the aneurysm. Through the catheter, your doctor will insert one or more flexible wires into your aneurysm. The wires will coil up and cause a blood clot to form inside your aneurysm, which will block off the aneurysm from the rest of your artery.

title: ANH13099 Cerebral Aneurysm
summary_zh: 未破裂脑动脉瘤也可采用夹闭或栓塞治疗；若动脉瘤较小且无症状，医生可能先观察一段时间再决定手术
text_en: In a similar manner, if you have an unruptured cerebral aneurysm, your doctor may recommend surgical clipping or endovascular coiling. In addition, if your aneurysm is small and not causing pain or other symptoms, your doctor may observe your aneurysm for a period of time before performing either of these procedures.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 高血压的定义与基本心脏泵血机制：血压是血液对动脉壁的压力，心室每次搏动将血液泵向肺部和全身动脉
text_en: High blood pressure or hypertension is a common condition in which the force of blood on the walls of your arteries is often too high. Arteries are the blood vessels that carry blood away from your heart to supply your tissues with oxygen and nutrients. In your heart, two chambers, called ventricles, contract with each heartbeat to push blood to your lungs and through your arteries to your body.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 影响血压的前两个因素：心输出量（每分钟心室泵出的血量）和血容量（体内血液总量），两者增加均导致血压升高
text_en: As blood flows through them, three main factors affect the pressure on your artery walls. The first is cardiac output or the amount of blood your ventricles push out of your heart each minute. Your blood pressure goes up as cardiac output increases. The second factor affecting your blood pressure is blood volume or the total amount of blood in your body. Blood pressure also goes up as blood volume increases.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 影响血压的第三个因素——血管阻力：包括动脉壁弹性（健康动脉可扩张缓冲压力）、动脉直径（扩张降压/收缩升压）、血液黏稠度（蛋白质和脂肪增多使血液变稠，心脏需更用力泵血）
text_en: The third factor that affects your blood pressure is resistance, which is anything working against the blood flow through your arteries. Several factors contribute to resistance. One resistance factor is the flexibility of your artery wall. Healthy arteries expand with each heartbeat to help reduce blood pressure on the wall. Another resistance factor is the diameter of your arteries. Your body is able to increase the diameter of your arteries to lower your blood pressure or reduce the diameter to raise your blood pressure. A third resistance factor is blood viscosity or thickness. In your blood, more particles, such as proteins and fat, increase viscosity. If your blood is thicker, your blood pressure goes up as your heart works harder to push it through your arteries.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 血压测量与诊断标准：使用血压计测量收缩压和舒张压，正常值应低于120/80 mmHg，收缩压持续高于130或舒张压持续高于80即为高血压
text_en: Your blood pressure can be measured with a device called a Sphigmomanometer or blood pressure When your heart beats, the pressure of blood on the walls of your arteries is called systolic When your heart relaxes between beats, pressure on the artery wall is called diastolic pressure. While your blood pressure may change throughout the day, it should optimally be less than 120 millimeters of mercury for systolic pressure and less than 80 millimeters of mercury for diastolic pressure. If your systolic pressure frequently stays above 130 or your diastolic pressure frequently stays above 80, you have high blood pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 高血压对动脉的损害：动脉壁变弱形成动脉瘤或破裂出血；内壁撕裂吸引胆固醇、脂肪和钙沉积形成斑块；血细胞附着形成血栓阻塞血流；最终导致中风、心脏病发作或肾病
text_en: Your time, high blood pressure will damage the walls of your arteries. Your artery wall may become weak and form an enlargement called an aneurysm or the wall may burst and bleed into the surrounding tissue. Small tears in your artery wall may attract certain substances in your blood, such as cholesterol, fat and calcium to form a buildup called a plaque. Blood flow through your artery decreases as the plaque enlarges. Blood cells can stick to the plaque and form solid clumps called clots, further reducing or completely blocking your blood flow. Damage to your arteries raises your blood pressure even more by making your heart beat more forcefully. Artery damage and reduced blood flow lead to conditions such as a stroke, heart attack or kidney disease.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 原发性高血压：大多数高血压病因不明，称为原发性或特发性高血压
text_en: In most cases, the cause of high blood pressure or hypertension is unknown. This type of high blood pressure is called primary or essential hypertension.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 生活方式治疗：健康饮食、限制钠盐和加工食品摄入（钠导致水潴留增加血容量）、避免过量饮酒、规律运动、减重、戒烟；医生也可能推荐药物治疗
text_en: Treatment for essential hypertension includes lifestyle changes, such as eating a healthy If you are sensitive to the sodium in salt, your doctor may recommend limiting your intake of salt and highly processed foods. Your doctor may cause your body to retain water, which increases both your blood volume and your blood pressure. Other lifestyle changes that can reduce blood pressure include avoiding excessive alcohol intake, getting regular exercise, losing weight if you are overweight, and quitting smoking, and your doctor may also recommend medications that act on your kidneys, blood vessels, or heart to help reduce your blood pressure.

title: ANH13100 High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 降压药物类型：利尿剂促进肾脏排出盐和水以降低血容量；β受体阻滞剂降低心率和收缩力；ACE抑制剂、ARB、钙通道阻滞剂和直接血管扩张剂通过舒张血管增大管径来降压
text_en: Diuretics commonly called water pills cause your kidneys to move more salt and water from your blood into your urine, which reduces your blood volume and pressure. Beta blockers reduce the workload on your heart by decreasing both the rate of your heart beat and the strength of your heart's contractions. Several types of drugs act directly or indirectly to reduce your blood pressure by relaxing your blood vessels, which increases their diameter. These drugs include ACE inhibitors, angiotensin-2 receptor blockers, calcium channel blockers, and direct acting vasodilators.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 高脂血症（高胆固醇）的定义：血液中脂质水平过高，主要脂质类型为胆固醇和甘油三酯
text_en: If you have hyperlipedemia, commonly known as high cholesterol, you have high levels of lipids in your blood. Lipids are fats and fat-like substances. In this condition, the most common types of lipids are cholesterol and triglycerides.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 胆固醇的正常生理功能：肝脏合成胆固醇，用于构成细胞膜、皮肤合成维生素D、腺体合成激素、肝脏合成胆汁酸帮助消化脂肪
text_en: Your body needs cholesterol to function properly. It makes all the cholesterol you need, mostly in your liver. The cells in your body need cholesterol as part of their cell membrane. Your skin uses sunlight and cholesterol to make vitamin D. Certain glands, such as the testicles in men and the adrenal glands, use cholesterol to make important chemicals called hormones. And your liver uses cholesterol to make bile acids, which help to digest fat in the food

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 甘油三酯的功能与来源：为身体提供能量，来源于食物和肝脏，高脂食物、精制碳水化合物、高糖食物和酒精会升高甘油三酯
text_en: Your body uses triglycerides for energy. Triglycerides in your blood come from food and your liver. Foods high in triglycerides include fatty foods, refined carbohydrates, foods high in simple sugars, and alcohol. From your liver, your lipids are not able to move through your bloodstream without some adjustment.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 脂蛋白的形成与分类：肝脏将蛋白质包裹在脂质外形成脂蛋白以便在血液中运输，脂蛋白类型决定胆固醇的好坏（VLDL为坏胆固醇LDL的来源）
text_en: To enable their movement, your liver wraps certain proteins around the lipids. The resulting new molecule called a lipoprotein can move through your bloodstream and throughout your body to the cells that need it. Depending on which type of lipoprotein your cholesterol is part of, determines whether your cholesterol is good or bad. For example, your liver makes very low-density lipoproteins, or VLDLs, which are the source of bad cholesterol, or LDL.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: VLDL的代谢过程：VLDL富含胆固醇和甘油三酯，在血液中运输甘油三酯至细胞供能；多余的甘油三酯储存为体脂，VLDL失去甘油三酯后转变为LDL
text_en: These lipoproteins are stuffed with cholesterol and triglycerides. VLDL travels through your bloodstream, delivering triglycerides to your cells, which use them for energy. If your liver makes more VLDL than your body needs for energy, the VLDL stores the extra triglycerides as body fat.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: LDL（坏胆固醇）与动脉粥样硬化：LDL过多会在血管壁沉积形成脂质斑块，血管壁损伤更易促进斑块形成，逐渐使血管狭窄、血流减少
text_en: After losing its triglycerides, a VLDL becomes a low-density lipoprotein or LDL. LDL travels through your bloodstream, delivering cholesterol to the cells that need it. If your body makes too much LDL, it can build up in your blood. LDL can deposit in the walls of your blood vessels, causing a build-up of fatty material called plaque. Ligitage to vessel walls can make it easier for LDL to form plaques. Over time, this build-up can narrow the blood vessel and reduce blood flow. This is why LDL is called the bad cholesterol.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 斑块的临床后果：冠状动脉斑块导致冠心病和心脏病发作风险增加，颈动脉斑块减少脑部血流并增加中风风险
text_en: A common place this plaque can build up is in your coronary arteries, which are the blood vessels that feed your heart muscle. This build-up causes coronary artery disease and increases your risk of a heart attack. Plac in other arteries, such as the carotid arteries in your neck, can reduce blood flow to your brain and increase the risk of a stroke.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: HDL（好胆固醇）的保护作用：HDL含蛋白质多、脂质少，能从细胞和血管斑块中清除多余胆固醇，并将其运回肝脏排出体外（胆固醇逆转运）
text_en: Your liver also makes high-density lipoprotein or HDL, also known as the good cholesterol. HDL has more protein and very little cholesterol and triglycerides compared to LDL. HDL helps remove excess cholesterol from your cells and from plaque in your blood vessels. This is why HDL is called the good cholesterol. HDL returns the excess cholesterol to your liver, which removes it from your body.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 血脂检测方法：建议20岁以上每五年做一次空腹脂蛋白谱检测，需禁食9-12小时后抽血，检测总胆固醇、LDL、HDL和甘油三酯水平
text_en: If you are 20 years of age or older, the National Institutes of Health recommends you have a blood test every five years called a fasting lipoprotein profile. It measures your levels of total cholesterol, LDL, HDL and triglycerides. During this test, a blood sample will be taken from your arm or finger after you have not eaten for nine to twelve hours.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 血脂目标值：总胆固醇<200、HDL≥60、LDL<100、空腹甘油三酯<150（mg/dL）；具体目标因个人健康状况而异，建议咨询医生
text_en: The total cholesterol goal should be less than 200 mg per desoliter. For most people, an ideal HDL or good cholesterol should be 60 or higher. An ideal LDL or bad cholesterol should be less than 100. And fasting triglycerides should be less than 150. Your specific goals may vary depending on your health situation. Ask your doctor what your lipid goals should be.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 饮食管理与脂肪类型：心脏健康饮食应多吃瘦肉蛋白和蔬果；饱和脂肪和反式脂肪升高LDL（来源于肉类、乳制品、加工食品、热带油脂），不饱和脂肪更健康（来源于鱼、坚果、植物油）
text_en: If your cholesterol levels are too high, it's important to eat a heart-healthy diet with lean sources of protein and plenty of fruits and vegetables. Also, the types of fats you eat can affect your cholesterol level. For example, saturated fats and trans fats tend to raise LDL cholesterol in your blood. These fats are usually solid at room temperature and are found in meat and dairy products, many processed foods and tropical oils such as coconut oil, palm oil and cocoa butter. Unsaturated fats, including polyunsaturated and mono-unsaturated fats, are healthier fats that are usually more liquid at room temperature. They are found in foods such as fish, nuts and vegetable oils.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 生活方式干预：戒烟和规律运动（如快走、跑步）有助于改善胆固醇水平；生活方式改变不足时医生可能开具药物
text_en: Other lifestyle decisions you can make to help your cholesterol are not smoking and getting regular exercise such as brisk walking or running. If lifestyle changes can reduce your cholesterol levels enough, your doctor may prescribe certain medications to reduce it.

title: ANH13104 Hyperlipidemia (High Cholesterol)
summary_zh: 降脂药物治疗：他汀类（减少肝脏合成胆固醇）、烟酸（降LDL和甘油三酯、升HDL）、胆汁酸结合剂（促进肝脏消耗胆固醇补充胆汁）、贝特类（降甘油三酯、升HDL）、胆固醇吸收抑制剂（减少肠道吸收）
text_en: Statins are drugs that reduce the amount of cholesterol made in your liver. They affect LDL levels more than HDL or triglyceride levels. Niasin reduces LDL cholesterol and triglycerides and increases HDL cholesterol made in your liver. Bial acid binding resins are drugs that prevent the reuse of bile after helping to digest your food. As a result, your liver uses more cholesterol to replace the lost bile, which means less cholesterol in your bloodstream. Fibrates are mainly used to reduce triglyceride levels in your blood, but they can also raise HDL levels. Cholesterol absorption inhibitors reduce the amount of cholesterol absorbed by your intestines from the food you eat.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 颅脑解剖结构：头骨作为大脑的保护层，头骨内侧有三层脑膜（硬脑膜、蛛网膜、软脑膜），蛛网膜与软脑膜之间的蛛网膜下腔含有血管和脑脊液
text_en: Your skull is made of bone and serves as a hard protective covering for your brain. Just inside your skull, three layers of tissue called meninges surround your brain. The thick, outermost layer is the duromotor. The middle tissue layer is the arachnoid motor. And the innermost layer is the pia-motor. Between the arachnoid motor and the pia-motor is the subarachnoid space, which contains blood vessels and a clear fluid called cerebrospinal fluid.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 脑部血管与颅内压平衡：桥静脉连接脑表面与硬脑膜，脑动脉为大脑供血；正常脑功能需要血管内血液、脑脊液和脑组织之间的压力平衡，即正常颅内压
text_en: Blood vessels, called bridging veins, connect the surface of your brain with the duromotor. Other blood vessels, called cerebral arteries, bring blood to your brain. Inside your skull, normal brain function requires a delicate balance of pressure between the blood in your blood vessels, the cerebrospinal fluid that surrounds your brain, and your brain This is called normal intracranial pressure.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 颅内压升高的原因与后果：脑肿瘤、头部损伤、血管问题或脑脊髓感染可导致颅内压升高，压迫大脑引起肿胀或变形，可能导致严重脑损伤
text_en: Increased intracranial pressure may result from brain tumors, head injuries, problems with your blood vessels, or infections in your brain or spinal cord. These conditions put pressure on your brain and may cause it to swell or change shape inside your skull, which can lead to serious brain injury.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 开颅术的适应症：切除脑肿瘤、活检取样、清除血肿、引流脑脊液、排脓；缓解脑肿胀、止血、修复异常血管和颅骨骨折、修复受损脑膜；治疗癫痫、脑内给药、植入深部脑刺激器等医疗设备
text_en: Your doctor may recommend a craniotomy to remove abnormal brain tissue such as a brain tumor, a sample of tissue by biopsy, a blood clot called a hematoma, excess cerebrospinal fluid, or pus from an infection called an abscess. A craniotomy may also be done to relieve brain swelling, stop bleeding called a hemorrhage, repair abnormal blood vessels, repair skull fractures, or repair damaged meninges. Finally, a craniotomy may also be done to treat brain conditions such as epilepsy, deliver medication to your brain, or implant a medical device such as a deep brain stimulator.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 手术准备：以切除脑肿瘤为例，患者接受全身麻醉，在肿瘤位置剃除头皮毛发
text_en: The most common reason for a craniotomy is to remove a brain tumor. For your procedure, you will be given general anesthesia to make you unconscious and pain-free. The skin on your scalp will be shaved over the location of the tumor.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 开颅术手术步骤：切开头皮，在颅骨上钻孔并连接形成骨瓣后取下，切开硬脑膜暴露脑组织，切除肿瘤，缝合硬脑膜，用金属板和螺钉将骨瓣复位固定
text_en: To begin, your surgeon will make an incision in your scalp. One or more small holes will be made in your skull. Then your surgeon will connect the holes to create a circular piece of bone called a bone flap and remove it. Your surgeon will make an incision in the duromotor to expose your brain. Then your tumor will be removed. The incision in the duromotor will be closed with sutures. Your surgeon will reattach the bone flap with metal plates and sprues that will remain in your skull for life.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 去骨瓣减压术（craniectomy）：当脑肿胀严重或颅骨感染时，术中不立即回置骨瓣，待肿胀消退后再行回置；无论哪种术式，最后用订书钉缝合头皮切口并包扎
text_en: You may have a variation on a craniotomy called a craniectomy if your brain is very swollen or your skull is infected. Your surgeon may delay reattaching the bone flap until a later procedure to allow time for the swelling to go down. At the end of either a craniotomy or a craniectomy, your scalp incision will be closed with staples. And your head will be wrapped in bandages.

title: ANH13109 Craniotomy and Craniectomy
summary_zh: 颅骨成形术（cranioplasty）：去骨瓣减压术后待肿胀消退，择期行颅骨成形术，用金属板和螺钉重新固定骨瓣并缝合皮肤切口
text_en: If you had a craniectomy, you will have another procedure called a cranioplasty at a later date after the swelling goes down. At that time, your surgeon will reattach your bone flap with metal plates and sprues. Then close the skin incision with staples.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 急性冠脉综合征的定义：由心肌供血阻塞引起的一组疾病，属医学急症，可能正在发生心肌梗死（心肌开始坏死）。
text_en: If you have acute coronary syndrome, you have one or more conditions caused by a blockage of blood flow to your heart muscle. This is a medical emergency because you may be having a heart attack, a condition in which your heart muscle begins to die.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 心脏正常解剖与供血机制：心脏作为泵血器官，左心室收缩将富氧血液经主动脉泵出，冠状动脉从主动脉分支为心肌提供氧气和营养。
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients your body needs. The main pumping chamber of your heart is the left ventricle. When your left ventricle contracts, it sends oxygen-rich blood to your body through a large artery called the aorta. Connected to your aorta are small arteries called coronary arteries. Blood flows from your aorta through the coronary arteries to supply your heart muscle with oxygen and nutrients.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 急性冠脉综合征的常见病因——动脉粥样硬化：脂肪斑块在冠状动脉内壁积聚使管腔狭窄，斑块破裂可形成血栓阻塞动脉，血栓是冠脉阻塞最常见的原因。
text_en: If you have acute coronary syndrome, blood flow through your coronary arteries is severely reduced or completely blocked. One possible cause of reduced blood flow is atherosclerosis. In this condition, a build-up of a fatty substance called plaque can narrow your coronary If this plaque ruptures, a blood clot can form and block the artery. A blood clot is the most common cause of coronary artery blockage.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 其他较少见病因：冠状动脉痉挛（药物、吸烟、寒冷、极端情绪等诱因导致冠脉突然收缩）和冠状动脉夹层（冠脉内壁分离阻断血流）。
text_en: Other less common causes of reduced blood flow include coronary artery spasm or dissection. In a coronary artery spasm, triggers such as drugs, smoking, cold weather, and extreme stress or emotions can cause a temporary and sudden tightening of a coronary artery. During a coronary artery dissection, the inside wall of one of your coronary arteries separates which can block blood flow.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 冠脉阻塞的后果——心肌梗死：无论何种原因，冠脉阻塞使对应区域心肌缺氧缺营养并开始坏死，即心脏病发作（心肌梗死/MI）。
text_en: Regardless of the cause, a blockage in either coronary artery prevents the oxygen and nutrients in your blood from reaching the part of your heart supplied by the artery. As a result, heart muscle in that area starts to die. Death of part of your heart muscle is called a heart attack. It's also known as a myocardial infarction or MI.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 急性冠脉综合征的症状：胸部疼痛、紧缩或烧灼感（心绞痛），可放射至上腹部、肩部、手臂、颈部和下颌；静息或轻度活动时发作的不稳定型心绞痛是ACS的主要症状；其他症状包括呼吸困难、头晕、恶心和出汗。
text_en: A blocked coronary artery may also cause you to feel sudden pain, discomfort, tightening, or a burning sensation in your chest called angina. This pain may extend to your upper abdomen, shoulders, arms, neck, and lower jaw. If you have angina when you're at rest or frequent angina that prevents even moderate physical activity, you have unstable angina which is the main symptom of acute coronary Other symptoms of acute coronary syndrome include shortness of breath, dizziness, nausea, and sweating.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 药物治疗：氧疗增加血氧、阿司匹林等抗凝药预防血栓、溶栓药溶解已有血栓、硝酸甘油和吗啡扩张冠脉并缓解心绞痛、β受体阻滞剂减慢心率降低心肌耗氧。
text_en: If you've had a heart attack or have other types of acute coronary syndrome, your doctor may prescribe oxygen therapy to get more oxygen into your blood. You may take aspirin or other prescription blood thinner drugs to prevent blood clots. Thrombolytics, also known as clot buster drugs, may be used to break up any existing blood Cards such as nitroglycerin and morphine will relax your coronary arteries and relieve the pain of angina. You may also receive drugs called beta blockers that slow down your heart and reduce its need for oxygen.

file: ANH13113_Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack.mp4
title: ANH13113 Acute Coronary Syndrome and Heart Attack
summary_zh: 手术治疗：冠脉血管成形术（球囊扩张阻塞的冠脉并可植入支架保持通畅）和冠状动脉旁路移植术（CABG，用静脉或人工移植材料绕过阻塞区域）。
text_en: Your doctor may also recommend immediate surgical procedures such as coronary angioplasty, in which a balloon-tipped catheter inflates inside your blocked coronary artery to open it. After inflating, the balloon catheter may leave behind a mesh-like device called a stent to hold your artery open. Or you may have a coronary artery bypass graft or cabbage. Cigilage is a surgical procedure in which the blocked areas of the coronary arteries are bypassed with veins or artificial graft material.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
summary_zh: 精索静脉曲张的定义：阴囊内一组肿胀的静脉血管
text_en: A varicoseal is a group of swollen blood vessels called veins in a man's scrotum.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
summary_zh: 男性生殖系统基础解剖：睾丸的位置、功能（产生精子和睾酮），阴囊的散热作用
text_en: The male reproductive system includes the testicles, also known as testes, which are two glands inside a pouch of skin called the scrotum. The testicles normally produce and store reproductive cells called sperm. They also produce a hormone called testosterone. Testosterone is important for sperm production, muscle strength, and sex drive. The scrotum is outside the body, which keeps the testicles cool enough to make sperm.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
summary_zh: 精索及睾丸血管解剖：精索结构（血管和输精管）、睾丸动脉供血、蔓状静脉丛回流、睾丸静脉内单向瓣膜防止血液回流
text_en: Each testicle is suspended inside the scrotum by a structure called the spermatic cord. Inside each cord are blood vessels, and a tube that sperm travel through called the vas deferens. The testicular artery is the blood vessel that brings most of the blood to the testicle. Most blood leaves the testicle through a group of veins called the Pampiniform Plexus. The blood also leaves the testicle through smaller veins next to the vas deferens that bypass the Pampiniform Plexus. From the Pampiniform Plexus, blood flows into the testicular veins. A series of one-way valves inside the testicular veins prevent blood from flowing backward into the testicles.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
