summary_zh: 精索静脉曲张的病因机制：静脉瓣膜损坏导致血液回流、左侧睾丸静脉更长更直导致压力更大、左肾静脉受肠系膜上动脉压迫（胡桃夹综合征）
text_en: A varicoseal may begin to form when the valves inside the testicular vein are damaged or don't work properly. The valves may not close completely, allowing blood to flow backward inside the vein to the testicle. In addition, since the left testicular vein is straighter and longer than the right testicular vein, there is a greater downward pressure of the blood inside the left vein. Another problem may occur where your left testicular vein connects to a larger vein coming from your kidney, called the left renal vein. An artery to the intestines may squeeze the left renal vein, causing blood to back up in the left testicular vein. This is called nutcracker syndrome.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
summary_zh: 精索静脉曲张的形成过程及危害：血液淤积导致静脉肿胀、局部温度升高影响精子生成、降低男性生育能力、长期可导致睾酮水平下降
text_en: As a result of any of these problems, a varicoseal can form in the left Pampiniform Plexus when blood begins to back up in it. The blood pooling in these veins causes them to become swollen. Depending on the severity of the varicoseal, the pooled blood may raise the temperature around the testicle. This higher temperature can make it harder for the testicle to produce sperm. This condition may decrease male fertility, which is the ability to get a woman pregnant. Over time, a varicoseal may also decrease the level of testosterone made in the testicles. Low testosterone can lead to decreased energy and muscle strength, as well as sexual problems.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
summary_zh: 治疗指征与手术方式选择：通常无需治疗，疼痛或不育时可行腹股沟或腹股沟下显微精索静脉结扎术，手术切口位置
text_en: A varicoseal doesn't usually cause symptoms or require treatment. However, if a varicoseal causes pain, discomfort, or problems with fertility, the most common treatment is either an in-winnel or sub-in-winnel microsurgical varicoselectomy. To begin, the surgeon will make a small incision low on the groin for a sub-in-winnel varicoselectomy, or slightly higher for an in-winnel varicoselectomy.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
summary_zh: 显微精索静脉结扎术操作步骤：提出精索、分离曲张静脉、金属夹夹闭并切断静脉、输精管旁小静脉保留回流、精索复位并缝合切口
text_en: Then, the spermatic cord will be pulled out through the incision. The surgeon will open the outer tissue of the spermatic cord and separate the varicoseal from the rest of the tissue. Small metal clips will be placed on these veins. Then, the veins will be cut. After these veins are cut, blood will still be able to leave the testicle through the smaller veins on the vas deferens. These veins are less likely to become swollen. The surgeon will finish the procedure by placing the spermatic cord back through the skin incision and closing the incision with sutures and skin closure tape.

title: ANH14119 Varicocele Repair
summary_zh: 其他治疗方式简述：腹腔镜精索静脉修补术和经皮精索静脉栓塞术
text_en: Other procedures that may be done to repair a varicoseal include a laparoscopic varicoseal repair or percutaneous varicoseal embolization.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
summary_zh: 引入干细胞概念：人体含有多种器官，构成这些器官中所有不同细胞的基础细胞称为干细胞。
text_en: The human body contains organs such as bones, the brain, heart, and reproductive organs. The basic cells that give rise to all of the different cells in these organs are called stem cells.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
summary_zh: 全能干细胞（受精卵/合子）：卵子受精后形成的合子是人体发育的第一个细胞，称为全能干细胞，可以分化为体内任何类型的细胞，还能形成脐带和胎盘。
text_en: One type of stem cell is an egg cell from a woman that has been fertilized by a sperm from a man. This single cell, called a zygote, is the first cell in a developing human being. It's also called a totipotent stem cell because it can form any type of cell in the body, as well as the umbilical cord and placenta.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
summary_zh: 胚胎干细胞与多能性：合子分裂数次后形成囊胚，胚胎干细胞来源于囊胚内部细胞，具有多能性（pluripotent），可分化为体内任何细胞，但不能形成脐带或胎盘。
text_en: After a zygote has divided a few times, it becomes an early stage embryo called a blastocyst. Embryonic stem cells come from the cells inside the blastocyst. Embryonic stem cells are pleuripotent. This means they can form any cell in the body, but not the cells in the umbilical cord or placenta.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
summary_zh: 胚胎干细胞的实验室来源：实验室中的胚胎干细胞来自体外受精（IVF）过程中剩余的胚胎捐赠，体外受精是一种帮助女性怀孕的辅助生殖技术。
text_en: In the lab, embryonic stem cells are donated from leftover embryos created during in vitro fertilization. In vitro fertilization is a procedure to help a woman become pregnant.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
summary_zh: 成体干细胞：出生后某些器官（如皮肤）中存在少量成体干细胞，具有多潜能性（multipotent），只能分化为与所在器官相关的少数细胞类型。以皮肤为例，成体干细胞可分裂产生新的干细胞或分化为更特化的皮肤细胞，替换因衰老或损伤丢失的细胞。
text_en: Another type of stem cell is an adult stem cell. Small groups of these cells are found in some organs such as the skin, after birth, and into adulthood. Adult stem cells are multipotent. This means they can only become a few different cell types related to the organ where they're For example, the skin contains a small number of adult stem cells that can divide to create new skin adult stem cells. Or they can become more specialized skin cells to replace those that are lost due to cell aging or damage.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
summary_zh: 诱导多能干细胞（iPSC）：科学家可在实验室中将普通体细胞（如皮肤细胞）诱导转变为多能干细胞，与胚胎干细胞类似，可分化为体内任何类型的细胞。
text_en: In the lab, scientists can now induce or cause a regular body cell such as a skin cell to change into a pleuripotent stem cell. Like embryonic stem cells, these induced pleuripotent stem cells can become any type of cell in the body.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
summary_zh: 干细胞研究意义与前景：科学家研究干细胞如何分化为多种细胞类型，未来有望用于再生因损伤或疾病而受损的组织和器官。
text_en: Most study stem cells to learn how and why they become many different types of cells. In the future, these cells may be used to regrow tissues and organs that have been damaged by injury or disease.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
summary_zh: 干细胞疗法与白血病：干细胞疗法目前仅用于治疗血液疾病和血液肿瘤。以白血病为例，患者骨髓产生大量异常白细胞，无法正常抵抗感染，异常细胞逐渐排挤健康白细胞的生成。
text_en: Stem cell therapy is a procedure that uses stem cells to treat a disease or condition. Currently, stem cell therapy only treats diseases and cancers of the blood. In leukemia, for example, the patient's bone marrow makes many abnormal white blood cells that can't do their job to fight infection. Over time, these abnormal cells crowd out the production of healthy white blood cells.

title: ANH14132 Stem Cells
summary_zh: 造血干细胞移植治疗过程：医生从患者、捐献者或新生儿脐带血中采集含健康造血干细胞的样本，先通过化疗药物或放射线杀灭患者体内的异常白细胞及其干细胞，再将健康造血干细胞移植入患者体内，使其生成正常血细胞（包括白细胞）以恢复免疫功能。
text_en: In stem cell therapy for leukemia called hematopoetic stem cell transplantation, a doctor will take a sample of blood or bone marrow from the patient or a donor. In some cases, donor blood may come from a baby's umbilical cord after it's born. These tissue samples contain healthy hematopoetic or blood-forming stem cells. In addition, doctors will give the patient chemotherapy drugs or use radiation to kill the abnormal white blood cells and their stem cells. Once the abnormal cells are gone, the doctor will transplant the healthy hematopoetic stem cells from the tissue samples into the patient. These healthy stem cells will make new blood cells, including normal white blood cells, which will allow the body to fight off infections.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
summary_zh: 正常心率介绍：心脏每分钟跳动60-100次，运动时加快、休息时减慢，正常心率和节律确保富氧血液输送到全身器官（如大脑和肺）。
text_en: Depending on activity level, the heart beats about 60 to 100 times per minute. It may be higher during exercise or lower at rest. A normal heart rate and rhythm ensures the delivery of oxygen-rich blood to all of the body's organs, such as the brain and lungs.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
summary_zh: 心脏传导系统的工作原理：心脏传导系统通过电脉冲控制心跳的速度和节律。每次心跳从右心房的窦房结（SA node）开始，电信号扩散至心房和心室壁，引起它们依次收缩，此过程每次心跳重复进行。
text_en: A group of cells in the heart, called the cardiac conduction system, uses electrical impulses to control the speed and rhythm of each heartbeat. Each heartbeat starts in the right atrium in the sinusoidal atrial or SA node, then spreads through the walls of the heart chambers, called the atria, and ventricles, causing them to contract. This process repeats with each heartbeat.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
summary_zh: 心律失常的定义与分类：心脏传导系统出现问题导致心律异常，称为心律失常（arrhythmia），可发生在心房或心室。三种主要类型包括：颤动（fibrillation，心跳节律不规则）、心动过速（tachycardia，心率>100次/分）和心动过缓（bradycardia，心率<60次/分）。
text_en: Problems with the cardiac conduction system cause the heart to have an abnormal rhythm called aniridhmia. This may cause an irregular pulse. Aridhmia may happen in the atria or ventricles. Ritms of aridmias include fibrillation, which is an irregular heartbeat rhythm, tachycardia, which is a fast heartbeat of more than 100 beats per minute, and bradycardia, which is a slow heartbeat of less than 60 beats per minute.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
summary_zh: 心房性心律失常：房颤（atrial fibrillation）是最常见的心律失常类型，随机电脉冲导致心房快速无序颤动。室上性心动过速（SVT）中，局灶性房性心动过速由心房壁小区域异常放电引起快速但不规则收缩；心房扑动（atrial flutter）由心房壁较大区域异常传导引起快速但不规则收缩。
text_en: Fibrillation is the most common type of aridhmia. Random impulses cause the atria to fibrillate or twitch rapidly and randomly. Tachycardia in the atria is called supraventricular tachycardia. In focal atrial tachycardia, small areas within the atria wall start or pass along impulses that cause the atria to contract rapidly, but with irregular rhythm. In atrial flutter, larger areas within the atrial wall start or pass along impulses that cause the atria to contract rapidly, but with irregular rhythm.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
summary_zh: 心室性心律失常：心室性心动过速表现为心室快速规律收缩，因心室未完全充盈即收缩导致身体供血不足。最严重的心律失常是室颤（ventricular fibrillation），心室壁大量随机电脉冲导致心室颤抖而非正常搏动，属于医疗紧急情况，心脏无法有效泵血。
text_en: Tachycardia may also happen in the ventricles with rapid and regular contractions. The body may not receive enough blood because the ventricles contract before completely filling with blood. The most serious aridhmia is ventricular fibrillation, where many random impulses fire rapidly within the ventricular walls. In ventricular fibrillation, the ventricles are quivering instead of beating. This is a medical emergency because the heart cannot effectively pump blood to the body

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
summary_zh: 心动过缓：窦房结功能异常或电脉冲传导至心室的通路出现问题，可导致心动过缓（心跳过慢）。心跳过慢时，身体可能无法获得足够的富氧血液。
text_en: Sometimes problems with the SA node or problems with the pathway of the electrical impulses to the ventricles can cause the slow heartbeat in bradycardia. If the heartbeat is too slowly, the body may not receive enough oxygen-rich blood.

title: ANH14135 Cardiac Arrhythmia
summary_zh: 心律失常的治疗方法：根据心律失常类型，医生可能建议以下治疗方案——生活方式改变（健康饮食、运动、戒烟）、药物治疗（抗心律失常药物和β受体阻滞剂）、导管消融术（通过导管用热能或冷能破坏引起心律失常的组织）、以及植入式设备（起搏器或除颤器）来纠正心脏节律。
text_en: Depending on the type of aridhmia, a doctor may recommend one or more of the following treatments. Life style changes, such as eating a heart-healthy diet, exercising, and quitting smoking. Medication, such as anti-aridmic drugs and beta blockers, catheter ablation, where thin wires inserted into the heart, destroy the tissue causing the aridhmia with hot or cold energy, and implantable devices such as a pacemaker or defibrillator to correct the pace or rhythm of the heart.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
summary_zh: 正常心率与心脏功能概述：心脏每分钟跳动60-100次，运动时加快、休息时减慢，正常的心率和节律确保富氧血液输送到全身器官（如大脑和肺）。
text_en: Depending on activity level, the heart beats about 60 to 100 times per minute. It may be higher during exercise or lower at rest. A normal heart rate and rhythm ensures the delivery of oxygen-rich blood to all of the body's organs, such as the brain and lungs.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
summary_zh: 心脏传导系统与心律失常：心脏中的心脏传导系统通过电脉冲控制心跳的速度和节律；当传导系统出现问题时，就会发生心律失常（arrhythmia）。
text_en: A group of cells in the heart, called the cardiac conduction system, uses electrical impulses to control the speed and rhythm of each heartbeat. An abnormal heart rate or rhythm, called an arrhythmia, occurs when there's a problem with the heart's conduction system.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
summary_zh: 心律失常的类型与导管消融术适应症：心动过速（tachycardia）是心跳过快的心律失常，颤动（fibrillation）是心跳不规则的心律失常；对于某些类型的心律失常，可能需要进行导管消融术来阻止引起心律失常的心脏组织。
text_en: Taca-cardia is a type of arrhythmia where the heart beats too fast. Fibrillation is a type of arrhythmia where the heart beats irregularly and may be too For certain types of arrhythmias, a catheter ablation procedure may be necessary to stop the heart tissue from causing the arrhythmia.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
summary_zh: 导管消融术的血管入路操作：在腹股沟区域局部麻醉后，医生将导管鞘插入股静脉，然后将柔性导管通过鞘管插入，并经下腔静脉引导至心脏。
text_en: After numbing a small area in the groin with a needle, the doctor will insert a short hollow tube called a catheter sheath into the femoral vein. Next, a long flexible tube called a catheter will be inserted through the sheath. The doctor will guide the catheter to the heart through a blood vessel that goes to the heart, called the inferior vena-cava.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
summary_zh: 导管定位与消融过程：监测导管位置和进程，医生利用心脏电活动的三维图谱定位异常区域，导管尖端释放热能或冷能消融该区域组织，使其停止工作。
text_en: The location and progress of the catheter will be monitored. When the catheter reaches the heart, the doctor will guide it to the area that is causing the arrhythmia. The doctor will find the problem area is using a 3D map of the electrical activity of the patient's heart. The tip of the catheter will emit either hot energy or cold energy to ablate the tissue in this area. The patient makes the treated area stop working.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
summary_zh: 心房心律失常的消融方式：对于心房心律失常，医生消融引起问题的心房组织；若病变区域小且明确，采用局灶消融（focal ablation）；若病变区域较大且节律紊乱复杂，则采用消融重塑术（ablation remodeling）。
text_en: For an atrial arrhythmia, a doctor will ablate the atrial tissue causing it. If the affected tissues are small, well-defined areas, the procedure is called focal ablation. Or if the affected tissues are larger areas with more complex rhythm disturbances, the doctor may perform a procedure called ablation remodeling.

title: ANH14137 Catheter Ablation
summary_zh: 消融效果与心室心律失常治疗：两种消融方式均可恢复正常电脉冲传导、预防心律失常复发；若心律失常源于心室，医生同样可采用局灶消融或消融重塑术进行治疗。
text_en: Both types of ablation restore normal electrical impulses and prevent an arrhythmia from happening. If the cause of the arrhythmia is in the ventricle, the doctor can do either focal ablation or ablation remodeling to treat more complex arrhythmias of the ventricle.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
summary_zh: 心脏四腔结构介绍：右心房、左心房、右心室、左心室，以及左心室通过主动脉向全身泵血供氧的正常功能
text_en: The heart has four muscular pumping chambers, the right atrium, the left atrium, the right ventricle, and the left ventricle. The left ventricle pumps blood out through a large artery called the aorta to supply the body with the oxygen and nutrients it needs.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
summary_zh: 左心衰竭的定义与分型：左心室无法泵出足够血液；舒张性心衰（左心室无法充分舒张充盈）和更常见的收缩性心衰（左心室收缩力不足）
text_en: When left-sided heart failure, the left ventricle is not able to pump enough blood to the body. The two types of left-handed heart failure are diastolic, where the left ventricle can't relax enough to fill with blood, and more commonly systolic, where the left ventricle can't contract strongly enough to push blood to the body.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
summary_zh: 终末期心衰与LVAD适应症：严重心衰时心脏持续衰弱且对药物无反应，医生可能建议植入左心室辅助装置（LVAD）帮助心脏泵血
text_en: In severe or end-stage heart failure, the heart is getting weaker and doesn't respond to medication. For left-sided end-stage systolic heart failure, a doctor may recommend an implantable left ventricular assist device, or LVAD, to help the heart pump enough blood to the body.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
summary_zh: LVAD工作原理：LVAD是植入胸腔或腹腔的小型机械泵，血液从左心室经入流管进入泵体，再经出流管被推入主动脉，随后正常输送至全身
text_en: An LVAD is a small mechanical pump implanted inside the chest or abdomen that takes over the job of the weak left ventricle. Blood flows from the left ventricle through an inflow tube to the pump. The pump pushes the blood through an outflow tube into the aorta. From here, the blood travels normally from the aorta to the rest of the body.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
summary_zh: LVAD外部组件：驱动线缆连接体内泵与体外控制单元；控制单元是监控泵功能并发出警报的小型电脑；电池组为泵和控制单元供电；控制单元和电池装在轻便的患者背包中
text_en: A cable, called a drive line, connects the LVAD pump inside the chest to a control unit located on the outside of the body. The control unit is a small computer that controls the functions of the pump and alerts the patient if there are any problems. The pump and control unit receive power through a cord connected to a battery pack. Finally, the control unit and battery pack are held in a lightweight patient pack.

title: ANH14141 Left Ventricular Assist Device (LVAD)
summary_zh: LVAD的临床用途：可作为等待心脏移植前的过渡治疗，或对于无法接受移植的患者用作终点治疗（长期治疗）
text_en: An LVAD may be a temporary treatment until a heart transplant can be performed, or if the patient isn't able to have a heart transplant, an LVAD may be used for destination therapy, which is long-term treatment.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
summary_zh: 甘油三酯的定义与来源：甘油三酯是血液中的一种脂质，为身体提供能量。其来源包括肝脏合成和饮食摄入。可升高甘油三酯的食物包括高脂肪食物（如热狗、热带油脂）、精制碳水化合物（如薯条）、高糖食物（如甜甜圈）以及酒精。
text_en: Triglycerides are a type of fat or lipid found in your blood. Your body uses triglycerides for energy. One place triglycerides come from is your liver. Another is from the food you eat. Foods that may raise your triglycerides include fatty foods such as hot dogs and tropical oils, refined carbohydrates such as french fries, foods high in simple sugars such as donuts, and alcohol.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
summary_zh: 甘油三酯的转运与利用机制：食物中的甘油三酯经消化后到达肝脏，在肝脏中与蛋白质结合形成脂蛋白（lipoprotein）。脂蛋白通过血液循环将甘油三酯运送至全身细胞供能，多余的甘油三酯则被脂肪细胞储存为体脂。
text_en: So how do triglycerides get to the cells that need them? Glycerides in food are digested and eventually reach your liver. In the liver, both the triglycerides from your food and those made in your liver are packaged together with proteins. Each resulting molecule is called a lipoprotein. The lipoproteins travel throughout the body in your bloodstream. They deliver triglycerides to your cells which use them for energy. Or if you have more than you need, fat cells store the triglycerides as body fat.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
summary_zh: 甘油三酯的检测与数值范围：美国国立卫生研究院建议20岁以上人群每5年进行一次空腹脂蛋白谱检测（禁食9-12小时后抽血）。正常空腹甘油三酯应低于150 mg/dL，500 mg/dL及以上属于极高水平，意味着血液中脂肪过多。
text_en: If you are 20 years of age or older, the National Institutes of Health recommends you have a blood test every 5 years. The test is called a fasting lipoprotein profile. During this test, a blood sample will be taken from your arm after you have not eaten for 9-12 hours. This blood test can reveal whether your triglycerides fall into a healthy range. For most people, fasting triglycerides should be less than 150 mg per desoliter. 500 or higher puts you into the very high range. This means there is too much fat in your blood.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
summary_zh: 高甘油三酯的健康风险：极高的甘油三酯水平会增加胰腺炎及心血管疾病（如心脏病）的风险。建议咨询医生了解自身甘油三酯的目标水平。
text_en: Very high triglycerides can increase your risk of pancreatitis and other cardiovascular events like heart disease. Ask your doctor what your triglyceride level should be.

title: ANH15144 Understanding Triglycerides
summary_zh: 甘油三酯的管理与治疗：生活方式改变（低糖低精制碳水饮食、限酒、规律运动、体重管理、戒烟）有助于维持健康的甘油三酯水平。药物也可用于治疗，需遵医嘱服药并报告副作用。
text_en: In many cases, lifestyle changes can help maintain healthy triglyceride levels. These changes include eating a diet low in sweets, refined carbohydrates and alcohol, eating regular exercise, managing your weight and not smoking. Medications can also help treat impaired triglyceride levels. If your doctor prescribed medication to treat high triglycerides, it is important to take it as directed and report any side effects you may have.

title: ANH15145 LDL and HDL Cholesterol
summary_zh: 胆固醇基础知识：胆固醇是肝脏合成的脂类物质，也来源于食物，被包装成脂蛋白颗粒。人体需要胆固醇来合成激素、维生素D和帮助消化的胆汁。
text_en: Cholesterol is a fat-like substance made by your liver and also comes from foods you intake that is then packaged into particles called lipoproteins. Your body needs cholesterol to make hormones, vitamin D, and a substance that helps you digest food called bile.

title: ANH15145 LDL and HDL Cholesterol
summary_zh: 两种脂蛋白介绍：低密度脂蛋白（LDL，坏胆固醇）和高密度脂蛋白（HDL，好胆固醇）的命名与分类。
text_en: This video discusses two types of lipoproteins that carry cholesterol, low-density lipoprotein or LDL cholesterol, also known as bad cholesterol, and high-density lipoprotein or HDL cholesterol also known as good cholesterol.

title: ANH15145 LDL and HDL Cholesterol
summary_zh: LDL的作用与危害：LDL在血液中运输胆固醇至细胞；过量LDL沉积在动脉壁内，与其他物质形成脂肪斑块（粥样硬化），逐渐使动脉狭窄、血流减少，因此被称为坏胆固醇。
text_en: LDL travels through your bloodstream, delivering cholesterol to the cells that need it. If your body has too much LDL, it can build up in the walls of your arteries. LDL and other substances in your artery wall form a fatty deposit called plaque. Over time, plaque can narrow the artery and reduce blood flow. LDL carries cholesterol into the plaque. This is why LDL is called the bad cholesterol.

title: ANH15145 LDL and HDL Cholesterol
summary_zh: 斑块导致的疾病：冠状动脉斑块堆积引发冠心病，增加心脏病发作风险；颈动脉等其他动脉的斑块堆积减少脑部血流，增加中风风险。
text_en: A commonplace this plaque can build up is in your coronary arteries, which are the blood vessels that feed your heart. This plaque build up causes coronary artery disease and increases your risk of a heart attack. plaque build up in other arteries, such as the carotid arteries in your neck, can reduce blood flow to your brain and increase the risk of a stroke.

title: ANH15145 LDL and HDL Cholesterol
summary_zh: HDL的保护作用：肝脏合成HDL（好胆固醇），HDL从细胞、组织和血管斑块中移除多余胆固醇，并将其运回肝脏排出体外，发挥逆向胆固醇转运的保护功能。
text_en: Your liver also makes high-density lipoprotein or HDL also known as the good cholesterol. HDL helps remove excess cholesterol from your cells, tissues, and from plaque in your blood vessels. This is why HDL is called the good cholesterol. HDL returns the excess cholesterol to your liver, which removes it from your body.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
summary_zh: 心脏健康饮食与有害脂肪：饱和脂肪、氢化脂肪和反式脂肪会升高低密度脂蛋白（LDL）即坏胆固醇，这些脂肪存在于肉类、乳制品、热带油脂和加工食品中
text_en: If your cholesterol levels are too high, it's important to eat a heart-healthy diet. To eat a heart-healthy diet, you need to understand how the types of fats you eat can affect your cholesterol level. For example, saturated fats, hydrogenated fats, and trans fats tend to raise the bad cholesterol, also called low-density lipoprotein or LDL cholesterol in your blood. These fats are found in meat and dairy products, tropical oils, and many processed foods, such as cakes and cookies.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
summary_zh: 不饱和脂肪的益处：用不饱和脂肪（多不饱和脂肪和单不饱和脂肪）替代饱和脂肪可降低坏胆固醇，这类脂肪存在于三文鱼、牛油果、坚果和橄榄油中
text_en: Facing saturated fats with unsaturated fats may help lower the bad cholesterol in your blood when eaten as part of a heart-healthy diet. Unsaturated fats include polyunsaturated and mono-unsaturated fats and are found in foods such as salmon, avocados, nuts, and olive oils.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
summary_zh: 其他健康生活方式：戒烟和中等强度有氧运动（如每周五天每天30分钟步行骑车或游泳），建议咨询医生制定适合的饮食和运动计划
text_en: Other heart-healthy lifestyle decisions you can make are to stop smoking and get adequate, moderate intensity aerobic exercise. Walking, biking, or swimming 30 minutes a day, five days a week, may be a good exercise Talk to your doctor about the type of diet and exercise plans most appropriate for your health and cholesterol levels.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
summary_zh: 他汀类药物：当生活方式改变不足以降低胆固醇时医生可能处方药物，他汀类是最常见的降LDL药物，通过减少肝脏合成胆固醇起效，同时可适度降低甘油三酯并升高HDL好胆固醇
text_en: If lifestyle changes can't reduce your cholesterol levels enough, your doctor may prescribe certain medications to reduce it. Modest medicines are the most common medication for lowering LDL or bad cholesterol by reducing the amount made in your liver. However, they have modest effects on lowering another type of fat in your blood called triglycerides, and raising good cholesterol, also called high-density lipoprotein or HDL cholesterol.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
summary_zh: 胆酸结合剂：肝脏利用胆固醇合成胆汁帮助消化，胆酸结合药物与胆汁结合阻止其参与消化，迫使肝脏消耗更多胆固醇来制造胆汁从而降低血液中胆固醇水平
text_en: Your liver uses cholesterol to make a substance called bile, which helps digest your food. Cholesterol acid binding drugs bind to bile and prevent it from being used in digestion. This causes the liver to use more cholesterol to make more bile, thereby decreasing the amount of cholesterol in your bloodstream.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
summary_zh: 胆固醇吸收抑制剂与贝特类药物：胆固醇吸收抑制剂阻断肠道对食物中胆固醇的吸收，贝特类药物主要降低甘油三酯同时可升高HDL水平
text_en: Cholesterol absorption inhibitors block some of the cholesterol taken up by your intestines from the food you eat. Fibrates are mainly used to reduce triglyceride levels in your blood, but they can also raise HDL levels.

title: ANH15146 High Cholesterol Management
summary_zh: 烟酸类药物：烟酸可降低LDL胆固醇和甘油三酯，同时增加肝脏合成的HDL胆固醇
text_en: Cholesterol acid reduces LDL cholesterol and triglycerides and increases HDL cholesterol made in your liver.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
summary_zh: 空腹脂蛋白检测介绍：NIH建议20岁以上每5年检测一次，检测项目包括总胆固醇、LDL（坏胆固醇）、HDL（好胆固醇）和甘油三酯
text_en: The National Institutes of Health cholesterol treatment guidelines recommend you have a blood test every five years if you are twenty years or older. The test, called a fasting lipoprotein profile, measures your levels of total cholesterol LDL, also known as bad cholesterol HDL, also known as good cholesterol and a type of fat in your blood called triglycerides.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
summary_zh: 空腹采血要求：采血前需禁食9-12小时，空腹对确保检测结果准确至关重要
text_en: During this test, a blood sample will be taken after you have not eaten for nine to twelve Helping is important to ensure accurate results for this test.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
summary_zh: 各项血脂指标的理想目标值：总胆固醇<200mg/dL，HDL≥60，LDL<100，空腹甘油三酯<150
text_en: The total cholesterol goal should be less than 200mg per deciliter. For most people, an ideal HDL or good cholesterol should be 60 or higher. An ideal LDL or bad cholesterol should be less than 100. And fasting triglycerides should be less than 150.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
summary_zh: 高胆固醇与心脏病的关系：高胆固醇增加心脏病风险，冠状动脉被含胆固醇的蜡状斑块堵塞，斑块减少或阻断富氧血液流向心肌，导致心脏病发作
text_en: If you have high blood cholesterol levels, you have a greater risk of heart disease. In heart disease, blood vessels called coronary arteries become narrowed or blocked by a waxy substance containing cholesterol, called plaque. For time, plaque may reduce or block the flow of oxygen-rich blood to your heart muscle and cause a heart attack.

title: ANH16169 Blood Test for Cholesterol
summary_zh: 心脏病的危险因素：可控因素包括高胆固醇和甘油三酯、高血压、糖尿病/糖尿病前期、超重/肥胖、吸烟、缺乏运动、不健康饮食和压力；不可控因素包括年龄、性别和心脏病家族史
text_en: There are many known risk factors for heart disease. Risk factors you can control include high blood cholesterol and triglycerides, high blood pressure, diabetes, pre-diabetes, being overweight or obese. Smoking, lack of physical activity, an unhealthy diet, and stress. Risk factors you can't control include age, gender, and having a family history of heart

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
summary_zh: 血压测量工具：使用血压袖带（血压计/sphygmomanometer）测量血压的方式。
text_en: Your blood pressure can be measured with a blood pressure cuff called a sphigmomanometer.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
summary_zh: 收缩压与舒张压的定义：心脏跳动时血液对动脉壁的压力为收缩压，心脏在两次跳动之间舒张时的压力为舒张压。
text_en: When your heart beats, the pressure of blood on the walls of your arteries is called systolic pressure. When your heart relaxes between beats, pressure on the artery wall is called diastolic pressure.

file: ANH16170_How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed.mp4
title: ANH16170 How High Blood Pressure Is Diagnosed
summary_zh: 血压正常值与高血压诊断标准：血压全天会波动，但理想收缩压应低于120、舒张压应低于80；若收缩压经常高于130或舒张压经常高于80，则诊断为高血压。
text_en: Your blood pressure may change throughout the day. However, optimal systolic pressure should be less than 120 and optimal diastolic pressure should be less than 80. If your systolic pressure frequently stays above 130 or your diastolic pressure frequently stays above 80, you have high blood pressure.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
summary_zh: 血压的基本定义：血压是血液对动脉壁施加的压力，身体严格调控血压以确保血液到达全身各处。动脉是将血液从心脏输送到组织的血管。
text_en: Blood pressure is the amount of force caused by blood pressing against the walls of your arteries. It is tightly controlled to ensure sufficient blood reaches all parts of your body. Arteries are blood vessels that carry blood away from your heart to your tissues.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
summary_zh: 心脏泵血机制：心脏有两个心室，右心室将缺氧血泵向肺部获取氧气，左心室将富氧血通过动脉泵向全身，为组织提供氧气和营养。
text_en: This blood supplies your tissues with the oxygen and nutrients they need to function. In your heart there are two lower chambers called ventricles which contract with each heart beat. The right ventricle pushes oxygen poor blood to your lungs to pick up oxygen and the left ventricle pushes oxygen rich blood through your arteries to your body.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
summary_zh: 影响血压的第一个因素——心输出量：血液流经动脉时有四个主要因素影响血压，第一个是心输出量（每分钟心室泵出的血量），心输出量增加则血压升高。
text_en: As blood flows through your arteries four main factors affect the pressure on the artery walls. The first is the amount of blood your ventricles push out of your heart each minute, also known as cardiac output. Your blood pressure goes up as cardiac output increases.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
summary_zh: 影响血压的第二个因素——血容量：体内血液总量增加会导致血压升高。
text_en: The second factor affecting your blood pressure is the total amount of blood in your body or blood volume. Blood pressure also goes up as blood volume increases.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
summary_zh: 影响血压的第三个因素——血管阻力：阻力是阻碍血液流动的力量，主要受动脉管径影响。动脉扩张可降低血压，动脉收缩则升高血压。
text_en: The third factor that affects your blood pressure is resistance. Resistance is anything working against the blood flow through your arteries. Resistance is mainly affected by the width of your arteries. Your body is able to increase the width of your arteries to lower your blood pressure. Or it can reduce the width to raise your blood pressure.

title: ANH16171 Understanding Basic Blood Pressure Control
summary_zh: 影响血压的第四个因素——血液黏度：血液中蛋白质和脂肪等颗粒增多会使血液变稠，血液越稠心脏泵血越费力，血压随之升高。
text_en: The fourth factor that affects your blood pressure is viscosity. Viscosity is how thick your blood is. In your blood more particles such as proteins and fat increase viscosity. If your blood is thicker your blood pressure goes up as your heart works harder to push it through your arteries.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 高血压对动脉壁的损伤：长期高血压会损害动脉壁，导致危及生命的疾病
text_en: Over time, high blood pressure will damage the walls of your arteries. This damage can lead to life-threatening conditions.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 动脉瘤的形成与破裂：动脉壁变薄膨大形成动脉瘤，可能破裂出血至周围组织
text_en: For example, an artery wall may become weak and form an enlarged area called an aneurysm. The wall may burst and bleed into the tissue around it.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 动脉粥样硬化与血栓形成：受损动脉壁吸引胆固醇、脂肪和钙质形成斑块，斑块增大导致血流减少，血细胞附着形成血栓，进一步阻塞血流
text_en: In another example, damage to an artery wall may attract certain substances in your blood, such as cholesterol, fat and calcium. They may form a buildup called a plaque. As the plaque gets bigger, blood flow in the artery is reduced. Blood cells can stick to the plaque and form solid clumps called clots. Blood clots further reduce or completely block your blood flow.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 血栓导致脑卒中和心肌梗死：脑部血管血栓可引发中风，心脏血管血栓可引发心脏病发作
text_en: If this happens in your brain, it can lead to a stroke. If it happens in your heart, it can lead to a heart attack.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 高血压导致肾脏疾病：肾脏动脉受损使肾功能下降，可发展为肾病
text_en: In another example, damage to the arteries in your kidneys may reduce their ability to work properly. This can lead to kidney disease.

title: ANH16172 Complications of High Blood Pressure (Hypertension)
summary_zh: 高血压导致心力衰竭：动脉损伤迫使心脏更用力跳动，血压进一步升高，最终可导致心衰
text_en: In a final example, damage to your arteries makes your heart beat even harder, which raises your blood pressure even more and can lead to heart failure.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
summary_zh: 高血压（hypertension）的病因概述：多数情况下病因不明，这类高血压称为原发性高血压（primary/essential hypertension）
text_en: In most cases, the cause of high blood pressure or hypertension is unknown. This type of high blood pressure is called primary or essential hypertension.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
summary_zh: 饮食相关的生活方式干预：健康饮食、减少钠盐摄入；解释钠导致体液潴留从而升高血压的机制
text_en: Treatment for it includes lifestyle changes such as eating a healthy diet. If you are sensitive to the sodium insult, your doctor may tell you to eat less salt and processed foods. Sodium may cause your body to retain water. This increases the amount of your blood and makes your blood pressure go up.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
summary_zh: 其他生活方式干预措施：限制饮酒、规律运动、减重、戒烟
text_en: Other lifestyle changes can lower your blood pressure. They include not drinking too much alcohol. Getting regular exercise. Losing weight if you need to. And quitting smoking.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
summary_zh: 过渡到药物治疗：医生可能推荐作用于肾脏、血管或心脏的降压药物
text_en: Your doctor may also recommend medications that act on your kidneys, blood vessels, or heart to help lower your blood pressure.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
summary_zh: 利尿剂（水丸）的作用机制：促使肾脏将更多盐分和水分从血液排入尿液，从而减少血容量、降低血压
text_en: Diuretics commonly called water pills cause your kidneys to move more salt and water from your blood into your urine. This reduces both the amount of your blood and your blood pressure.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
summary_zh: β受体阻滞剂的作用机制：通过减慢心率和降低每次心搏的力量来减轻心脏负荷
text_en: Beta blockers reduce the workload on your heart in two ways. They slow your heart beat and lower the force of each beat.

title: ANH16173 Treatment for High Blood Pressure
summary_zh: 血管扩张类药物：通过舒张血管降低血压，包括ACE抑制剂、血管紧张素II受体阻滞剂、钙通道阻滞剂和直接作用血管扩张剂
text_en: Several types of drugs lower your blood pressure by relaxing your blood vessels. This makes them open up wider. These drugs include ACE inhibitors, angiotensin-2 receptor blockers, calcium channel blockers, and direct acting vasodilators.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
summary_zh: 心脏基本解剖与冠状动脉供血：心脏作为泵血器官，左心室将富氧血通过主动脉泵出，冠状动脉从主动脉分出为心肌供应氧气和营养
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients your body needs. The main pumping chamber of your heart is the left ventricle. In your left ventricle contract, it sends oxygen-rich blood to your body through a large artery called the aorta. Connected to your aorta are small arteries, called coronary arteries. Blood flows from your aorta through the coronary arteries to supply your heart muscle with oxygen and nutrients.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
summary_zh: 冠状动脉阻塞的病理过程：脂肪斑块在冠状动脉内堆积，斑块破裂后形成血栓，导致血管严重狭窄或完全阻塞
text_en: Using your heart attack, blood flow through one of your coronary arteries may have been severely reduced or completely blocked. Your reduced blood flow may have been caused by a buildup of a fatty substance called plaque in your coronary arteries. If this plaque became disrupted, a blood clot might form and severely worsen the narrowing or lead to a sudden complete blockage stopping blood flow down the artery.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
summary_zh: 心肌梗死的发生机制：冠状动脉阻塞使氧气和营养无法到达心肌，导致心肌细胞坏死，即心肌梗死（MI）
text_en: A blockage in your coronary arteries prevented the oxygen and nutrients in your blood from reaching the part of your heart supplied by the artery. As a result, heart muscle in that area started to die. Damage to part of your heart muscle is called a heart attack. It's also known as a myocardial infarction, or MI.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
summary_zh: 冠状动脉介入治疗——球囊血管成形术与支架植入：通过球囊导管扩张阻塞的冠状动脉，并植入金属网状支架支撑血管保持通畅
text_en: Your heart doctor may have recommended a procedure to help open the blockage and improve blood flow to the damaged area. The procedure you had may have been a coronary angioplasty. During a coronary angioplasty, a balloon-tipped catheter inflates inside your blocked coronary artery to open it. The procedure may have involved placing a stent to help prop the artery open. This is usually a thin metal mesh that acts as a scaffold. It's important to know that while the stent opens up an artery in your heart, it will not help with plaque buildup that may be in other arteries in your body.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
summary_zh: 冠状动脉搭桥手术（CABG）：使用身体其他部位的静脉或动脉绕过冠状动脉阻塞区域，恢复心肌血供
text_en: Or you may have had a coronary artery bypass graft or cabbage. Cabbage is a surgical procedure in which the blocked areas of the coronary arteries are bypassed with veins or other arteries from the body.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
summary_zh: 术后药物治疗（一）——抗血小板药物：出院后需服用口服抗血小板药物，防止血小板聚集形成新血栓，尤其对支架患者防止支架内血栓
text_en: Before you left the hospital, your healthcare provider most likely prescribed several medications. Your medication may include the following. Oral antiplatelet therapy helps prevent platelets from sticking together and forming new blood If you have a stent, this medication helps prevent clots from forming inside it.

title: ANH16180 About Your Heart Attack
summary_zh: 术后药物治疗（二）——降压药与降脂药：包括β受体阻滞剂、ACE抑制剂、ARB、钙通道阻滞剂用于降压，他汀类及非他汀类药物配合低脂饮食降低胆固醇
text_en: You may have also received drugs called beta blockers that help lower your heart rate and blood pressure. Drugs such as angiotensin converting enzyme or ACE inhibitors, angiotensin receptor blockers or ARBs, and calcium channel blockers also work to lower your blood pressure if needed. And you may have been prescribed statins along with a low fat diet to lower your cholesterol. These drugs work by reducing the amount of cholesterol made in your liver. They also help your liver pull cholesterol out of your blood. If statins don't work well or have serious side effects, your healthcare provider may prescribe non-statin medications. They work in different ways to lower your cholesterol.

title: ANH16181 Heart Failure Overview - Basic
summary_zh: 心脏的基本功能：心脏是肌肉器官，负责泵送含有氧气和营养物质的血液供全身使用
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients your body needs.

title: ANH16181 Heart Failure Overview - Basic
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的定义与基本病理：心脏无法泵出足够血液，可能影响单侧或双侧心脏；导致肺部氧合不足、全身供血不足，引起体液潴留和肺水肿
text_en: Heart failure means that your heart can't pump enough blood around your body. In some cases, heart failure may be temporary, but in most cases, some of the heart damage is perforated. Heart failure can affect both sides of your heart or just one side. Your heart may not pump enough blood to your lungs to pick up enough oxygen. Your heart also may not pump enough oxygen-rich blood out to the rest of your body. This can cause the body to hold onto water and let fluid build up in your body and in your lungs.

title: ANH16181 Heart Failure Overview - Basic
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的病因：多种疾病可削弱或加重心脏负担，包括冠状动脉疾病、高血压、糖尿病、心脏瓣膜病变，以及先天性或感染/毒素引起的心肌病
text_en: Many health conditions that weaken or overwork the heart can cause heart failure. A health condition may keep your heart from squeezing as hard as it should. Health conditions that can cause heart failure include coronary artery disease, high blood pressure, diabetes, heart valve problems, and heart muscle problems that are present at birth or caused by infection or toxins.

title: ANH16181 Heart Failure Overview - Basic
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的常见症状：活动后气短、平卧呼吸困难、夜间阵发性呼吸困难、疲劳、下肢/腹部/颈静脉水肿；需每日称重监测体重突然增加作为病情恶化信号
text_en: Sometimes heart failure doesn't cause any symptoms, especially if it isn't severe. But it often causes these common symptoms, feeling out of breath with a small amount of activity, feeling out of breath when trying to lie flat, waking up in the middle of the night, feeling very short of breath, feeling tired a lot, and having swelling especially in the legs, belly, and blood vessels of the neck. Be sure to tell your health care provider right away if symptoms get worse. Your provider will ask you to weigh yourself every day and watch for sudden weight gain, which can be a sign of worsening heart failure.

title: ANH16181 Heart Failure Overview - Basic
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的诊断检查：心电图检测心脏电节律、超声心动图评估心功能、胸部X光检查心脏增大及肺部积液、血液检查寻找病因、运动负荷试验等
text_en: To find out if heart failure is causing your symptoms, your health care provider may do certain tests. You might need an electrocardiogram, which is a test that looks at your heart's electrical rhythm. You may also have an echocardiogram. This is a test that uses sound waves to see how well your heart works. A chest x-ray can show if your heart is enlarged or if there is fluid in your lungs. Sometimes blood tests can help find the cause of heart failure. Your health care provider might also want to see how your heart performs during exercise. You might need other tests too.

title: ANH16181 Heart Failure Overview - Basic
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的生活方式治疗：低盐饮食、限制液体摄入、限制酒精、减轻体重、保持体力活动、戒烟
text_en: Your health care provider will work with you to find the best treatment for your symptoms and to keep your heart failure from getting worse. You may also need to treat the cause of your heart failure. Lifestyle changes are an important part of treatment. Your health care provider may recommend a diet that is low in salt. He or she may also ask you to limit the amount of fluid you drink. You should also limit alcohol consumption. Losing weight Stain physically active and quitting smoking can also help.

title: ANH16181 Heart Failure Overview - Basic
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的药物与手术治疗：利尿剂排除多余液体、ACE抑制剂降低血压、其他药物减轻心脏负担；根据病因可能需要介入手术治疗
text_en: You will likely get prescription medications to treat your heart failure. One common type of medication is diuretic. Diuretics help to get rid of excess fluid. You may also be prescribed an ACE inhibitor. This is a drug to reduce your blood pressure. Other drugs may help decrease how hard your heart needs to work. Depending on the cause of your heart failure, your health care provider may recommend a procedure or surgery to help your heart. Your provider can tell you more about the best options for your heart failure.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
summary_zh: MRI概述：介绍磁共振成像的定义、基本原理（利用强磁体、射频波和计算机生成体内图像）及其相比其他成像方法的优势（图像更清晰详细）。
text_en: Magnetic resonance imaging, commonly known as MRI, helps doctors find, watch, and treat medical problems. MRI uses very strong magnets, radio waves, and a computer to make pictures of the inside of the body. MRI pictures are more detailed than those made with other methods.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
summary_zh: MRI的临床适应症：列举MRI可检测的多种病变，包括脑出血、脑卒中引起的脑部改变、腰椎间盘突出等脊柱问题，以及前交叉韧带撕裂等体内损伤。
text_en: There are many reasons to have MRI. MRI may find bleeding, such as bleeding in the brain. Brain changes as in stroke. Problems with your back, such as a herniated disc. And an injury inside the body, such as a torn anterior cruciate ligament.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
summary_zh: MRI安全注意事项：检查前需移除所有金属物品，部分医疗植入物（如心脏起搏器）不适合进行MRI检查，患者需向技术人员告知体内任何金属或植入装置。
text_en: Before MRI, you will remove all metal objects. And it's not safe to have MRI with some medical implants. You should tell the MRI technologist about any metal, device, or implant you may have in your body, such as a pacemaker.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
summary_zh: 检查前准备流程：患者躺在扫描台上，佩戴耳塞或耳机以隔绝噪音，使用枕头和固定带保持正确体位，准备就绪后扫描台滑入扫描仪内部。
text_en: To prepare for MRI, you will lie on a table just outside the scanner. MRI scanners are very noisy, so you will probably wear earplugs or headphones. Different equipment, such as pillows and straps, will help you stay in the correct position. Once you are ready, the table will slide into the scanner.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
summary_zh: 扫描仪类型与操作方式：对比封闭式扫描仪（内部空间狭窄但图像质量更高）和开放式扫描仪（侧面开放、减轻幽闭恐惧但图像质量可能较低）；技术人员在相邻房间操作设备，通过双向对讲系统与患者保持沟通。
text_en: Most often, closed scanners, such as this one, are used. The area inside is very narrow. In some areas, open scanners are available. This scanner is open on the sides, so you won't be completely surrounded by the machine. However, the pictures may not be as good as with closed scanners. For either type of scanner, a technologist will operate it from the next room. You will be able to talk to each other through a two-way speaker.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
summary_zh: MRI成像原理：扫描时机器内磁体产生强磁场，随后向人体发射射频波（均无害），关闭射频波后扫描仪接收人体释放的能量信号，利用这些信号生成图像。
text_en: During the scan, magnets in the machine will create a strong magnetic field. This will not harm you. Then, the scanner will send radio waves through your body. This will not harm you. When the radio waves are turned off, the scanner will pick up energy signals from your body. These signals are used to make the pictures.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
summary_zh: 扫描过程中的要求与造影剂使用：扫描期间患者需保持静止不动以确保图像清晰，可能需要短暂屏气；整个检查耗时30至45分钟或更长；部分检查需注射造影剂以获得更清晰的图像。
text_en: During the scan, you will have to stay very still, so the images will be clear. You may be asked to hold your breath for a short time. MRIs may take 30 to 45 minutes or longer to complete. In some cases, you may get contrast dye with your MRI. The dye helps make clearer pictures.

title: ANH16182 Magnetic Resonance Imaging (MRI)
summary_zh: 检查后注意事项：MRI结束后可恢复正常活动；若使用了造影剂，建议多饮水以帮助肾脏排出体内的造影剂。
text_en: After the MRI, you can resume your normal activities. If you received contrast dye, the technologist may ask you to drink plenty of water or other healthy drinks to help your kidneys remove the dye from your body.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
summary_zh: 静脉曲张的定义与就医原因：静脉曲张是扩大扭曲的静脉，通常发生在腿部，虽然一般不会引起问题，但疼痛或外观不佳时可寻求治疗
text_en: Varicose veins are enlarged, twisted veins. They usually occur in your legs. Varicose veins don't usually cause problems, but if they're painful or you don't like the way they look, you may want to seek treatment.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
summary_zh: 治疗的必要性与症状：治疗可预防未来手术（如全髋关节置换）中的血栓形成；患者可能出现皮肤变色、搏动性疼痛、痉挛、瘙痒、反复皮肤感染、出血或溃疡等症状
text_en: Treatment may also help prevent blood clots from forming in the event you have a future surgical procedure, such as a total hip replacement. Some patients may have discolored skin, throbbing, aching or cramping, itching, recurrent skin infections, bleeding, or ulcers.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
summary_zh: 四种常见治疗方法概述：硬化疗法、激光/微电治疗、静脉切除术和腔内消融术，这些手术可在诊所完成，无需大切口，恢复期短
text_en: Most cases of varicose veins can be treated with the following procedures. Flare therapy, surface treatments using laser or microelectric devices, phlebectomy, and endovineous ablation. These procedures can be done in your doctor's office, require no large cuts, and have a short recovery period.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
summary_zh: 硬化疗法（Sclerotherapy）：适用于较小静脉和蜘蛛静脉，通过向静脉注射化学物质使其受刺激、结疤，最终闭合消退；可能需要数周多次治疗
text_en: You may need more than one treatment over several weeks to see results. Most larytherapy works best on smaller veins and spider veins. In this procedure, a chemical is injected into the vein. The chemical irritates and scars the vein. This causes the vein to close off and fade away.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
summary_zh: 表面激光和微电治疗：用于治疗皮肤表面附近的较小静脉，通过激光或电流产生的热量使静脉消退
text_en: Surface laser and microelectric treatments are used to treat smaller veins near the skin In these procedures, the heat from small bursts of laser light or electric current make the vein fade away.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
summary_zh: 静脉切除术（Phlebectomy）：用于治疗皮肤表面附近的较小静脉，在静脉上方做微小切口，将静脉拉出并移除
text_en: A phlebectomy is also used to treat smaller veins near the skin surface. During a phlebectomy, a tiny cut is made in the skin over the vein. Then the vein is pulled out and removed.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
summary_zh: 腔内消融术（Endovenous Ablation）：用于治疗较深的静脉曲张，通过小切口插入导管，利用激光或射频产生的热量关闭静脉使其消退
text_en: Endovineous ablation is a procedure to treat deeper varicose veins. In this procedure, a flexible tube called a catheter is inserted through a small cut into The catheter is used to send heat from either laser light or radio waves into the vein. The heat closes off the vein and causes it to fade away.

title: ANH17190 Treatment for Varicose Veins  Procedures
summary_zh: 静脉剥离术（Vein Stripping）：少数情况下医生建议的更具侵入性的手术，通常在医院进行，恢复时间更长，在受损静脉上方做两三个切口，将静脉结扎后从下方切口拉出
text_en: On rare occasions, your healthcare provider may advise a more invasive procedure, such as vein stripping. It's usually performed in a hospital and may require more recovery time. Only two or three cuts are made in the skin over the damaged vein. Then the vein is tied off and pulled out through the lower cut.

title: ANH18207 Cardiovascular Conditions and Type 2 Diabetes
summary_zh: 高血压与2型糖尿病的关系：血液对血管壁的压力过高，心脏负担加重，部分高血压患者会发展为2型糖尿病
text_en: If you have high blood pressure, it means the force of the blood pushing on the walls of your blood vessels is too high. Your heart has to work harder when your blood pressure is high. Some people with high blood pressure develop type 2 diabetes.

title: ANH18207 Cardiovascular Conditions and Type 2 Diabetes
summary_zh: 血脂异常与2型糖尿病的关系：LDL胆固醇（坏胆固醇）过高、HDL胆固醇（好胆固醇）不足、甘油三酯过高，这些血脂异常可同时存在，且与2型糖尿病发病率升高相关
text_en: If you have abnormal levels of lipids in your blood, it may mean you have too much LDL cholesterol, also known as bad cholesterol. Or it may mean that you don't have enough HDL cholesterol, also known as good cholesterol. Or your triglyceride level, also known as the fat in the blood, may be too high. You may have more than one of these lipid abnormalities at the same time. People with abnormal lipid levels have a higher rate of type 2 diabetes.

title: ANH18207 Cardiovascular Conditions and Type 2 Diabetes
summary_zh: 2型糖尿病的定义与危害：血糖水平升高，长期高血糖会损伤心脏及其他器官的血管，导致其他健康问题
text_en: Type 2 diabetes is a condition where your blood glucose or blood sugar is elevated. Over time, high blood sugar can damage the blood vessels of your heart and other organs, leading to other health problems.

title: ANH18207 Cardiovascular Conditions and Type 2 Diabetes
summary_zh: 三种疾病并存的综合危害：增加冠心病、心梗、中风甚至死亡的风险，可能需要更多药物治疗和护理，还可能导致抑郁
text_en: Having high blood pressure, abnormal lipid levels and type 2 diabetes can affect you in many You will have a higher risk for other serious health problems such as a coronary artery disease, heart attack, stroke and even death. You may need help taking care of yourself. You may also need to take more medications. Living with these conditions may cause depression. The good news is that there are things you can do to help lower your risk of developing type 2 diabetes.

title: ANH18207 Cardiovascular Conditions and Type 2 Diabetes
summary_zh: 生活方式干预措施：戒烟、保持健康体重、低脂低钠饮食、在医生指导下进行适量运动，控制血压和血脂水平
text_en: For example, you may need to make changes to your lifestyle. If you smoke, get the help you need to quit. Maintain or get to a healthy weight. Follow a healthy eating plan including low fat and low sodium foods. And work with your healthcare provider to determine the right amount of physical activity Make sure you work with your healthcare provider to control your blood pressure and lipid

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
summary_zh: 糖尿病与心血管损伤的关系：高血糖会随时间推移损伤心脏及其他器官的血管，糖尿病病程越长，患心脏病的风险越大。
text_en: If you have diabetes, it means your blood glucose or blood sugar is elevated. Over time, high blood sugar can damage the blood vessels of your heart and other organs, leading to other health problems. This means the longer you have diabetes, the greater your risk for heart disease.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
summary_zh: 心脏病的分类：包括冠心病、心肌病、心律失常和心力衰竭等不同类型。
text_en: There are different types of heart disease, including coronary heart disease, cardiomyopathy, arrhythmias, and heart failure.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
summary_zh: 冠心病的机制：胆固醇沉积形成斑块堆积在心脏血管内壁，增加心脏病发作的风险。
text_en: Coronary heart disease is a build-up of a cholesterol-filled substance called plaque inside the blood vessels of your heart. The build-up of plaque in coronary heart disease can increase your risk of having a heart

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
summary_zh: 心肌病及其后果：心肌病是心肌疾病，可导致心律失常（异常心跳）和心力衰竭（心脏泵血能力不足，无法满足身体需求）。
text_en: cardiomyopathy refers to diseases of your heart muscle. Cardiomyopathy may lead to abnormal heartbeats called arrhythmias. Cardiomyopathy may also lead to heart failure. This means your weakened heart can't pump enough blood to meet your body's needs.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
summary_zh: 糖尿病合并心脏病的健康影响：增加心脏病发作、中风甚至死亡的风险，影响日常活动能力，可能需要他人照护、服用更多药物，并可能导致抑郁。
text_en: Even both diabetes and heart disease can affect you in many ways. You will have a higher risk for other serious health problems such as a heart attack, stroke, and even death. It may be harder for you to go about your daily activities. You may need help taking care of yourself. You may also need to take more medications. Living with these conditions may cause depression.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
summary_zh: 生活方式干预降低风险：戒烟（吸烟和糖尿病都会使血管狭窄，增加心脏病和中风风险）、改变不良生活习惯。
text_en: The good news is that there are things you can do to help lower your risk for heart disease. For example, you may need to make changes to your lifestyle. If you smoke, get the help you need to quit. Both smoking and diabetes can narrow your blood vessels which can increase your chance of developing heart disease or stroke.

file: ANH18208_Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease.mp4
title: ANH18208 Diabetes and Your Risk of Heart Disease
summary_zh: 健康管理措施：保持健康体重、遵循健康饮食计划、适量运动，并在医疗团队指导下控制血糖、胆固醇和血压。
text_en: Maintain or get to a healthy weight. Follow a healthy eating plan and get regular physical activity. Check with your healthcare provider about the right amount of physical activity for you. Make sure you work with your healthcare provider to control your blood sugar, cholesterol, and blood pressure.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
summary_zh: 大脑正常供血机制：动脉将血液输送至大脑，大脑需要持续的血流来获取氧气和营养以维持正常功能
text_en: Normally, blood vessels called arteries carry blood to the brain. The brain needs a constant flow of blood to get the oxygen and nutrients it needs to work properly.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
summary_zh: 缺血性中风之血栓性中风：中风（脑卒中）是脑部血流中断；最常见的类型是缺血性中风，动脉内脂肪斑块使血管变窄，斑块破裂形成血栓阻断脑部供血
text_en: During a stroke, also known as a brain attack, blood flow to part of the brain has stopped. The most common type of stroke is called ischemic stroke. It can happen in an artery where a fatty substance called plaque has made the artery narrow. In one type of ischemic stroke called a thrombotic stroke, the plaque can break apart and form a blood clot that blocks the flow of blood to the brain.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
summary_zh: 缺血性中风之栓塞性中风及脑细胞损伤：血栓在身体其他部位形成后随血流移动，到达脑部动脉时阻断血流；脑细胞因缺氧和营养在数分钟内开始死亡
text_en: In another type of ischemic stroke called an embolic stroke, a blood clot forms in another area of the body and travels through the blood stream. If it reaches an artery near or in the brain, it can block the flow of blood. When an artery to part of the brain is blocked during a stroke, oxygen and nutrients in the blood can't get to those brain cells. The cells start to die within minutes.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
summary_zh: 出血性中风：较少见的中风类型，由脆弱的血管破裂导致血液渗入脑组织，既阻断血流又对脑组织产生压迫损伤
text_en: A less common type of stroke is called a hemorrhagic stroke. It occurs when a weakened blood vessel breaks open and leaks blood into the brain. This blocks blood flow to the brain and the leaking blood puts pressure on the brain tissue, which damages it.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
summary_zh: 短暂性脑缺血发作（TIA）：血栓短暂阻断脑部血流，但血栓会在短时间内自行溶解，通常不造成永久性损伤
text_en: In a transient ischemic attack or TIA, a clot blocks the flow of blood to the brain. But it's different from the other types of stroke because the clot breaks up after a short time. Transient ischemic attacks usually don't cause any permanent damage.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
summary_zh: 中风的危险因素：高血压是主要风险因素，其他包括糖尿病、特定心脏疾病、吸烟、高胆固醇以及既往TIA病史
text_en: High blood pressure is the main risk factor for any type of stroke. Other risk factors include diabetes, certain types of heart disease, smoking, high cholesterol, and having previous transient ischemic attacks.

title: ANH18209 Your Risk of Stroke
summary_zh: 中风的症状与体征：面部/手臂/腿部突然麻木或无力（尤其单侧）、突然意识混乱和言语障碍、突然视力障碍、突然头晕失衡、突然剧烈头痛
text_en: It's important to know these signs and symptoms of a stroke, a sudden numbness or weakness in your face, arms and legs, especially if it's on one side of your body. Sudden confusion and trouble speaking or being unable to understand others speaking. Sudden trouble seeing out of one or both eyes. Sudden dizziness, loss of balance or being unable to walk. And a sudden severe headache.

title: ANH19224 What is High Potassium (Hyperkalemia)
summary_zh: 高钾血症的症状：轻度症状包括恶心、肌肉无力、麻木或刺痛，逐渐出现；严重症状包括呼吸急促、心悸、胸痛、恶心或呕吐，需立即就医。
text_en: There are often no symptoms of hyperkalemia, however mild symptoms that appear over time may include nausea, muscle weakness, numbness, or tingling. More severe symptoms that happen suddenly and require immediate medical care may include shortness of breath, heart palpitations, chest pain, nausea, or vomiting.

title: ANH19224 What is High Potassium (Hyperkalemia)
summary_zh: 钾在人体中的正常功能：身体需要维持血液中适量的钾以保证神经和肌肉（包括心脏）正常运作；钾来源于食物，大部分储存在细胞内，少量存在于细胞外和血液中；健康的肾脏通过尿液排出多余的钾来维持血钾平衡。
text_en: The body needs to keep the right amount of potassium in the blood for nerves and muscles including the heart to work normally. Potassium is one of several minerals that the body gets from food. Most of it is stored in the cells of the body, and a small amount stays outside the cells as well as in the blood. Healthy kidneys help to maintain normal levels of potassium in the blood by passing excess potassium out of the body in urine.

title: ANH19224 What is High Potassium (Hyperkalemia)
summary_zh: 高钾血症的危险因素：肾脏损伤或功能障碍可导致高钾血症。常见危险因素包括慢性肾病、心力衰竭、高血压（可能导致慢性肾病和心衰）、糖尿病，以及治疗这些疾病的药物可能影响肾脏维持正常钾水平的能力。
text_en: There are risk factors that may damage or impair the kidneys or prevent the body from keeping potassium in its cells, leading to hyperkalemia. Some common risk factors for hyperkalemia include chronic kidney disease, a condition where the kidneys gradually lose their ability to work properly. Heart failure, a condition where the heart has trouble pumping blood to the rest of the High blood pressure or hypertension, a disease where the force of blood on the walls of the arteries, is too high and may lead to complications like chronic kidney disease and heart failure. Diabetes, a disease where too much sugar is in the blood, and taking medications for these or other conditions which may prevent the kidneys or the body from maintaining normal levels of potassium.

title: ANH19224 What is High Potassium (Hyperkalemia)
summary_zh: 高钾血症的诊断与血钾水平：医生通过血液检测检查钾水平，正常范围约为3.5至5.0，超过5.0即为高钾血症。建议高风险人群与医生讨论管理钾水平的方法。
text_en: A doctor may do a blood test to check the body's potassium level. For most people, a normal range is from about 3.5 to 5.0. Hyperkalemia is a potassium level greater than 5. If you are at risk for high potassium, speak with your doctor about ways to manage your potassium levels.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 引入法洛四联症概念，介绍正常心脏的四个腔室结构：右心房、右心室、左心房、左心室
text_en: Petrology of fallow is a birth defect that changes normal blood flow through a baby's A healthy heart has four chambers, the right atrium and right ventricle, and the left atrium and left ventricle.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 正常心脏血液循环过程：缺氧血液从右心房→右心室→肺动脉→肺部获取氧气→左心房→左心室→主动脉→全身
text_en: After delivering oxygen to the body, oxygen poor blood flows into the right atrium. When the right atrium contracts, it loads blood into the relaxed right ventricle. Then the right ventricle contracts, sending blood to the lungs through the pulmonary artery. The blood picks up oxygen in the lungs, then returns to the left atrium. The left atrium contracts, filling the relaxed left ventricle with blood. And the left ventricle contracts to pump and deliver oxygen-rich blood to the body through

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 法洛四联症由四种心脏缺陷组成；第一种缺陷：室间隔缺损（VSD），即两个心室之间的隔壁上有一个孔，导致富氧血与缺氧血混合
text_en: Petrology of fallow is a birth defect made up of four problems with the heart and its blood vessels. A ventricular septal defect, or VSD, is a hole in the wall between the two ventricles. The hole allows oxygen-rich blood to mix with oxygen poor blood.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 第二种缺陷：主动脉骑跨，主动脉位置偏移至室间隔缺损上方，使两个心室的血液均可流入主动脉，导致部分缺氧血被输送至全身
text_en: An overriding aorta means the aorta is shifted slightly from its usual location on It sits right over the ventricular septal defect. This allows blood from both ventricles to flow through the aorta. As a result, some oxygen poor blood may flow to the body.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 第三种缺陷：肺动脉狭窄，肺动脉主干变窄且肺动脉瓣无法完全开放，导致到达肺部的血流减少
text_en: Pulmonary stenosis means that the main pulmonary artery is narrowed. And the pulmonary valve doesn't open all the way. As a result of the narrowing, less blood reaches the lungs.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 第四种缺陷：右心室肥厚，右心室壁异常增厚，由室间隔缺损的血流及右心室需更大力泵血通过狭窄肺动脉导致血压升高所致
text_en: Right ventricular hypertrophy means the wall of the right ventricle is thicker than normal. This is thought to happen in response to blood flow through the ventricular septal defect, and from having to work harder to pump blood through the narrowed pulmonary artery, which results in increased blood pressure.

title: ANH19225 Understanding Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 四种缺陷共同导致婴儿全身供氧不足，可引发紫绀症（蓝婴综合征），表现为皮肤、嘴唇和指甲呈蓝紫色
text_en: Together, these four problems may prevent enough oxygen from reaching the baby's body. This may result in episodes of a condition called cyanosis, or blue baby. In this condition, the baby's skin, lips, and fingernails may have a bluish tint.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 介绍法洛四联症的治疗概述，指出治疗方法为外科手术，用于修复四个心脏问题
text_en: The treatments for tetralogyofollow are surgical procedures to help fix the following four heart problems.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 逐一介绍法洛四联症的四个缺陷：室间隔缺损（VSD）、主动脉骑跨、肺动脉狭窄、右心室肥厚，以及各自的病理特征
text_en: A ventricular septal defect, or VSD, is a hole in the wall between the two ventricles. An overriding aorta happens when the aorta is located directly over the ventricular septal defect. This means the aorta is connected to both the left and right ventricles instead of its normal attachment to only the left ventricle. In pulmonary stenosis, the main pulmonary artery is narrowed, and the pulmonary valve doesn't open all the way. And right ventricular hypertrophy means the wall of the right ventricle is thicker than

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 说明法洛四联症通过开胸手术修复，手术分为临时修复（分流管或支架）和完全修复两种方式，两者都能增加肺部血流和提高血氧水平
text_en: Tetralogyofollow is repaired with open heart surgery, either soon after birth or in Pricle procedures may include temporary and complete repairs. Your child may not be ready for a complete repair right away. In this case, a temporary repair, such as a shunt or a stent, can be placed until a complete repair can be done. Both repairs increase blood flow to the lungs and improve oxygen levels in the blood delivered to the body.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 介绍分流管（shunt）临时修复手术：打开胸壁，在主动脉分支与右肺动脉之间放置分流管，为血液提供到达肺部获取氧气的替代通路
text_en: During a shunt procedure, the surgeon will open the chest wall to place a tube called a shunt between a branch of the aorta and the right pulmonary artery. The shunt will provide another path for blood to get to the lungs to pick up oxygen.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 介绍支架（stent）临时修复手术：通过腿部血管插入导管，将支架放置在肺动脉狭窄处以增加肺部血流
text_en: During a stent procedure, a tube called a catheter will be inserted through a small incision into a blood vessel in the leg. The cardiologist will use the catheter to place a device called a stent inside the narrowed area of the pulmonary artery. This allows more blood to flow to the lungs.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 介绍完全修复手术中肺动脉狭窄的修复方式：用补片加宽过小的肺动脉主干、加宽或替换过小的肺动脉瓣、切除阻塞血流的多余心肌组织，从而改善右心室到肺部的缺氧血流动
text_en: A complete repair is done when your child is strong enough for the procedure. During the procedure, the pulmonary stenosis defect may be repaired in the following ways. If the main pulmonary artery is too small, it will be widened with a patch. If the pulmonary valve is too small, it may be widened with a patch or replaced. If extra heart muscle is blocking blood flow below the pulmonary valve, the surgeon will remove the muscle tissue. These repairs will improve the flow of oxygen poor blood from the right ventricle to the lungs. As a result, the blood will get enough oxygen for the body's needs.

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 介绍室间隔缺损的修复：用补片覆盖室间隔上的孔洞，防止缺氧血与富氧血混合，确保只有富氧血从左心室经主动脉流向全身
text_en: To fix the ventricular septal defect, the surgeon will cover the hole with a patch. The patch will prevent the mixing of oxygen poor blood with oxygen rich blood. As a result, only oxygen rich blood will flow out of the aorta to the body from the left

title: ANH19226 Treatment for Tetralogy of Fallot
summary_zh: 说明修复肺动脉狭窄和关闭室间隔缺损后，右心室肥厚将自行恢复正常；同时在完全修复手术结束时，之前放置的临时分流管或支架将被移除
text_en: Over time, fixing the pulmonary stenosis and closing the ventricular septal defect will also fix the right ventricular hypertrophy. Since the right ventricle won't have to work as hard to pump blood, its wall will return to normal thickness. If your child still has a shunt from the temporary repair, it will either be removed or clipped shut at the end of this procedure. Similarly, if your child had a stent placed, it will also be removed.

title: ANH19227 Understanding Leukemia
summary_zh: 血液基础知识：血液由液体和三种细胞组成——红细胞（携氧）、白细胞（免疫防御）和血小板（凝血），这些细胞均由骨髓中的干细胞生成
text_en: Your blood contains fluid and three types of cells, red blood cells, white blood cells, and platelets. These cells are made in the spongy center of your bones called bone marrow. All blood cells come from parent cells called stem cells. Red blood cells carry oxygen to your body's tissues. Platelets help your blood to clot. And white blood cells help your body fight infections and any other harmful invaders or diseases.

title: ANH19227 Understanding Leukemia
summary_zh: 白血病定义与基本机制：白血病起源于白细胞形成过程中的异常，骨髓干细胞产生过多功能异常的白细胞，削弱免疫系统，并可能减少血小板和红细胞数量
text_en: Leukemia is a cancer that originates from certain white blood cells while they are forming. In leukemia, the stem cells in the bone marrow make too many abnormal white blood cells that don't work properly. This weakens your immune system. Leukemia may also reduce the amount of platelets and red blood cells.

title: ANH19227 Understanding Leukemia
summary_zh: 白血病的主要分类：按细胞来源分为淋巴细胞性白血病（异常淋巴细胞过度增殖）和髓系白血病（异常髓系细胞分化为异常髓母细胞或粒细胞）；按进展速度分为急性（快速进展）和慢性（缓慢发展）
text_en: The main types of leukemia are named for the cells they originate from and how quickly they develop. For example, lymphocytic leukemia affects white blood cells called lymphocytes. In this disease, too many lymphoid stem cells develop into abnormal lymphocytes. Myeloid leukemia affects white blood cells called myeloid cells. In this type of cancer, too many myeloid cells become abnormal and divide into abnormal myeloblasts or granulocytes which are different types of white blood cells. Each type of leukemia can be either acute which means it progresses quickly or chronic which means it develops over time.

title: ANH19227 Understanding Leukemia
summary_zh: 白血病的症状：因类型不同而异，常见症状包括疲劳虚弱、容易瘀伤和出血、发热、体重减轻及反复感染
text_en: Symptoms for leukemia vary depending on the type of leukemia and which blood cells are affected. Sometimes leukemia has no symptoms but in general, the symptoms may include feeling tired and weak, bruising and bleeding easily, fever, weight loss and frequent infections.

title: ANH19227 Understanding Leukemia
summary_zh: 白血病的分期：医生通过评估肿瘤大小和扩散程度进行分期，早期阶段意味着较少的癌症生长和扩散，晚期则意味着更广泛的扩散
text_en: As part of the diagnosis, your doctor will try to find out the size of the cancer tumors and if the cancer has spread. This is called staging. Staging varies with different types of leukemia. In general, an earlier stage or phase means less cancer growth and spread. A higher stage or phase means more cancer growth and spread throughout the body.

title: ANH19227 Understanding Leukemia
summary_zh: 白血病的危险因素：病因不明，但风险因素包括高剂量辐射暴露、某些异常基因、特定病毒感染（如EB病毒）、长期接触苯和化疗药物等化学物质、吸烟及高龄
text_en: The cause of leukemia isn't known but there are some things that can increase your risk. Being exposed to high dose radiation. Having certain abnormal genes. Infection with certain viruses such as the Epstein Bar Virus. Long-term exposure to certain chemicals such as benzene and chemotherapy drugs. Smoking and older age.

title: ANH19228 Treatment Options for Leukemia
summary_zh: 白血病概述：白血病起源于血细胞形成过程中（通常是白细胞），骨髓中的造血细胞产生大量功能异常的癌性白细胞，削弱免疫系统
text_en: Leukemia is a cancer that originates from blood cells while they are forming, usually white blood cells. In leukemia, blood forming cells in the bone marrow make many cancerous white blood cells that don't work properly. This weakens your immune system.

title: ANH19228 Treatment Options for Leukemia
summary_zh: 治疗计划与选项概览：医生会根据白血病类型、症状、年龄、健康状况等制定个性化治疗方案，选项包括化疗、放疗、靶向治疗、免疫治疗和干细胞移植
text_en: Your doctor will make a treatment plan specifically for your leukemia. It will be based on the type of leukemia, your symptoms, age, overall state of health, and other factors. Treatment options may include one or more of the following – chemotherapy, radiation therapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy, and stem cell transplant.

title: ANH19228 Treatment Options for Leukemia
summary_zh: 化疗：化疗药物通过血液循环杀灭快速生长的癌细胞
text_en: Chemotherapy uses drugs that travel through the bloodstream to kill fast growing cells like cancer cells.

title: ANH19228 Treatment Options for Leukemia
summary_zh: 放射治疗：利用辐射杀灭癌细胞或抑制其生长，白血病采用体外放射治疗，由机器从体外向癌细胞发射辐射，照射区域取决于白血病的分期和类型
text_en: Another treatment option is radiation therapy. It uses radiation to kill cancer cells or stop them from growing. The target area of the radiation varies depending on the stage and type of leukemia. The type of radiation therapy used for leukemia is external radiation. It uses a machine that aims radiation at the cancer from outside the body.

title: ANH19228 Treatment Options for Leukemia
summary_zh: 靶向治疗：靶向药物针对癌细胞表面的特异性标记物，癌细胞可能比正常细胞拥有更多这些标记物，药物结合标记物后可阻止癌细胞生长和分裂
text_en: Targeted therapy uses drugs that aim for specific targets or markers on cancer cells. These cancer cells may have more of these markers than normal cells do, targeted therapy may affect cancer cells more than normal cells. When targeted therapy drugs attach to the markers on cancer cells, the cancer cells may stop growing and dividing.

title: ANH19228 Treatment Options for Leukemia
summary_zh: 免疫治疗：利用免疫系统对抗白血病，例如单克隆抗体是实验室制造的蛋白质，可设计为结合白血病细胞上的标记物，帮助免疫细胞识别并摧毁白血病细胞
text_en: Immunotherapy can help your immune system fight leukemia. For example, monoclonal antibodies are laboratory-produced versions of proteins that your immune system makes to fight bacteria, viruses, chemicals, and other foreign substances. Monoclonal antibodies can be designed to attach to markers on leukemia cells and become part of the immune system attacking the leukemia. When attached to immune cells, monoclonal antibodies can help recognize and destroy the leukemia

title: ANH19228 Treatment Options for Leukemia
summary_zh: 干细胞移植：用自体或供体健康干细胞替换癌症治疗中受损的造血细胞，流程包括采集干细胞、储存、进行化疗或放疗杀灭血细胞、然后回输健康干细胞以恢复造血功能
text_en: A stem cell transplant replaces blood-forming cells that were damaged or destroyed during your cancer treatment with either your or a healthy donor's stem cells. First, healthy stem cells are collected from you or a donor. Then, the stem cells are stored while you receive treatment for cancer, such as chemotherapy or radiation, to kill all your blood cells. After these treatments are complete, the healthy stem cells are given to you. In your body, these stem cells provide a healthy supply of future blood cells.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
summary_zh: 血液癌症的基本概念：起源于骨髓等造血组织或免疫系统细胞，常见类型包括白血病、淋巴瘤和多发性骨髓瘤
text_en: Blood cancer, also known as hematologic cancer, begins in blood-forming tissue such as bone marrow. Bone marrow is the spongy tissue inside your bones that makes blood cells, or blood cancer may start within the cells of the immune system. Examples of blood cancer are leukemia, lymphoma, and multiple myeloma.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
summary_zh: 血液癌症的主要治疗方式：化疗、放疗、靶向治疗、免疫治疗和干细胞移植
text_en: Treatments such as chemotherapy, radiation therapy, targeted therapy, immunotherapy, and stem cell transplant may control blood cancers depending on the type of cancer and other factors.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
summary_zh: 随访护理计划：定期复查、监测癌症复发迹象和治疗副作用，包括血液检查、脑脊液检查和骨髓检查等
text_en: It is important to have a care plan in place not only for receiving therapy and personal care, but also for follow-up care. This includes scheduling and going to follow-up appointments. In addition to checking your general health and well-being, your doctor will want to check for any signs of the cancer returning and check for health problems that may have resulted from treatment. For example, you may have tests and imaging studies performed, including taking samples of your blood, fluid around your spinal cord, or bone marrow to check for cancer as relevant for your situation.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
summary_zh: 用药与治疗依从性：按医嘱服药、记录副作用、遵循化疗/放疗/骨髓移植后的护理指导
text_en: Continue taking all your medications as prescribed. Do not stop taking them without talking to your doctor. It is helpful to take notes for points to discuss that your next doctor's visit. This could include any side effects you may have noticed. If you've had treatments such as chemotherapy, radiation therapy, or a bone marrow transplant, follow any instructions you were given.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
summary_zh: 有益的生活方式改变：戒烟降低复发风险、健康饮食增强体质、适度运动（步行、骑车、游泳）缓解疲劳
text_en: There are lifestyle changes that can improve your health and help you feel better. There are benefits to stopping smoking. This lowers the risk for cancer returning or spreading. It's generally accepted that eating a healthy diet can help you feel better and stronger, both during and after treatment. Physical activities like walking, riding a bicycle or swimming can help you feel better and less tired. Talk to your doctor before starting an exercise routine.

title: ANH19229 Managing Blood Cancers
summary_zh: 心理与社会支持：面对癌症的心理压力，可寻求社工、信仰领袖、心理咨询师和互助小组的帮助
text_en: Knowing you have cancer can be overwhelming. You may have worries about your condition and how sick you could get, how it will affect you and your family, treatments and hospital stays, medical bills, and your job. There are ways to find support for you. Remember that your doctor and healthcare team are there to answer any questions you have. The following sources of support can help you, social workers, faith leaders, counselors, and support groups.

title: ANH20232 Understanding CKD Anemia
summary_zh: 红细胞基础知识：贫血的定义（红细胞不足），红细胞在骨髓中生成，铁元素用于合成携氧蛋白——血红蛋白
text_en: Anemia happens when you don't have enough red blood cells. Red blood cells are made in the center of bones called bone marrow. In red blood cells, iron is needed to make a protein that carries oxygen called hemoglobin.

title: ANH20232 Understanding CKD Anemia
summary_zh: 氧气运输过程：红细胞经血流到达肺部，氧气从肺泡进入血液并与血红蛋白结合，红细胞将氧气输送至全身细胞以维持其功能
text_en: Red blood cells flow through the bloodstream to the lungs to pick up oxygen. As blood flows through the lungs, oxygen moves from air sacs into the blood. Then oxygen attaches to the hemoglobin in red blood cells. These red blood cells deliver oxygen to cells throughout the body. Cells need oxygen to function and survive.

title: ANH20232 Understanding CKD Anemia
summary_zh: 贫血的影响：贫血时健康红细胞不足导致氧气输送减少，器官和组织功能受损；慢性肾病是贫血的常见病因
text_en: In Anemia, there are not enough healthy red blood cells to deliver oxygen to the body. This means that organs and tissues may not work as well as they should. While many conditions may cause anemia, it is often caused by chronic kidney disease.

title: ANH20232 Understanding CKD Anemia
summary_zh: CKD导致贫血的机制：肾脏产生促红细胞生成素（EPO），骨髓依赖EPO生成红细胞；肾病损伤肾脏使EPO分泌不足，骨髓生成红细胞减少从而引发贫血
text_en: The kidneys make a hormone called erythropoyetin or EPO. Bone marrow uses EPO to make red blood cells. Red kidney disease damages the kidneys so they don't make enough EPO. As a result, bone marrow makes less red blood cells causing anemia.

title: ANH20232 Understanding CKD Anemia
summary_zh: CKD贫血的症状：疲劳乏力、皮肤苍白、头痛、注意力不集中、食欲减退、胸痛、心跳加速、起立时头晕、活动后气短
text_en: Symptoms of CKD anemia may include feeling tired or weak, pale skin color, headaches, problems concentrating or thinking, loss of appetite, chest pain, a fast heartbeat, feeling light-headed when you stand up, and shortness of breath with activity.

title: ANH20232 Understanding CKD Anemia
summary_zh: 诊断方法：通过血液检测诊断CKD贫血，全血细胞计数可测量血红蛋白和红细胞数量；正常血红蛋白范围（男性13.8-17.2，女性12.1-15.1）及CKD贫血诊断阈值（男性<13，女性<12）
text_en: A doctor may test a sample of your blood to see if you have CKD anemia. For example, a complete blood count test can measure the amount of hemoglobin and red blood cells in a sample. A normal hemoglobin range in healthy adults is 13.8 to 17.2 for men and 12.1 to 15.1 for women. A low hemoglobin level in chronic kidney disease is below 13 for men and below 12 for women.

title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
summary_zh: 贫血的基本定义：血液中缺乏足够的健康红细胞来向身体输送氧气，贫血可由慢性肾病引起
text_en: Anemia means your blood does not have enough healthy red blood cells to deliver oxygen to the body. Anemia can result from chronic kidney disease.

title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
summary_zh: CKD贫血的发病机制：正常情况下肾脏产生促红细胞生成素（EPO），该激素刺激骨髓生成红细胞；肾脏受损后EPO分泌不足，骨髓无法生成足够红细胞，导致器官和组织功能受损
text_en: Normally, the kidneys make a hormone called erythropoyetin. This hormone helps bone marrow make new red blood cells. When the kidneys are damaged, they don't make enough of this hormone. As a result, bone marrow doesn't make enough red blood cells causing CKD Anemia. This means that organs and tissues may not work as well as they should.

title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
summary_zh: 治疗方案概述及ESA疗法：CKD贫血治疗包括促红细胞生成素刺激剂（ESA）、铁剂和输血；ESA帮助骨髓生成新的红细胞
text_en: Anemia treatment options for CKD Anemia may include one or more of the following. An erythropoyetin stimulating agent, iron, and the blood transfusion. An erythropoyetin stimulating agent or ESA helps your bone marrow make new red blood cells.

title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
summary_zh: 铁剂治疗：包括口服铁剂（药片）和静脉注射铁剂（通过IV输注增加体内铁含量）两种方式
