text_en: Another treatment option is iron. For Dr. May prescribed oral iron, this means pills taken by mouth. Or you may receive intravenous iron therapy. In this procedure, iron is delivered through an IV to increase the amount of iron in your

title: ANH20233 Treatment Options for CKD Anemia
summary_zh: 输血治疗：针对严重贫血，通过静脉输注红细胞和铁剂，快速提升血液中红细胞和铁的水平
text_en: For severe anemia, you may need a blood transfusion. This means you will be given red blood cells and iron through an IV. It will quickly increase the amount of these substances in your blood.

title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 镰状细胞贫血症的定义：一种遗传性血液疾病，影响红细胞，是镰状细胞病中最常见且最严重的类型
text_en: Cyclecell anemia is an inherited blood disease that affects your red blood cells. It's one of the most common and usually most severe forms of a group of red blood cell disorders called sickle cell disease.

title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 正常红细胞的生成与功能：骨髓中的造血干细胞生成红细胞，正常红细胞呈圆盘状，含血红蛋白，在肺泡中摄取氧气并通过血流输送至全身
text_en: In order to survive, your body needs red blood cells to supply oxygen to its tissues. Red blood cells are made inside the center of your bones called bone marrow. Their immature cells called blood stem cells can divide to make all types of blood cells, including red blood cells. Normal red blood cells are disc shape. Each of them contains protein molecules called hemoglobin that carry oxygen. Round flexible red blood cells flow easily through your bloodstream and pick up oxygen in your lungs. In tiny air sacs in the lungs, called alveoli, oxygen moves into the blood and attaches to the hemoglobin in red blood cells.

title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 红细胞向全身细胞递送氧气的过程
text_en: Then, the red blood cells deliver oxygen to cells throughout the body.

title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 镰状细胞贫血的遗传机制：父母双方遗传的缺陷基因导致β-珠蛋白基因突变，产生异常的血红蛋白S（HbS），HbS在红细胞内形成刚性链状结构，使红细胞变为镰刀形
text_en: In sickle cell anemia, there is a problem with your hemoglobin that makes your red blood cells sickle shaped instead of round. This is caused by a defective gene that is passed from both parents to children through genetic material in yourselves, called DNA. DNA is made up of sections called genes. Two of the genes called alfaglobin and beta-globin contain the blueprints for making the hemoglobin In sickle cell anemia, the beta-globin gene has changed or mutated. This mutation leads to the formation of an abnormal hemoglobin protein called hemoglobin S. Hemoglobin S forms into rigid strands inside your red blood cells, which causes the red blood cell to be sickle shaped.

title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 镰状细胞的病理后果：阻塞血流导致疼痛和组织损伤；镰状细胞寿命仅10-20天（正常为90-120天），身体无法及时补充被破坏的红细胞，导致贫血
text_en: Sickle cells can block blood flow and can cause pain and tissue damage. And while normal red blood cells last 90 to 120 days, sickle cells only last 10 to 20 Your body may not be able to keep up with how fast the cells are destroyed. As a result, your blood has less red blood cells than normal. This condition is called anemia.

title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 镰状细胞贫血的症状与并发症：症状包括手脚/腰背/胸腹疼痛、黄疸、疲劳；长期并发症包括眼、心、肺、肝、肾、关节的严重损害
text_en: The symptoms of sickle cell anemia vary from person to person, but may include episodes of pain in your body parts such as your hands and feet, lower back, chest, and abdomen. A yellow tint in the whites of the eyes and skin called jaundice and feeling tired due Over time sickle cell anemia can lead to complications such as severe pain and damage to your eyes, heart, lungs, liver, kidneys, and joints.

title: ANH22254 Understanding Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 脾脏损伤增加感染风险；高风险人群包括撒哈拉以南非洲、南美西班牙裔地区、加勒比海、中美洲、沙特阿拉伯及地中海国家的后裔
text_en: Damage to your spleen and organ that makes immune cells can increase your risk of infections. The risk for sickle cell anemia is highest among people with ancestors from sub-Saharan Africa, Hispanic areas of South America, the Caribbean and Central America, Saudi Arabia, and Mediterranean countries.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 镰状细胞贫血症的基本病理：异常血红蛋白S使红细胞从柔软的圆盘形变为僵硬的镰刀形，导致血流阻塞、疼痛和组织损伤，且镰状细胞寿命短，造成贫血
text_en: Cycle cell anemia is an inherited blood disease that affects your red blood cells. In this disease, an abnormal protein molecule called hemoglobin S changes your disc-shaped flexible red blood cells into rigid, sickle-shaped cells. Cycle cells can block blood flow and can cause pain and tissue damage. And sickle cells don't last as long as normal red blood cells. As a result, your blood has less red blood cells than normal. This condition is called anemia.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 镰状细胞贫血的治疗概述：包括药物治疗、医疗操作缓解症状，以及造血干细胞移植可能治愈疾病
text_en: Treatment options for sickle cell anemia may include medicines or procedures to help relieve symptoms, or in certain cases a hematopoid-extem cell transplant to potentially cure the disease.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 药物治疗方案：抗生素和疫苗预防感染，止痛药缓解疼痛，羟基脲（hydroxyurea）作为长期用药减少红细胞损伤和并发症，以及其他处方药物如crizanlizumab、voxelotor和L-谷氨酰胺
text_en: Antibiotics and vaccine shots for diseases can help prevent infections. Your healthcare provider may advise you to take pain relievers for severe or chronic pain. One of the most common medicines for long-term treatment of sickle cell anemia is called hydroxyuria. It helps reduce pain by preventing damage to your red blood cells. It also reduces the risk of long-term complications. Other prescribed medicines that help prevent complications and improve long-term outcomes include chrysanlysmab, buxelator, and elglutamine.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 输血治疗与造血干细胞移植适应症：严重疼痛发作时可进行输血替换镰状细胞；对于有并发症或高风险的年轻患者，建议进行造血干细胞移植
text_en: If you have an episode of very severe pain, you may have a procedure called a blood transfusion. This replaces the sickle cells with healthy red blood cells. For young patients who have, or are at risk for complications from sickle-cell anemia, a hematopoid-extem cell transplant may be advised.

title: ANH22256 Treatment for Sickle Cell Anemia
summary_zh: 造血干细胞移植的具体过程：先用化疗药物和/或放射治疗摧毁骨髓中的异常造血干细胞，再从组织配型匹配的供体（通常是家族成员）获取健康干细胞，输入后干细胞迁移至骨髓生成正常红细胞
text_en: It can cure some people who have this disease. For the transplant, you may be given chemotherapy drugs, radiation therapy, or both. This will destroy the abnormal blood-forming stem cells in your bone marrow. Then, you will receive healthy stem cells from either the bone marrow or blood of a donor. The donor is often a family member whose tissue type closely matches yours. The healthy blood-forming stem cells from the donor will travel through your blood to your bone marrow, where they will make normal red blood cells instead of sickle cells.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
summary_zh: 主动脉瓣的正常解剖与功能：主动脉瓣位于左心室与主动脉之间，由三个杯状瓣叶组成。左心室收缩时血液通过瓣叶泵入主动脉，舒张时瓣膜关闭防止血液回流。
text_en: Your aortic valve is one of the four valves in your heart. It lies between your left ventricle or lower left chamber and the large blood vessel supplying oxygen-rich blood to your body called the aorta. When your left ventricle contracts during each heartbeat, it pushes blood through three flexible cup-like leaflets that make up your aortic valve. When your left ventricle relaxes, your aortic valve closes to prevent blood from flowing back into your left ventricle.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
summary_zh: 钙化性主动脉瓣狭窄的病理过程：随年龄增长，瓣叶出现钙质沉积，变厚变硬，无法完全开放，导致瓣口狭窄、血流减少，左心室壁因代偿性增厚而肥大。
text_en: As you get older, your aortic valve may develop deposits of calcium that cause your leaflets to get thicker and less flexible. In this condition, your leaflets may not open fully. This condition, called calcific aortic valve stenosis, creates a narrowed opening that reduces the amount of blood flowing into your aorta and out to your body. As a result, the wall of your left ventricle thickens because it has to work harder to pump blood through the narrowed valve opening.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
summary_zh: 手术入路与导丝置入：在腹股沟做小切口，经股动脉插入引导鞘管，通过鞘管送入柔性导丝，导丝沿股动脉上行至主动脉，穿过主动脉瓣进入左心室。
text_en: At the beginning of your procedure, your doctor will make a small incision in your groin. After creating an opening in a blood vessel, called the femoral artery, your doctor will insert a flexible tube called an introducer sheath. Through the sheath, your doctor will insert a flexible guide wire into your femoral artery. The guide wire will be passed through your femoral artery all the way up to your aorta. Then the guide wire will be pushed through the opening in your aortic valve and into your left ventricle.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
summary_zh: 球囊预扩张：沿导丝送入带球囊的导管穿过主动脉瓣，充盈球囊扩张狭窄的瓣口并将原有瓣叶推向两侧，随后回缩球囊并撤出导管。
text_en: Your doctor will use the guide wire to pass a flexible tube called a catheter through your aortic valve. A balloon on the tip of the catheter will be inflated to widen the opening in your aortic valve to push the valve leaflets to the sides. After deflating the balloon, your doctor will remove the catheter.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
summary_zh: 人工瓣膜植入：替换瓣膜由组织瓣和金属支架组成，被压缩后装载于另一根导管尖端，送至主动脉瓣位置后充盈球囊使瓣膜及支架展开就位，支架固定瓣膜，随后撤出球囊导管和导丝。
text_en: The replacement valve consists of a tissue valve surrounded by a wire mesh called a stent. The replacement valve will be compressed and placed on the tip of another catheter. This catheter will be inserted through your aortic valve. When the catheter reaches the opening in your aortic valve, your doctor will inflate a balloon underneath the replacement valve to expand it and the stent. After placement of the new aortic valve, your doctor will deflate the balloon. The stent will support and secure the replacement valve in place. The catheter and guide wire will be removed.

file: ANH23268_Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach.mp4
title: ANH23268 Transcatheter Aortic Valve Implantation (TAVI) - Transfemoral Approach
summary_zh: 手术结束与切口关闭：撤出引导鞘管并缝合腹股沟切口；对侧腹股沟用于术中心脏监测的器械切口也一并缝合关闭。
text_en: At the end of the procedure, the introducer sheath in your groin will be removed and your skin incision will be closed. You will have another incision on the other side of your groin through which instruments were inserted to monitor your heart during the procedure. This skin incision will also be closed.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 二尖瓣的解剖位置与正常功能：二尖瓣位于左心房与左心室之间，控制含氧血液从左心房流入左心室，心室收缩时瓣膜关闭防止血液回流
text_en: Your mitral valve is one of the four valves in your heart. It lies between your left atrium or upper chamber and left ventricle or lower chamber. Oxygen-rich blood coming from your lungs goes into the left atrium. When your left atrium contracts, blood flows through your open mitral valve and into your left ventricle. When your left ventricle contracts, your mitral valve closes to prevent blood from flowing back into your left atrium.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 二尖瓣的结构：二尖瓣由前叶和后叶两片瓣叶组成，腱索将瓣叶连接至左心室内壁，辅助防止血液反流
text_en: Your mitral valve has two leaflets, an anterior or front leaflet, and a posterior or back String-like tissue, called tendinous cords, attach both leaflets to the inside of your left ventricle. The tendinous cords help prevent blood from flowing back into your atrium.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 二尖瓣狭窄：瓣膜开口过窄导致从左心房流向左心室的血流量减少，病因包括风湿热和先天性心脏缺陷
text_en: You may have one or both of the two main problems that can happen to your mitral valve. One problem is stenosis, in which the opening in your mitral valve is two narrow. The narrow opening reduces the amount of blood flowing through the valve from your left atrium to your left ventricle. Your mental valve stenosis may be caused by rheumatic fever or heart-birth defects.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 二尖瓣关闭不全（反流）：瓣膜无法完全关闭导致血液回流至左心房，病因包括二尖瓣脱垂、风湿性心脏病、先天缺陷及心脏扩张；狭窄和反流均加重心脏负担
text_en: The other problem your valve may have is regurgitation, in which your mitral valve doesn't close completely. Retraction allows blood to leak back into your left atrium when your left ventricle contracts. Mitral valve regurgitation may be caused by mitral valve prolapse, in which the valve leaflets and cords become weak and loose. Rheumatic heart disease, which may result from rheumatic fever, heart-birth defects, or heart enlargement, called dilation, which may result from a heart attack. Both stenosis and regurgitation strain your heart because it has to pump harder to move oxygen-rich blood to your body.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 术前准备：建立静脉通路、预防性使用抗生素、全身麻醉、插入气管导管辅助呼吸
text_en: Before your procedure, an intravenous line will be started. You may be given antibiotics through the IV to decrease your chance of infection. You will be given general anesthesia to make you unconscious and pain-free during the A breathing tube will be inserted through your mouth and down your throat to help you breathe during the procedure.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 机器人手术系统操作：在右侧胸壁做4-5个小切口，插入内窥镜和机器人手臂工具，工具可360度旋转；外科医生通过操控台的操纵杆和脚踏板控制机器人臂，计算机精确转译手部动作，提供放大的三维立体视野
text_en: Your surgeon will typically make four or five small incisions on the right side of your An assistant will insert a special camera called an endoscope and other tools attached to the robotic arms through the incisions. Unlike standard laparoscopic instruments, these tools can rotate 360 degrees and have more flexibility than the human wrist. Seated at a special console, your surgeon will operate the robotic arms and the camera with joystick-like controls and foot pedals. A computer will translate the exact movements of your surgeon's hands into precise movements of the surgical tools. Your surgeon will have a magnified three-dimensional stereoscopic view inside your body through a high-definition vision system.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 心脏手术核心步骤：连接体外循环机接管心肺功能并停跳心脏，切开左心房暴露二尖瓣，切断瓣膜与心脏的连接并移除病变瓣膜
text_en: After the robotic arms are in place, you will be placed on a heart-long machine. This machine will take over the functions of your heart and lungs while your heart is stopped for your operation. Your surgeon will begin the procedure on your heart by making an incision in your left atrium to get to your mitral valve. Next, all of your mitral valve's attachments to your heart will be cut and the valve will be removed.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 人工瓣膜选择：机械瓣可终身使用但需终身服用抗凝药物；生物组织瓣（人或动物组织）寿命可能较短但无需抗凝治疗
text_en: Your damaged mitral valve may be replaced with an artificial mechanical valve. A mechanical mitral valve will last a lifetime. However, they require you to take blood thinner medication for the rest of your life. Alternatively, your mitral valve may be replaced with a valve made from human or animal tissue. A tissue mitral valve may not last as long as a mechanical valve. However, the natural tissue does not require blood thinner medication.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 手术收尾：植入新瓣膜后恢复心跳、撤除体外循环，用缝线、吻合钉、医用胶或皮肤闭合条关闭切口
text_en: Once your new valve is in place, your surgeon will restart the beating of your heart and remove the attachments to the heart-long machine. At the end of your procedure, your skin incisions will be closed with stitches, staples, surgical glue, or skin closure strips.

title: ANR13009 Robotic Mitral Valve Replacement
summary_zh: 术后护理与出院：拔除气管导管、转入ICU监护、给予镇痛药及静脉输液、机械瓣患者使用抗凝药，大多数患者术后约一周出院
text_en: After your procedure, your breathing tube will be removed and you will be taken to the intensive care unit or ICU for monitoring. You will be given pain medication as needed. You may continue to receive fluids and medications through your IV. You will be given blood thinner medication if you have a mechanical valve. Most patients are released from the hospital about a week after the procedure.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
summary_zh: 动脉粥样硬化概述：一种可能从儿童期开始发展的危及生命的疾病，脂肪物质（斑块）在动脉壁内堆积，减少或完全阻断血流，严重时可导致心脏病发作或中风。
text_en: Atherosclerosis is a life-threatening disease that may have begun to develop during childhood. This condition is a process in which deposits of fatty material, called plaque, build up inside the walls of arteries, reducing or completely blocking blood flow. In extreme cases, this disease can lead to a heart attack or a stroke.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
summary_zh: 动脉粥样硬化的危险因素：包括高血压、高胆固醇、吸烟、糖尿病、肥胖和缺乏运动。
text_en: These factors for atherosclerosis include high blood pressure, high cholesterol, tobacco smoke, diabetes, obesity, and physical inactivity.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
summary_zh: 病变起始阶段：动脉内壁（内皮层）受损后，血液中的胆固醇、脂肪和细胞废物等物质在受损区域逐渐积聚。
text_en: Although the exact causes of atherosclerosis are not clear, many scientists think it begins with damage to the inner wall of an artery, called the endothelium. Over time, substances traveling in the blood, such as cholesterol, fats, and cellular waste products accumulate inside the damaged area of the arterial wall.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
summary_zh: 炎症反应与泡沫细胞形成：胆固醇分子氧化引发炎症反应，内皮细胞释放化学信号，单核细胞从血流中迁移至损伤处，在氧化胆固醇刺激下转化为巨噬细胞，巨噬细胞吞噬胆固醇后变成泡沫细胞，聚集形成斑块。
text_en: Chemical reactions occurring within the build-up of material cause cholesterol molecules to This initiates an inflammatory response in which the endothelial cells, at the damage site, release chemicals that signal a call for help. In response, monocytes from the bloodstream travel to the damage site. Demulation from oxidized cholesterol converts the monocytes into macrophages. The macrophages eat and digest the cholesterol molecules. As a result of this process, the macrophages change into foam cells, which accumulate to form plaque.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
summary_zh: 斑块增长与纤维帽形成：斑块增大导致动脉壁增厚硬化，平滑肌细胞增殖并迁移至斑块表面，形成坚固的纤维帽覆盖斑块，动脉通道逐渐狭窄，减少血流和器官供氧。
text_en: As the plaque increases in size, the arterial wall thickens and hardens. At the same time, smooth muscle cells within the arterial wall begin to multiply. Most of the smooth muscle cells move to the surface of the plaque. These cells contribute to the formation of a firm, fibrous cap covering the plaque. Eventually, the passageway through the artery narrows, enough to reduce blood flow and the amount of oxygen received by the organs it supplies.

title: ANS00149 Atherosclerosis Bulleted Version
summary_zh: 纤维帽破裂与血栓形成：纤维帽侵蚀破裂释放斑块进入血流，斑块随血流形成血栓阻断血流，导致周围组织缺血坏死，心脏或大脑严重损伤可引发心脏病发作或中风。
text_en: Over time, the cap may erode and break open, releasing plaque into the bloodstream. The plaque can flow downstream and contribute to the formation of the blood clot, which can stop blood flow. As a result, limited blood supply is available to the areas surrounding the partially blocked artery, degrading and potentially killing the neighboring tissue. Significant damage in organs, such as the heart or brain, can result in a heart attack or a stroke.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
summary_zh: 球囊血管成形术操作过程：医生将柔性导丝插入动脉，沿导丝推进带球囊的导管至阻塞部位，快速充气和放气使血管壁向外扩张，恢复动脉血流。
text_en: Your doctor will insert a flexible guide wire into the artery and advance a balloon-tipped catheter over this wire and into position at the side of the blockage. Rapid inflation and deflation of the small balloon pushes the vessel wall out, reestablishing blood flow through the artery.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
summary_zh: 球囊扩张术后续步骤：提示患者术中可能感到胸痛；对每处阻塞重复相同操作；完成后撤出放气的球囊和导管。
text_en: You may feel some chest pain while this is happening. The same procedure is repeated for each blockage being treated. Afterwards, your doctor will withdraw the deflated balloon and catheter from your arteries.

title: ANS00201 Balloon Angioplasty with Stent Repair (Detail)
summary_zh: 冠状动脉支架植入过程：介绍支架为金属丝网小管，用于支撑已治疗的动脉；将压缩状态的支架套在未充气球囊上送入病变部位；球囊充气时支架膨胀并锁定就位；撤出球囊和导管后，支架永久留置以保持动脉通畅。
text_en: Some patients will receive a coronary stent, which is a small tube made of wire mesh, designed to prop open the artery once it has been treated. A collapsed stent is placed over the deflated balloon as it's moved into position. When the balloon is inflated, the stent expands and locks into place. After the balloon and catheter are withdrawn, the stent remains behind to hold the artery open permanently.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
summary_zh: 冠状动脉搭桥术（CABG）概述：为严重冠心病患者改善心肌供血，将身体其他部位的健康动脉或静脉连接到阻塞的冠状动脉上，绕过阻塞段将富氧血液输送至心肌
text_en: A coronary artery bypass graft, or cabbage, is performed to improve circulation to the heart muscle in people with severe coronary artery disease. In this procedure, a healthy artery or vein from another part of the body is connected or grafted to the blocked coronary artery. A grafted artery or vein bypasses the blocked portion of the coronary artery, carrying oxygen rich blood to the heart muscle.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
summary_zh: 手术开胸步骤：单次手术可搭桥一条或多条冠状动脉；外科医生在胸骨上方切开皮肤，锯开胸骨并撑开肋骨架以暴露心脏
text_en: One or more coronary arteries may be bypassed during a single operation. During a conventional bypass surgery, your surgeon begins by making an incision in the skin over your breastbone or sternum. He or she will then cut the sternum and move your ribcage in order to get to your heart.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
summary_zh: 体外循环（心肺机）：术中将循环系统连接到体外循环泵，该机器暂时替代心肺功能，使心脏停搏，便于外科医生缝合移植血管
text_en: Throughout the procedure, your circulatory system will be connected to a cardiopulmonary bypass pump or heart lung machine. This machine temporarily performs the functions of your heart and lungs during the surgery, allowing your heart to be stopped while the surgeon soars the grafts into place.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
summary_zh: 移植血管来源与内乳动脉搭桥：常用胸廓内动脉或大隐静脉作为移植血管；内乳动脉搭桥时保留其上端与锁骨下动脉的连接，将下端从胸壁游离后吻合至冠状动脉阻塞远端
text_en: One of two blood vessels is typically used for the grafts, the internal thoracic artery, in the chest or the saffinous vein in the leg. For the internal thoracic artery graft, your surgeon will leave the upper end attached to the subclavian artery and divert the lower end from your chest wall to your coronary artery just beyond the blockage.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
summary_zh: 大隐静脉搭桥：外科医生将移植静脉一端缝合至主动脉，另一端缝合至阻塞远端的狭窄冠状动脉
text_en: Your surgeon will then sew the graft into place. For the saffinous vein graft, your surgeon will suture one end to the aorta and the other end to the narrowed artery just beyond the blockage.

title: ANS00253 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure
summary_zh: 术后关胸与恢复：搭桥完成后用电信号恢复心跳并安装临时起搏器，心跳恢复正常后断开心肺机，用钢丝固定胸骨、缝合皮肤切口，并放置临时引流管
text_en: With the grafts securely in place, your surgeon will use electrical signals to restore the heart beat and attach a temporary pacemaker to the heart. Once your heart is again beating normally, the heart lung machine will be disconnected. Your surgeon will wire the breastbone back together and suture the skin incision closed. A temporary drainage tube will be placed through the skin beneath the incision.

file: ANS00254_Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed.mp4
title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
summary_zh: 冠状动脉搭桥术（CABG）概述：介绍手术目的是改善严重冠心病患者的心肌血液循环，将身体其他部位的健康动脉或静脉连接到阻塞的冠状动脉，绕过阻塞段，将富氧血液输送至心肌。
text_en: A coronary artery bypass graft, or cabbage, is performed to improve circulation to the heart muscle in people with severe coronary artery disease. In this procedure, a healthy artery or vein from another part of the body is connected or grafted to the blocked coronary artery. A grafted artery or vein bypasses the blocked portion of the coronary artery, carrying oxygen rich blood to the heart muscle.

file: ANS00254_Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed.mp4
title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
summary_zh: 传统搭桥手术的开胸步骤：一次手术可搭桥一条或多条冠状动脉。外科医生在胸骨上方切开皮肤，锯开胸骨并撑开肋骨笼以暴露心脏。
text_en: One or more coronary arteries may be bypassed during a single operation. During a conventional bypass surgery, your surgeon begins by making an incision in the skin over your breastbone or sternum. He or she will then cut the sternum and move your ribcage in order to get to your heart.

file: ANS00254_Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed.mp4
title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
summary_zh: 体外循环（心肺机）的使用：手术中将循环系统连接至体外循环泵（心肺机），由机器暂时替代心肺功能，使心脏停跳，以便外科医生缝合移植血管。
text_en: Throughout the procedure, your circulatory system will be connected to a cardiopulmonary bypass pump or heart lung machine. This machine temporarily performs the functions of your heart and lungs during the surgery, allowing your heart to be stopped while the surgeon soars the grafts into place.

file: ANS00254_Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed.mp4
title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
summary_zh: 内乳动脉移植物的取材与吻合：常用移植血管为胸廓内动脉或腿部大隐静脉。内乳动脉移植时，上端保留连接锁骨下动脉，下端从胸壁游离后吻合至冠状动脉阻塞远端。
text_en: One of two blood vessels is typically used for the grafts, the internal thoracic artery, in the chest or the saffinous vein in the leg. For the internal thoracic artery graft, your surgeon will leave the upper end attached to the subclavian artery and divert the lower end from your chest wall to your coronary artery just beyond the blockage.

file: ANS00254_Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed.mp4
title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
summary_zh: 大隐静脉移植物的吻合：外科医生将移植物缝合就位；大隐静脉移植时，一端缝合至主动脉，另一端吻合至狭窄冠状动脉的阻塞远端。
text_en: Your surgeon will then sew the graft into place. For the saffinous vein graft, your surgeon will suture one end to the aorta and the other end to the narrowed artery just beyond the blockage.

file: ANS00254_Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed.mp4
title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
summary_zh: 术后恢复与关胸：移植血管就位后，使用电信号恢复心跳并连接临时起搏器；心跳恢复正常后断开心肺机；用钢丝固定胸骨，缝合皮肤切口，并放置临时引流管。
text_en: With the grafts securely in place, your surgeon will use electrical signals to restore the heart beat and attach a temporary pacemaker to the heart. Once your heart is again beating normally, the heart lung machine will be disconnected. Your surgeon will wire the breastbone back together and suture the skin incision closed. A temporary drainage tube will be placed through the skin beneath the incision.

file: ANS00254_Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed.mp4
title: ANS00254 Coronary Artery Bypass Graft (CABG) Procedure- Condensed
summary_zh: 非体外循环搭桥术与微创搭桥术：非体外循环（跳动心脏）搭桥术不停心、不用心肺机，使用机械装置固定心脏局部；微创搭桥术通过胸壁小切口置入专用器械完成手术，有时仍需心肺机辅助。
text_en: Off-pump cabbages also called beating heart bypass grafting because the heart isn't stopped and a heart lung machine isn't used. Instead a mechanical device is used to steady the part of the heart where grafting is being Students perform minimally invasive bypass procedures using specially designed instruments inserted through small incisions or ports in the chest. These procedures sometimes require a heart lung machine.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
summary_zh: 动脉粥样硬化的定义：一种可能从童年开始发展的危及生命的疾病，脂肪沉积物（斑块）在动脉壁内堆积，减少或完全阻断血流。
text_en: Atherosclerosis is a life-threatening disease that may have begun to develop during childhood. This condition is a process in which deposits of fatty material, called plaque, build up inside the walls of arteries, reducing or completely blocking blood flow.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
summary_zh: 动脉粥样硬化的早期病理过程：动脉内皮损伤后，胆固醇、脂肪和细胞废物在损伤区域积聚，发生化学反应导致胆固醇氧化。
text_en: Although the exact causes of atherosclerosis are not clear, many scientists think it begins with damage to the inner wall of an artery, called the endothelium, substances traveling in the blood, such as cholesterol, fats, and cellular waste products accumulate inside the damaged area of the arterial wall. Chemical reactions occurring within the build up of material cause cholesterol molecules to oxidize.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
summary_zh: 炎症反应与泡沫细胞形成：内皮细胞释放信号招募单核细胞，单核细胞在氧化胆固醇刺激下转化为巨噬细胞，巨噬细胞吞噬胆固醇后变为泡沫细胞，泡沫细胞聚集形成斑块。
text_en: This initiates an inflammatory response in which the endothelial cells, at the damage site, release chemicals that signal a call for help. In response, monocytes from the bloodstream travel to the damaged site. Stimulation from oxidized cholesterol converts the monocytes into macrophages. The macrophages eat and digest the cholesterol molecules. As a result of this process, the macrophages change into foam cells, which accumulate to form plaque.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
summary_zh: 斑块增大与纤维帽形成：斑块增大导致动脉壁增厚硬化，平滑肌细胞增殖并迁移至斑块表面，形成覆盖斑块的坚硬纤维帽。
text_en: As the plaque increases in size, the arterial wall thickens and hardens. At the same time, smooth muscle cells within the arterial wall begin to multiply. Most of the smooth muscle cells move to the surface of the plaque. These cells contribute to the formation of a firm, fibrous cap covering the plaque.

title: ANS00260 Atherosclerosis
summary_zh: 动脉狭窄、斑块破裂与血栓形成：动脉通道变窄减少血流和供氧，纤维帽侵蚀破裂释放斑块，斑块随血流促成血栓形成，阻断血供导致周围组织退化甚至坏死。
text_en: Eventually, the passageway through the artery narrows, enough to reduce blood flow, and the amount of oxygen received by the organs it supplies. Over time, the cap may erode and break open, releasing plaque into the bloodstream. The plaque can flow downstream and contribute to the formation of a blood clot, which can stop blood flow. As a result, limited blood supply is available to the area surrounding the partially blocked artery, degrading, and potentially killing the neighboring tissue.

title: ANS00339 Electrocardiography (ECG)
summary_zh: 心电图（ECG）检查过程：将粘性电极片贴在胸部以监测心率
text_en: Sticky pads will be placed on your chest to check your heart rate.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
summary_zh: 手术切开与暴露：从胸骨至脐下做腹部正中切口，定位动脉瘤后在其上方和下方钳夹主动脉以阻断血流
text_en: Your surgeon will begin by making an incision from the breastbone to below the navel. He or she will then locate the aneurysm and clamp the aorta just above and below it.

title: ANS00367 Open Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
summary_zh: 人工血管移植物置入：切开动脉瘤囊壁，将与健康主动脉同等尺寸和形状的人工血管移植物缝合就位，再将动脉瘤壁缝合包裹在移植物外侧以加固主动脉壁
text_en: Your surgeon will then open the aneurysm and sew an artificial graft the same size and shape as a healthy aorta into place. Your surgeon will stitch the wall of the aneurysm closed over the newly placed graft. The graft will reinforce the walls of the aorta.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
summary_zh: 主动脉概述：主动脉是人体最大的动脉，与心脏相连，直接从左心室（主要泵血腔室）接收血液。画面展示主动脉与心脏的解剖位置关系。
text_en: The largest artery in the body is the aorta, which is attached to the heart and receives blood directly from the left ventricle or main pumping chamber.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
summary_zh: 主动脉的四个分段：升主动脉、主动脉弓、胸主动脉和腹主动脉。画面依次标注主动脉从上到下的四个解剖分区。
text_en: The aorta is divided into four parts, the ascending aorta, aortic arch, thoracic aorta, and the abdominal aorta.

title: ANS00368 Anatomy of the Aorta
summary_zh: 腹主动脉的解剖与功能：腹主动脉是主动脉最末端也是最大的一段，负责向下半身供血，包括腹腔和盆腔器官。腹主动脉在脐部水平分为左右两条髂动脉，向双下肢供血。画面展示腹主动脉走行及其末端分叉为髂动脉的结构。
text_en: The abdominal aorta is the last and largest section of the aorta. It supplies blood to the lower regions of your body, including the organs and the abdomen and pelvis. The abdominal aorta ends around naval level, where it splits into the two iliac arteries that carry blood to the legs.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
summary_zh: 手术入路与导丝置入：在腹股沟股动脉处做小切口，插入导丝并沿动脉推送至动脉瘤部位
text_en: Your surgeon will begin by making a small incision in your groin over your femoral artery. Then he or she will insert a guide wire into the artery and gently push it up toward the site of your aneurysm.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
summary_zh: 导管与支架输送：通过导丝送入导管（中空管），在X线透视引导下，将压缩的覆膜支架（织物与金属管）经导管输送至动脉瘤处
text_en: A catheter or hollow tube will be passed over the guide wire and pushed toward your aneurysm. Using live x-ray pictures as a guide, your surgeon will pass an endovascular stent graft, a compressed fabric and metal tube, through the catheter to the aneurysm.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
summary_zh: 支架释放与扩展：释放并展开支架，通过金属钩和支撑架固定就位；可能插入额外组件将支架延伸至供应双腿的血管
text_en: Then he or she will open the graft which will be held in place with metallic hooks and stents. Your surgeon may insert additional graft components to extend the graft into the blood vessels supplying your legs.

title: ANS00369 Endovascular Abdominal Aortic Aneurysm Repair
summary_zh: 血流恢复与减压：支架就位后血液经支架内流通，不再进入周围动脉瘤囊，从而解除瘤体压力
text_en: Once the graft is in place, your blood will flow through it, not into the surrounding aneurysm sac, which will remove the pressure from your aneurysm.

title: ANS00423 Arterial Stenosis
summary_zh: 动脉粥样硬化斑块在动脉内壁堆积，使管腔变窄、阻碍血流，这种动脉阻塞称为狭窄（stenosis）
text_en: Fatty deposits called atherosclerotic plaques can build up inside your arteries, narrowing the passageway and interfering with blood flow. This arterial blockage is called stenosis.

title: ANS00423 Arterial Stenosis
summary_zh: 动脉狭窄导致组织供血不足，通常引起疼痛症状
text_en: Insufficient blood flow to a tissue generally causes pain.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 介绍非心脏经皮腔内血管成形术（NC-PTA）的定义和目的：通过该手术恢复被动脉粥样硬化斑块阻塞的血管血流。
text_en: Non-cardiac percutaneous transliminal angioplasty. A surgeon will perform a non-cardiac percutaneous transliminal angioplasty, or NC-PTA, to restore blood flow in an artery block by an atherosclerotic plaque.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 术前准备：患者被置于配备透视机的手术台上，透视机可从多角度拍摄X线图像；医生在导管插入部位周围进行局部麻醉。
text_en: To begin the procedure, you'll be placed on a table equipped with a fluoroscope. A fluoroscope is capable of taking x-ray images of many parts of the body from multiple angles. Your doctor will numb your skin around where the catheter is to be inserted.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 血管入路建立：选择腹股沟处股动脉作为最常用的穿刺部位，医生通过插入空心针建立通路，再经针鞘送入球囊导管。
text_en: Since the arteries of the leg are most easily accessed, the femoral artery in the groin serves as the insertion site in most cases. Your doctor will initially gain access to the artery by inserting a hollow needle through which the balloon-tipped catheter is passed.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 导管推进与造影定位：在透视屏幕引导下将导管推进至目标动脉，注射造影剂后拍摄血管造影图像（angiogram），精确定位阻塞位置。
text_en: Watching on the fluoroscopy screen, your doctor will gently advance the catheter until it is positioned in the artery being treated and eject a special die. This die allows the fluoroscope to take x-ray images called angiograms, which lets your doctor identify the exact location of the blockages.

file: ANS00424_Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version).mp4
title: ANS00424 Non Cardiac Percutaneous Transluminal Angioplasty (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 球囊扩张治疗：将导管前端未充气的球囊定位于狭窄处，通过快速反复充气和放气将斑块及血管壁向外推开，部分恢复血流；如有其他阻塞可重复该操作。
text_en: Next, your doctor will move the catheter until the deflated balloon at its tip is positioned at the site of the stenosis. Then, by rapidly inflating and deflating the balloon, your doctor will gently push the plaque and vessel wall out, partially re-establishing blood flow. This procedure may be repeated for any additional blockages.

title: ANS00425 The Heart
summary_zh: 心脏概述：心脏是一个通过泵血维持全身血液循环的肌性器官
text_en: The heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood through the body.

title: ANS00425 The Heart
summary_zh: 心脏四腔结构：上方为左右心房，下方为左右心室
text_en: It consists of four chambers. The upper chambers are the right and left atria. The lower chambers are the right and left ventricles.

title: ANS00425 The Heart
summary_zh: 腔室连接与瓣膜功能：心房连接心室，右心室连接肺动脉，左心室连接主动脉，瓣膜位于各连接处以确保血液单向流动
text_en: The right and left atria connect to their respective ventricles, the right ventricle connects to the pulmonary artery, and the left ventricle to the aorta. Those are located at these connections to ensure that blood moves in only one direction.

title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
summary_zh: 主动脉瓣的正常解剖与功能：位于左心室与主动脉之间，心脏收缩时开放将血液泵入主动脉，舒张时关闭防止血液回流；正常主动脉瓣有三个光滑的瓣叶，可充分开放和紧密关闭。
text_en: The aortic valve is located between the left ventricle and the aorta. When the heart contracts, the aortic valve opens and blood is pumped into the aorta. When the heart relaxes, it closes to prevent blood from leaking back into the left ventricle. A normal aortic valve has three smooth cusps that open widely and close tightly.

title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
summary_zh: 主动脉瓣的两种病变类型：瓣膜关闭不全（主动脉瓣反流）导致血液在心跳间期回流至左心室；瓣膜狭窄（主动脉瓣狭窄）导致瓣膜无法完全开放，限制血液从左心室流入主动脉。
text_en: For aortic valve may be leaky or narrowed, causing your heart to work harder and disrupt your blood flow. A leaky valve does not close all the way, causing blood to leak back into the ventricle between each heartbeat. This condition is called aortic regurgitation. A narrowed aortic valve does not open all the way, which restricts the amount of blood passing out of the ventricle into the aorta. This condition is called aortic stenosis.

title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
summary_zh: 主动脉瓣疾病的病因：先天性异常如二叶式主动脉瓣（仅两个瓣叶）导致开闭不良；后天获得性病因包括风湿热（链球菌感染并发症）、感染性心内膜炎（细菌感染瓣膜）以及瓣叶硬化。
text_en: You may have been born with an abnormal aortic valve. For example, your aortic valve may only have two cusps instead of three, so it may not open and close properly. Your aortic valve may have become damaged during your lifetime. A condition called acquired aortic valve disease. Cusps include rheumatic fever, which can be a complication of untreated strep throat. Andocarditis, which is a bacterial infection of the valve, and sclerosis or hardening of the aortic leaflets.

title: ANS00426 The Aortic Valve
summary_zh: 主动脉瓣病变对心脏的长期影响：缺陷瓣膜可导致心肌增大和减弱，左心室壁可能异常增厚或变薄，病变最终可累及右心。
text_en: After a period of time, a faulty aortic valve can cause the heart muscles to become enlarged and weakened. Depending on the nature of the valve's defect, the walls of the left ventricle may become abnormally thick or thin, and the problem can eventually affect the right side of the heart as well.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 术前准备：建立静脉通路输液给药、留置导尿管、全身麻醉诱导及气管插管辅助呼吸
text_en: Before your procedure, an IV line will be started in your arm to provide fluids and medications. A catheter will be inserted into your bladder to drain urine. Aortic valve replacements are done under general anesthesia. This means you will be asleep for the duration of the operation. Add a tube will be placed through your mouth and into your windpipe to help you breathe during surgery.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 手术入路：沿胸骨正中切开皮肤，锯开胸骨暴露心包膜，打开心包显露心脏
text_en: Once the anesthesia takes effect, your surgeon will begin by making an incision in the chest. The traditional incision is made in the middle of the chest, overlying the breastbone or sternum. The surgeon then separates the sternum to expose the pericardium, which is a protective membrane encircling the heart. Your surgeon will then open the pericardium to expose the heart.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 体外循环建立：将血液经管道引流至人工心肺机，由机器代替心肺功能；随后停搏心脏并排空心内血液，使主动脉瓣静止便于手术操作
text_en: At this point, your heart will be connected to a heart lung machine. Tubes are used to reroute your blood into this machine, which takes over the functions of the heart and lungs during the operation. Once the heart lung machine has taken over, your heart will be temporarily stopped and the blood inside removed. This keeps the aortic valve still and allows your surgeon to see it.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 瓣膜切除与置换：切开主动脉暴露病变瓣膜，沿瓣缘切除损坏瓣膜，将人工瓣膜缝合就位并缝合关闭主动脉
text_en: Your surgeon will open the aorta to expose the aortic valve, make an incision around the edge of the damaged valve, and remove it. Your surgeon will then carefully sew the replacement valve into place and close the aorta with stitches.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 恢复心脏搏动：重新灌注血液检查新瓣膜功能，必要时电击除颤复跳，心脏自主搏动后脱离体外循环，放置临时起搏导线以维持术后正常心律
text_en: At this point, blood will again be allowed to flow into your heart to check the function of the new valve. If needed, an electric shock will be given to restart the heart. Once your heart is beating on its own, it will be detached from the heart lung machine. Generally, temporary pacemaker wires will be placed on the surface of the heart to ensure the heart maintains a normal rhythm during recovery.

title: ANS00427 Aortic Valve Replacement (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 术后监护：转入重症监护室监测生命体征，保留气管插管暂不能说话，胸腔引流管引流血液和积液，保留导尿管
text_en: After surgery, you'll be taken to the intensive care unit, where your vital functions will be monitored. You will still have a breathing tube in your throat, which will prevent you from talking. Several tubes will be in place to drain blood and fluid from your incision, and your bladder catheter will still be draining your urine.

title: ANS00431 Carotid Arteries
summary_zh: 颈动脉的解剖位置与功能：颈部有两条主要血管称为颈动脉，负责将富含氧气的血液从心脏输送至大脑。
text_en: In your neck are two major blood vessels called the carotid arteries, which carry oxygen-rich blood from your heart to your brain.

title: ANS00431 Carotid Arteries
summary_zh: 颈动脉搏动的体表触诊：可在颈部侧面触摸到颈动脉的脉搏跳动。
text_en: You can feel the pulse of the carotid artery in the side of your neck.

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
summary_zh: 颈动脉的解剖位置与功能：颈部两条主要血管称为颈动脉，负责将富氧血液从心脏输送至大脑，可在颈侧触摸到颈动脉搏动
text_en: In your neck are two major blood vessels called the carotid arteries, which carry oxygen-rich blood from your heart to your brain. You can feel the pulse of the carotid artery in the side of your neck.

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
summary_zh: 动脉粥样硬化斑块与狭窄形成：脂肪沉积物（动脉粥样硬化斑块）在颈动脉内壁堆积，使动脉通道变窄，阻碍流向大脑的血流，这种阻塞或变窄称为狭窄（stenosis）
text_en: Fatty deposits called atherosclerotic plaques can build up inside the carotid artery. These plaques can narrow the passageway of the artery and interfere with blood flow to the brain. This blockage or narrowing is called a stenosis.

title: ANS00432 Carotid Artery Disease
summary_zh: 脑缺血的临床后果：脑部血流暂时受阻可引发短暂性脑缺血发作（TIA）；当血流完全阻断（如颈动脉完全堵塞）时，则会导致中风（stroke）
text_en: If blood flow to the brain is blocked temporarily, a transient ischemic attack, also known as a TIA, may occur. When the blood flow is completely obstructed, as in a carotid artery blockage, a stroke

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 深静脉血栓（DVT）的定义与危险因素：长期制动（手术、妊娠、疾病、旅行）可导致深静脉中形成血栓，常见于下肢。
text_en: If you are a mobile for an extended period of time because of surgery, pregnancy, illness, or travel, you may be at risk for deep vein thrombosis or DVT. A condition where a blood clot forms in a deep vein in the body, typically the leg.

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 正常静脉回流机制：与动脉依赖心脏泵血不同，静脉依靠肌肉收缩推动血液回流，并通过静脉瓣膜防止血液逆流和淤积。
text_en: Unlike arteries which rely on the heart to force blood through them, veins transport blood with the help of muscle contractions while you move and walk. Things also have valves that keep blood from flowing backward and pooling in your arms

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: DVT的形成过程：长期制动导致静脉循环减慢，血液中的凝血因子使缓慢流动的血液聚集，形成血栓（thrombus），即深静脉血栓。
text_en: When you are a mobile for an extended period, your venous circulation slows down. As a result, clotting factors in the blood may cause the slow moving blood to clump together, forming a blood clot or thrombus. This condition is called deep vein thrombosis or DVT.

title: ANS00433 DVT (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: DVT的常见症状：下肢肿胀、静脉肿胀、疼痛或压痛、局部皮肤温度升高、皮肤发红或变色。
text_en: Common symptoms of DVT include swelling of the leg or a vein in the leg, pain or tenderness in the area, increased warmth in the skin, and red or discolored skin.

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
summary_zh: 深静脉血栓脱落并随血流迁移的过程：血栓从腿部深静脉脱落，经腹部静脉进入心脏，最终嵌塞在肺动脉或其分支中，阻断肺部血流供应。
text_en: In some DVT cases, a blood clot may detach from a deep vein and join the bloodstream. The clot travels from the deep veins of the leg through the veins of the abdomen, into the heart, and finally lodges in the pulmonary artery or its branches, blocking blood flow to the lungs.

title: ANS00434 Pulmonary Embolism Caused by DVT
summary_zh: 肺栓塞的定义与严重后果：该病症称为肺栓塞（PE），可永久性损伤部分肺组织；若血栓较大或数量较多，可导致死亡。
text_en: This condition, called pulmonary embolism, is very serious and may permanently damage a portion of the lung. If a clot is large or if there are many clots, pulmonary embolism may also cause death.

title: ANS11476 Atrial Fibrillation (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 房颤的定义：房颤是心律失常的一种，即心跳节奏或力度的异常
text_en: Atrial fibrillation is a type of arrhythmia, which is an abnormality in the pace or force of your heartbeat.

title: ANS11476 Atrial Fibrillation (Abbreviated Version)
summary_zh: 房颤的具体表现：心脏上腔（心房）快速、颤动性收缩，导致心跳不规则、不协调
text_en: If you have atrial fibrillation, the heart's upper chambers contract in a fast, twitchy fashion, resulting in an irregular, uncoordinated heartbeat.

title: ANS11477 Four Heart Chambers
summary_zh: 心脏是由四个腔室组成的肌性器官：左右心房和左右心室。展示心脏的基本解剖结构。
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ made up of four chambers, the right and left atria, and the right and left ventricles.

title: ANS11477 Four Heart Chambers
summary_zh: 正常心跳过程中，四个腔室按节律性收缩和舒张，维持全身稳定的血液循环。展示心脏搏动的基本机制。
text_en: During a normal heartbeat, the chambers of your heart contract and relax in a rhythmic pattern to deliver a consistent flow of blood throughout your body.

title: ANS11477 Four Heart Chambers
summary_zh: 右心房收集全身回流的静脉血，右心室将其泵入肺部进行气体交换（摄氧排碳）；同时左心房接收来自肺部的含氧血，左心室将其泵出供应全身组织氧气和营养。展示体循环与肺循环的完整血流路径。
text_en: The right atrium collects blood from the body, and the right ventricle pumps it to the lungs, which bring oxygen to and remove carbon dioxide from the blood. At the same time, the left atrium receives blood from the lungs, and the left ventricle pumps it out to supply your body's tissues with oxygen and nutrients.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
summary_zh: 超声心动图概述：利用声波检测心脏的形态、大小及泵血功能，帮助医生发现心脏瓣膜和心腔的异常。
text_en: An echocardiogram is a common test using sound waves to map out the shape and size of your heart, thus allowing your doctor to see how well your heart pumps blood and look for abnormalities of the heart valves and chambers.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
summary_zh: 经胸超声心动图（TTE）操作流程：超声技师在胸部涂抹凝胶，用探头发射无痛声波至心脏，声波反射回探头后转换为心肌、心腔和瓣膜的动态影像显示在屏幕上。
text_en: In a trans thoracic echocardiogram, your synographer will put a cool gel on your chest. Your synographer will move a transducer firmly through the gel, sending painless sound waves to your heart. The sound waves bounce off your heart and echo back to the transducer. The sound waves are converted to moving images of your heart muscles, chambers, and valves on a video screen.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
summary_zh: 多普勒超声心动图：记录血细胞的回声信号，通过血流速度和方向评估心脏内部的血液循环状况。
text_en: A Doppler echocardiogram records echoes from blood cells. The speed and direction of the blood flow indicate how well blood gets around inside your heart.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
summary_zh: 负荷超声心动图（压力超声）：对比运动前后（或药物模拟运动后）的心脏影像，帮助医生发现仅在运动时出现的心脏问题，如冠状动脉疾病。
text_en: A stress echocardiogram evaluates images of the heart before and after exercise or following medication that simulates exercise on the heart. Comparing your echo results before and after activity provides your doctor information about certain heart problems, such as coronary artery disease, that may only occur with exertion.

title: ANW10009 Echocardiogram
summary_zh: 经食道超声心动图（TEE）：在镇静状态下将探头经咽喉送入食道，从更近距离对心脏进行更详细的评估。
text_en: A transisophageal echocardiogram sends sound waves through a transducer past gently down your throat while your under sedation. This test provides a closer, more detailed evaluation of your heart.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
summary_zh: 心脏的正常功能：心脏泵送含氧血液至全身，心脏内部有节律的电脉冲使心肌壁收缩，确保心脏稳定跳动。
text_en: Your heart pumps oxygenated blood throughout your body. Rhythmic electrical impulses inside your heart cause its muscular walls to contract, ensuring your heart beats steadily.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
summary_zh: 心律失常与ICD的适应症：当心脏电传导系统出现问题时，可能发生危及生命的心律失常；医生可能建议植入心律转复除颤器（ICD）来恢复正常心律并预防心脏性猝死。
text_en: If problems occur with your heart's electrical system, you may develop an abnormal, potentially life-threatening heart beat called arrhythmia. Your doctor may want you to have an implantable cardio-verter defibrillator or ICD to restore the heart's normal rhythm and prevent sudden cardiac death.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
summary_zh: ICD植入手术过程：医生将细导线经静脉送入右心室，导线持续监测心律；确认导线位置正确且工作正常后，将导线连接至ICD脉冲发生器，然后将ICD装置植入锁骨下方胸部皮肤下。
text_en: During the procedure, your doctor will thread a small wire through a vein until it reaches your right ventricle. The wires continuously monitor the heart's rhythm. After verifying the wire is in the correct location and works properly, your doctor will attach the wire to the ICD generator. Then your doctor will insert the ICD device under the skin of your chest below the collarbone.

title: ANW10012 Implantable Cardioverter Defibrillator (ICD)
summary_zh: ICD的工作原理：电池驱动的ICD通过内置计算机持续监测心跳；当检测到危及生命的心律失常时，ICD向心脏发送电击以重置心律恢复正常；ICD还可被编程进行心脏起搏以恢复自然心律。
text_en: The battery-powered ICD constantly monitors your heart beat using a computer. If a life-threatening arrhythmia occurs, the ICD delivers an electrical shock to your heart, resetting your heart beat back to normal. The ICD can also be programmed to paste the heart and restore its natural rhythm.

title: ANZ23179 Ventricular Fibrillation
summary_zh: 心室颤动（室颤）的动画演示：展示心室肌细胞电活动紊乱，心室失去协调收缩能力，无法有效泵血的病理过程
text_en: ANZ23179 Ventricular Fibrillation

title: ANZ23373 Blood Oxygenation
summary_zh: 血液氧合过程动画展示（无语音旁白，仅标题标识 ANZ23373 Blood Oxygenation）
text_en: ANZ23373 Blood Oxygenation

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
summary_zh: 总述：任何损害心脏泵血能力的疾病或状况都可能导致心力衰竭
text_en: Any condition or disease that damages your heart's ability to pump blood can cause heart failure.

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
summary_zh: 最常见病因——冠状动脉疾病：为心肌供血的冠状动脉被脂肪物质（粥样斑块）阻塞，导致心肌缺血
text_en: For example, the most common cause is coronary artery disease in which blood vessels that supply your heart muscle, called coronary arteries, become blocked by a fatty substance called

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
summary_zh: 其他常见病因：未控制的高血压，以及糖尿病引起的高血糖对心脏的损害
text_en: Other common causes of heart failure include uncontrolled high blood pressure and high blood sugar in diabetes.

title: Common Causes of Heart Failure
summary_zh: 较少见病因：心脏瓣膜病变/感染/损伤、心律失常、先天性心脏缺陷、毒物或药物滥用、肺部疾病、以及睡眠呼吸暂停
text_en: Less common causes of heart failure include diseased, infected or damaged heart valves, your regular heartbeats, called arrhythmias, heart defects, poisons or substance abuse, lung diseases, and breathing problems during sleep, called sleep apnea.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
summary_zh: 心力衰竭的定义与两种基本机制：心室壁薄弱无力导致泵血不足，或心室壁僵硬增厚导致充盈不足
text_en: If you have heart failure, your heart has lost the ability to pump enough blood to meet your body's needs. You may have weak or damaged ventricular walls that are not able to push enough blood out of your heart. You may also have stiff and thickened ventricular walls that do not allow your heart to fill with enough blood.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
summary_zh: 左心衰竭的影响：左心室无法输送足够含氧血液导致疲劳和气短；左心衰竭使肺静脉压力升高，液体渗入肺组织造成呼吸困难（肺水肿）
text_en: If you have left-sided heart failure, your left ventricle does not deliver enough oxygen rich blood to your body, making you feel tired and out of breath. Your failing left ventricle also increases the blood pressure in the blood vessels between your lungs and left ventricle. This increased pressure forces fluid out of your blood and into your lung tissues, which makes it difficult for you to breathe.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
summary_zh: 右心衰竭的影响：右心室收缩力不足无法将血液泵入肺部，静脉血液淤积导致全身组织液体积聚（外周水肿）
text_en: If you have right-sided heart failure, your right ventricle is unable to contract with enough force to push blood to your lungs. The result is a build-up of blood in your veins, which causes a build-up of fluid called edema throughout the tissues in your body.

title: Effects of Congestive Heart Failure
summary_zh: 心衰的代偿与恶性循环：心室逐渐扩大、泵血减少；神经系统释放应激激素加快心率以代偿，但长期激素刺激反而损伤心肌细胞，加重心衰
text_en: Over time, heart failure on either side of your heart results in weakened and large ventricles that deliver less blood to your body. To make up for the decreased amount of blood, your nervous system releases stress hormones that increase the speed and force of your heart beat. Unfortunately, the continued release of these stress hormones makes your heart fail your worse because they damage the muscle cells in your ventricles.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
summary_zh: 心衰患者需要改变生活方式以防止心脏进一步损伤，戒烟是最重要的措施之一
text_en: As a heart failure patient, you may need to make some lifestyle changes to prevent further damage to your heart. If you smoke, quitting is one of the most important things you can do for your heart.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
summary_zh: 心脏健康饮食计划的总体原则：包括蔬菜、水果、瘦肉、全谷物和低脂乳制品，用果蔬替代高热量食品如饼干和汽水
text_en: Work with your healthcare provider to create a nutritious eating plan that also helps you maintain a healthy weight. A heart healthy diet includes vegetables, fruits, lean meats and whole grains, as well as low fat or fat-free dairy products. High calorie foods such as cookies and soda should be replaced with fruits and vegetables that don't contain added salt and sugar.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
summary_zh: 健康饮食的具体食物示例：蔬菜（胡萝卜、西兰花、番茄）、水果（苹果、橙子、草莓）、瘦肉蛋白（鱼、鸡肉、豆类）、乳制品（低脂酸奶和奶酪）、淀粉类（全麦面包和红薯）以及健康饮品（水和脱脂牛奶）
text_en: Examples of foods in a healthy diet include vegetables such as carrots, broccoli and tomatoes, fruits such as apples, oranges and strawberries, lean proteins such as fish, chicken and beans, dairy foods such as low fat yogurt and cheese, starches such as whole wheat bread and sweet potatoes, and healthy drinks such as water and fat-free milk.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
summary_zh: 饮食中的具体限制措施：减少盐和糖摄入，钠摄入低于2000毫克以减少体内水分潴留，用橄榄油等健康油脂替代固体脂肪，限制液体摄入量，禁止饮酒
text_en: Reduce the amount of salt and sugar you eat. Keep sodium levels below 2000mg to reduce the amount of water retained by your body. Substitute solid fats with heart healthy oils such as olive oil. Limit the amount of fluid you drink. Ask your healthcare provider how much fluid you should drink each day. And remember, a heart healthy diet doesn't include alcohol.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Lifestyle Changes
summary_zh: 运动建议：咨询医生制定适合的运动计划，每天30分钟低强度运动有助于增强心脏功能
text_en: Also, check with your healthcare provider about an exercise plan that's right for you. 30 minutes of daily low impact exercise can help strengthen your heart.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
summary_zh: 利尿剂：通过增加肾脏产生的尿量来减轻体内水肿
text_en: Diuretic medications reduce the swelling in your body by increasing the amount of urine produced by your kidneys.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
summary_zh: ACE抑制剂：扩张血管，降低血压，减轻心脏泵血负担，防止心脏进一步损伤
text_en: Ace inhibitors are medications that allow your blood vessels to expand. This helps decrease your blood pressure and prevents further damage to your heart by making it easier for your heart to pump blood.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Medication
summary_zh: β受体阻滞剂：阻断应激激素对心脏的作用，虽会减慢心率，但主要用于保护心肌免受应激激素的长期损害
text_en: Beta blockers are medications that block the effects of stress hormones on your heart. Although beta blockers slow down your heartbeat, they are mainly used to protect your heart muscle from the long-term damage caused by stress hormones.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
summary_zh: 晚期心力衰竭的两种常规外科手术：冠状动脉旁路移植术（搭桥手术）用于改善心肌血流，以及心脏瓣膜重建术用于改善心脏内部血流。
text_en: Surgery for advanced heart failure include coronary artery bypass surgery to improve blood flow to your heart muscle. And heart valve reconstruction surgery to improve blood flow through your heart.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
summary_zh: 当常规心衰治疗效果不佳、心脏仍然虚弱时，可植入心室辅助装置（VAD）。VAD是一种机械泵，连接心脏下腔室，辅助将血液泵送至全身及重要器官，模拟健康心脏的泵血功能。
text_en: If the heart remains weak with standard heart failure treatments, advanced treatment options for severe heart failure include insertion of a pump for your heart. A ventricular assist device, or VAD, is a mechanical pump that's used to support heart function and blood flow in people who have weakened hearts. The device takes blood from a lower chamber of the heart and helps pump it to the body and vital organs just as a healthy heart would.

title: Heart Failure Treatment  Procedures
summary_zh: 另一种重度心力衰竭的高级治疗方案是心脏移植手术。
text_en: Another advanced treatment for severe heart failure is a heart transplant.

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
summary_zh: 心脏概述：心脏是一个肌肉器官，负责泵送含有氧气和营养物质的血液供全身使用
text_en: Your heart is a muscular organ that pumps blood containing the oxygen and nutrients your body needs.

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
summary_zh: 心脏四腔结构介绍：心脏的泵血部分由右心房、右心室、左心房和左心室四个腔室组成
text_en: The pumping sections of the heart are the right atrium, right ventricle, left atrium, and left ventricle.

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
summary_zh: 右心循环（肺循环入路）：缺氧血液从全身经大静脉流入右心房，再进入右心室，右心室收缩将血液泵向肺部进行气体交换
text_en: In your heart, oxygen poor blood flows from your body through large veins into your right atrium. Next, your blood moves into your right ventricle, which contracts, sending blood out of the heart to pick up oxygen from your lungs.

file: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart.mp4
title: Normal Anatomy and Function of the Heart
summary_zh: 左心循环（体循环出路）：富氧血液从肺部流入左心房，再进入左心室，左心室收缩将富氧血液泵出心脏供应全身
text_en: Oxygen rich blood moves out of your lungs into your left atrium, then moves into your left ventricle. Finally, your left ventricle contracts, sending oxygen rich blood out of your heart to
